mainOrgs.name: Maine Coast Fishermens Association
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Support equitable access to healthy fisheries and the well-being of community-based fishermen in Maine.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Grant programs for fishery-related businesses and resource management
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Supports fisheries which impacts water quality and access indirectly.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Focused primarily on community support and resource management in fisheries.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Responded to federal disaster with grants to support local fishermen.

riskSignals.brief_explanation: Board restructuring and change in membership policy.
Changes to organization's purpose and articles.

mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With 13.8 years of experience and a revenue trend of 72.9%, the Maine Coast Fishermen's Association demonstrates strong fiscal health. Their mission aligns with sustainable resource management, emphasizing community support for fisheries, contributing to water access indirectly.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Community raised over $200,000 after a devastating fire on the Portland waterfront to support affected fishermen.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (89%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TO INFORM NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT, SUPPORT EQUITABLE ACCESS TO HEALTHY FISHERIES AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE LONG-TERM WELL-BEING OF COMMUNITY-BASED FISHERMEN, THEIR FAMILIES, BUSINESSES AND COMMUNITIES IN MAINE.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE ORGANIZATION FILED AN H ELECTION DURING THE 2024 TAX YEAR IN ANTICIPATION OF CONDUCTING LOBBYING ACTIVITIES.

IN 2024, WINTER STORMS DEVASTATED MAINES WORKING WATERFRONT RESULTING IN A FEDERAL DISASTER DECLARATION. THE MAINE COAST FISHERMENS ASSOCIATION (MCFA) AWARDED $5,000 GRANTS TO 40 FISHERY-RELATED, WORKING WATERFRONT PROPERTY OWNERS (2 NONPROFIT AGENCIES, 15 BUSINESSES AND 23 SOLE PROPRIETORS) TO ASSIST WITH REBUILDING EFFORTS. GRANTS WERE MADE FOLLOWING AN OPEN APPLICATION PROCESS WHICH ASKED ABOUT DAMAGES AND REBUILDING PLANS. SELECTED RECIPIENTS ACKNOWLEDGED INTENDED USE OF GRANT UPON RECEIPT, AND MCFA HAS FOLLOWED UP ON PROGRESS.

TWO OF THE PERSONS ON OUR BOARD OF DIRECTORS ARE BROTHERS

DOMESTIC NONPROFIT CORPORATION ARTICLES OF AMENDMENT WERE ADOPTED ON DECEMBER 17,2024:1) TO STATE THAT HENCEFORTH THERE WILL BE NO MEMBERS OF THE ORGANIZATION2) TO CHANGE THE PURPOSE OF THE ORGANIZATION AND RELATED TECHNICAL AND CLARIFYING REVISIONS TO INCLUDING AMENDING ARTICLES SECOND AND FIFTH, AS FOLLOWS:-SECOND: THE CORPORATION IS ORGANIZED AS A PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION UNDER TITLE 13-B, M.S.R.A. AND 501(C)3 OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE.-FIFTH: THERE SHALL BE NO CLASSES OF MEMBERS OR MEMBERS OF THE CORPORATION. THE BOX THERE SHALL BE NO MEMBERS ON THE ORIGINAL ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION IS HEREBY CHECKED SO AS TO APPLY.

TWO OF THE DIRECTORS ON THE BOARD OVERSEEING OUR NONPROFIT ARE BROTHERS

RESILIENT CITIES CATALYST, INC. LEASES EMPLOYEES FROM A PROFESSIONAL EMPLOYMENT ORGANIZATION, JUSTWORKS EMPLOYMENT GROUP LLC. THE EMPLOYEES ARE CONSIDERED COMMON LAW EMPLOYEES OF RESILIENT CITIES CATALYST, INC., HOWEVER, JUSTWORKS EMPLOYMENT GROUP LLC IS THE EMPLOYER OF RECORD AND EMPLOYEES GET THEIR W2S FROM JUSTWORKS EMPLOYMENT GROUP LLC.

RCC HAS NOT ESTABLISHED ANY COMMITTEES TO ACT ON BEHALF OF THE GOVERNING BODY.

RCC ASSOCIATE DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS REVIEWS THE 990 AND SENDS TO THE TREASUER OF THE BOARD AND RCC PRINCIPALS FOR THEIR REVIEW. A COPY OF THE 990 IS ALSO SHARED WITH THE GOVERNING BODY, RCC'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. ADDITIONALLY, THE 990 IS REVIEWED INDEPENDENTLY BY RCC'S CONTROLLER ORGANIZATION YPTC.

ALL BOARD MEMBERS DISCLOSE AND SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ANNUALLY. ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICTS ARE REVIEWED AT THE BOARD LEVEL. A DIRECTOR OR OFFICER WITH A CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS PROHIBITED FROM PARTICIPATING IN DELIBERATIONS AND DECISIONS REGARDING THE TRANSACTION. NO COVERED PERSON MAY ENGAGE IN ANY TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT OR UNDERTAKE POSITIONS WITH OTHER ORGANIZATIONS THAT INVOLVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, EXCEPT IN COMPLIANCE WITH THIS POLICY. COVERED PERSONS SHOULD AVOID BOTH ACTUAL CONFLICTS AND THE APPEARANCE OF CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. EVERY COVERED PERSON SHALL: DISCLOSE ALL ACTUAL AND POTENTIAL CONFLICTS AND RECUSE HIMSELF/HERSELF FROM VOTING ON ANY TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT IN WHICH HE/SHE HAS A POTENTIAL OR ACTUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST, AND SHALL NOT BE PRESENT WHEN ANY SUCH VOTE IS TAKEN.

RESILIENT CITIES CATALYST, INC. UTILIZED A REPORT BY AN HR CONSULTANCY PREPARED FOR THE ORGANIZATION WHICH TOOK INTO ACCOUNT THE AVERAGE AND MEDIAN SALARIES FOR NY-DC MARKETS IN DIFFERENT, DEFINED BAND AREAS.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.name: Squam Lakes Association
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Conserves the natural beauty and resources of the Squam Lakes area.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Promotes lake protection, low impact boating education, and summer camp for environmental appreciation.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on lake conservation and stewardship.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Focused on lake protection with two main programs and a summer camp.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Mature organization with 70.7 years of activity, strong financial health.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: SQUAM LAKES ASSOCIATION has been active for over 70 years with strong financial health, $2.7M revenue, and a high current ratio indicating sustainability in its conservation mission.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Camping reservations for the 2026 season are now open.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (81%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: SLA is dedicated to conserving for the public benefit the natural beauty, peaceful character and unique resource values of the lakes surrounding area. In cooperation with local state authorities other conservation organizations, promotes the protection, careful use shared enjoyment of the lakes, mountains, forests, open spaces wildlife.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Summer camp for area children to instill an understanding and appreciation of the natural environment
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Lake use - promote low impact boating on the lakes through various education campaigns
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
The Association has been notified by the Internal Revenue Service that it is exempt from federal income taxes under Section 501c3 of the Internal Revenue Code. The Association is further classified as an organization that is not a private foundation under Section 509a3 of the Code. The most significant tax positions of the Organization are its assertion that it is exempt from income taxes and its determination of whether any amounts are subject to unrelated business tax UBIT. The Organization follows the guidance of Accounting Standards Codification ASC 740, Accounting for Income Taxes, related to uncertain income taxes, which prescribes a threshold of more likely than not for recognition and recognition of tax positions taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. All significant tax positions have been considered by management. It has been determined that it is more likely than not that all tax positions would be sustained upon examination by taxing authorities. Accordingly, no provision for income taxes has been recorded.

The Executive Director and an Officer of the Board review the 990 before filing.

Annually, Board Members are required to fill out and sign a related party/conflict of interest questionnaire as part of the Organizations external audit.

Annually, the Board goes into executive session to review and analyze employee salaries.

All of SLAs policies, governing documents and financial statements are available upon request.

mainOrgs.name: Watersheds United Vermont Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Empower community-based watershed groups to protect and restore Vermont's waters.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Subgrant program for watershed protection and restoration.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on watershed protection directly aligns with water conservation goals.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission centered on watershed protection in Vermont.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: WATERSHEDS UNITED VERMONT INC has been active for over six years, generating $1.9M in revenue with a strong revenue trend of 233.7%. It successfully manages subgrant programs, ensuring effective oversight and community engagement in water stewardship.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Grant release for enhancement clean water projects announced on 03/24/2026.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: WUVS MISSION IS TO EMPOWER COMMUNITY-BASED WATERSHED GROUPS IN ALL PARTS OF THE STATE TO PROTECT AND RESTORE VERMONTS WATERS.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
WATERSHEDS UNITED VERMONT (WUV) OPERATES A SUBGRANT PROGRAM TO SUPPORT THE PROTECTION AND RESTORE VERMONTS WATERS. FOR THESE SUBGRANT PROGRAMS, WUV DEVELOPS APPLICATION MATERIALS SENDS OUT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS, AND ESTABLISHES A GRANT REVIEW TEAM TO ASSESS THE VALUE AND ELIGIBILITY OF THE PROJECT. WUV DEVELOPS SUBGRANT CONTRACTS WITH ALL ENTITIES AND OVERSEES THE PROGRAM INCLUDING EVALUATION OF ALL REPORTS AND DELIVERABLES FROM SUBGRANTEES. WUV FOLLOWS ALL DEC SUBGRANT REQUIREMENT GUIDELINES. WUV WORKS WITH GROUPS TO SELECT ELIGIBLE PORJECTS, CREATES SUBGRANT AGREEMENTS WITH ENTITIES AND EVALUTES ALL DELIVERABLES INCLUDING FOLLOW UP MONITORING.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, BOOKKEEPER, BOARD TREASURER, AND BOARD CHAIR REVIEW THE FORM 990.

THE BOARD CHAIR IS RESPONSIBLE FOR REQUESTING THAT ALL DIRECTORS DISCLOSE ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST.

UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.name: Wetland Trust
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Restore, conserve, and protect wetland biota, functions, and values.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Restoring wetlands on private and public lands, securing conservation agreements.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Direct focus on wetland restoration and conservation aligns with water stewardship.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission centered around wetland conservation.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: WETLAND TRUST has 16.4 years of experience with a strong financial position, evidenced by a current ratio of 25.04 and a revenue trend of 23.4%. Their mature mission focuses on wetland restoration and protection, enhancing their capacity for effective water resource stewardship.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Current initiatives include Eastern Hellbender Recovery, Hudson Valley Turtle Initiative, and Large Wetland Complex Preservation.
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE MISSION OF THE WETLAND TRUST IS TO RESTORE, CONSERVE AND PROTECT WETLAND BIOTA, FUNCTIONS AND VALUES. WE WILL WORK WITH PARTNERS TO RESTORE WETLANDS ON PRIVATE AND PUBLIC LANDS OR OUR OWN PROPERTIES. WE WILL HELP CONSERVE AND PROTECT WETLANDS BY HELPING SECURE CONSERVATION AGREEMENTS ON PRIVATE LAND AND, WHEN APPROPRIATE, ACQUIRING HIGH QUALITY PROPERTIES. WE CONSERVE AND PROTECT WETLANDS THROUGH FEE SIMPLE OWNERSHIP.

LAND CONSISTS OF LAND PARCELS THAT ARE HELD FOR CONSERVATION AND REHABILITATION PURPOSES. EACH LAND PARCEL IS CATALOGED IN THE ORGANIZATION'S ACCESS DATABASE AND WORK IS PERFORMED AS NECESSARY BASED ON NEED OR SPECIFIC GRANT REQUIREMENTS. LAND THAT IS ACQUIRED THROUGH PURCHASE OR DONATION IS NOT CAPITALIZED. LAND PARCELS PURCHASED ARE RECORDED AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITHOUT DONOR RESTRICTIONS IF PURCHASED WITH NET ASSETS WITHOUT DONOR RESTRICTIONS AND RECORDED AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITH DONOR RESTRICTIONS IF PURCHASED WITH DONOR-RESTRICTED ASSETS. CONTRIBUTIONS OF LAND ARE NOT RECOGNIZED IN THE STATEMENT OF ACTIVITIES AND CHANGE IN NET ASSETS. IF LAND IS SOLD, THE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE ARE USED TO PURCHASE ADDITIONAL LAND.

FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 49,465.

FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 49,465.

42.98 ACRES OF LAND IN THE TOWN OF NEWFIELD, COUNTY OF TOMPKINS AND 5.151 AND 38.376 ACRES OF LAND IN THE TOWN OF COEYMANS, COUNTY OF ALBANY ALL IN THE STATE OF NEW YORK WERE DONATED AS QUALIFIED CONSERVATION CONTRIBUTIONS. THESE CONTRIBUTIONS ARE NOT RECORDED IN THE STATEMENT OF REVENUE BECAUSE THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT CAPITALIZE ITS LAND CONSERVATION COLLECTION. LAND THAT IS ACQUIRED THROUGH PURCHASE OR DONATION IS NOT CAPITALIZED. CONTRIBUTIONS OF LAND ARE NOT RECOGNIZED IN THE STATEMENT OF ACTIVITIES AND CHANGE IN NET ASSETS. IF LAND IS SOLD, THE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE ARE USED TO PURCHASE ADDITIONAL LAND.

THE DRAFT OF THE FORM 990 IS EMAILED TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS PRIOR TO FILING AND SUBMISSION OF THE FINAL RETURN.

THE ORGANIZATION DISCUSSES THE COMPLIANCE WITH THE POLICY AT EVERY BOARD MEETING.

THE VICE CHAIR DID AN ANALYSIS OF EQUIVALENT POSITIONS FOR THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND DEVELOPED A JOB DESCRIPTION AND THEN THE BOARD NEGOTIATED WITH THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR ON SALARY, WHICH WAS WELL WITHIN THE GENERAL SALARY RANGE OF SIMILAR POSITIONS.

THE ORGANIZATION'S GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIALS STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

# The Wetland Trust | Restoring, conserving, and protecting wetlands

mainOrgs.websiteTextExcerpt:
Title: Mass Woodlands Institute

URL Source: https://www.masswoodlands.org/

Markdown Content:
## Welcome to the Massachusetts Woodlands Institute.

T he Massachusetts Woodlands Institute is a non-profit organization that encourages and assists landowners in responsibly managing their woodlands. We believe that taking an active role in managing your woods will benefit wildlife, the local community and economy, and can provide you with financial, recreational, and personal rewards.

MWI is subsidiary organization of the [Franklin Land Trust](http://franklinlandtrust.org/), and is housed at shared offices in Shelburne Falls, MA. By working with FLT, we are able to offer expertise and assistance to landowners about permanently conserving their land, as well as managing and caring for the land. We host the [Western Mass Wood](http://www.westernmasswood.org/) website with searchable listings,stories and marketplace for local wood products.

![Image 1](https://masswoodlands.org/media/1140/program1image.jpg)

## [Forest Stewardship](https://masswoodlands.org/programs/forest-stewardship/)

Licensed consulting foresters will work with you to develop a 10 year Forest Stewardship Plan based on your stewardship goals. Consulting foresters will recommend actions to protect or increase the environmental values of the land while yielding desired social or economic benefits.

![Image 2](https://masswoodlands.org/media/1138/program2image.jpg)

## [Foresters for the Birds](https://masswoodland

mainOrgs.name: Mecox Bay Conservancy Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: The Mecox Bay Conservancy improves water quality in Mecox Bay through research and eco-friendly strategies.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Contaminant reduction, marine ecosystem research, water quality restoration.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focus on restoring water quality in a specific bay aligns closely with core water conservation goals.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission centered on water quality improvement for Mecox Bay.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: Mecox Bay Conservancy has $1.5M in revenue and an impressive 2404 days of cash. As an emerging organization, its strong focus on local water quality restoration is promising.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (57%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: The Mecox Bay Conservancy (MBC) is a not-for-profit corporation that serves as a steward and watchdog organization for Mecox Bay. MBC works with marine biologists and other experts to further our understanding of the marine ecosystem of the bay and to develop and implement strategies to improve its water quality. Simply put, The Mecox Bay Conservancys goal is to restore the water quality of the bay to the highest levels of purity for both marine life and those who enjoy its waters for swimming, fishing and other recreational activities. Contaminant reduction strategies may include: Permeable Reactive Barriers (PRBs), Biofilters, storm drain retrofits, better control of farm runoff, increased flushing, aeration, nitrogen reducing sanitary systems and reduction of fertilizer and pesticide use on residential properties.

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Preserves Maine's working waterfront through sustainable regeneration, improving marine health, and protecting maritime heritage.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Supports marine economy with environmental science and educational opportunities.

mainOrgs.missionStatement: The Sea Meadow Marine Foundation preserves Maine's working waterfront through a collaborative, sustainable approach to regenerate our coast, improve marine and public health, and protect our maritime heritage. We strive to integrate environmental science and educational opportunities into our support of the marine economy.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
Chad Strater, president of Sea Meadow Marine Foundation, owns a company unrelated to Sea Meadow Marine Foundation known as The Boat Yard. This company engages in marine construction and has a franchise for sales of electric outboard motors. The Boat Yard leases space from Sea Meadow Marine Foundation. The lease fee is consistent with the rates charged to other tenants of Sea Meadow Marine Foundation.

Nick Planson and Chad Strater are related in that Chad's domestic partner is Nick's sister. Nick is the uncle of Chad's children. Nick and Chad are officers of Sea Meadow Marine Foundation.

The Board of Directors will approve the 990 before filing.

The organization does have a Conflict-of-Interest Policy. Officers are required to annually read the policy and sign a conflict-of-interest disclosure form. The organization does monitor compliance.

The 990 will be made available on guidestar and the IRS website.

Documents will be made available upon request.

## Waterfronts that Work

### We help preserve the working waterfront

Sea Meadow is a Maine non-profit dedicated to the working waterfront: keeping it open to all who earn a living from the sea, expanding opportunities for new livelihoods, and nurturing technological innovation.

We are based in our tenant-informed, collaborative working boat yard on the Cousins River in Yarmouth, and we work with a rapidly expanding collection of partners and projects across Maine. We exist to support and advance Maine’s working waterfront stakeholders, operators, and communities.

mainOrgs.name: Charles River Conservancy Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Amplifying community voices to enhance the Lower Charles River and its parks.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Floating wetlands for urban river resilience and community engagement.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on the health and vibrancy of the Charles River ecosystem.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Includes wetland installation and community art programs related to river stewardship.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over 25 years of experience, the Conservancy has a mature operational history and a strong revenue trend, demonstrating effective stewardship of urban river ecosystems.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Launched the Charles River Swimming Initiative for community engagement and improved water quality.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (97%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (66%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE CONSERVANCY WORKS TO AMPLIFY COMMUNITY VOICE AND CATALYZE BOLD SOLUTIONS, TO MAKE THE LOWER CHARLES RIVER AND ITS PARKS MORE VIBRANT, HEALTHY AND WELCOMING NOW AND IN THE FUTURE. OUR WORKS ALIGNS WITH 3 "C"S: CONNECT ORGANIZATIONS, PEOPLE AND COMMUNITIES TO EACH OTHER AND THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS. CREATE BOLD, IMPACTFUL SOLUTIONS THAT ELEVATE AND STRENGTHEN THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS AS AN ESSENTIAL ECOLOGICAL FOUNDATION AND CULTURAL ASSET FOR GREATER BOSTON. CELEBRATE THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS AND THE INTERSECTION OF PEOPLE AND NATURE TO INSPIRE INVOLVEMENT.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: CHARLES RIVER FLOATING WETLAND - THROUGH THE INSTALLATION AND MAINTENANCE OF A HUMAN-MADE ISLAND OF WETLAND PLANTS, THIS PROJECT IS EXPANDING THE TOOLS AVAILABLE TO SUPPORT A RESILIENT URBAN RIVER AND IS INCREASING THE COMMUNITY'S UNDERSTANDING OF LOCAL ECOSYSTEMS. THROUGH RESEARCH THAT HAS INCLUDED 3 YEARS OF WATER QUALITY MONITORING AND A FISH CAMERA, THIS FLOATING WETLAND IS PROVIDING INSIGHTS INTO HOW THESE INSTALLATIONS CAN BENEFIT AQUATIC ECOSYSTEMS. THROUGH YOUTH PROGRAMMING AND COMMUNITY OUTREACH, INCLUDING BUILD-YOUR-OWN-WETLAND KITS AND KAYAK TOURS, THE PROJECT IS ALSO A PLATFORM FOR EXPERIENTAL ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: LYNCH FAMILY SKATEPARK ART PROGRAM - AFTER LEADING THE DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION OF THE LYNCH FAMILY SKATEPARK (WHICH OPENED IN 2015 AND IS NOW OWNED AND MANAGED BY THE STATE), THE CRC HAS EMBARKED ON A COMMUNITY-LED DESIGN PROCESS TO SUPPORT THE SOCIAL AND PHYSICAL INFRASTRUCTURE OF THE PARK, WHICH WILL CULMINATE IN PUBLIC ARTWORK THAT CELEBRATES PARK USERS AND INVITES NEW AUDIENCES TO THE RIVERFRONT.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE CONSERVANCY WORKS TO AMPLIFY COMMUNITY VOICE AND CATALYZE BOLD SOLUTIONS, TO MAKE THE LOWER CHARLES RIVER AND ITS PARKS MORE VIBRANT, HEALTHY AND WELCOMING NOW AND IN THE FUTURE. OUR WORKS ALIGNS WITH 3 "C"S: CONNECT ORGANIZATIONS, PEOPLE AND COMMUNITIES TO EACH OTHER AND THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS. CREATE BOLD, IMPACTFUL SOLUTIONS THAT ELEVATE AND STRENGTHEN THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS AS AN ESSENTIAL ECOLOGICAL FOUNDATION AND CULTURAL ASSET FOR GREATER BOSTON. CELEBRATE THE RIVER AND ITS PARKS AND THE INTERSECTION OF PEOPLE AND NATURE TO INSPIRE INVOLVEMENT.

ALL OTHER PROGRAMS - THE CHARLES RIVER CONSERVANCY STRIVES TO MAKE THE CHARLES RIVER AND ITS PARKS A WELL-MAINTAINED NETWORK OF NATURAL URBAN PLACES THAT INVITE AND ENGAGE ALL IN THEIR USE AND STEWARDSHIP. IN ADDITION TO THE PROGRAMS DISCUSSED IN 4A-C, OUR WORK SEEKS TO INCREASE ENGAGEMENT WITH, ACCESS TO AND UNDERSTANDING OF THESE PUBLIC NATURAL RESOURCES THROUGH A VARIETY OF OTHER PROGRAMMING, ADVOCACY AND OUTREACH.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS HAS DELEGATED RESPONSIBILITY FOR REVIEW OF FORM 990 TO THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR. DIRECTORS RECEIVE A COPY OF THE FORM 990 VIA EMAIL WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMMENT BEFORE FILING.

MEMBERS FOR THE BOARD ARE REQUIRED ANNUALLY TO COMPLETE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST FORM. THOSE FORMS ARE DISTRIBUTED TO THE ENTIRE BOARD AND THE BOARD REVIEWS AND HAS AN OPPORTUNITY TO DISCUSS THEM AT A BOARD MEETING.

BOARD MEMBERS RESEARCHED THE SALARIES AT COMPARABLE NOT-FOR-PROFITS IN THE BOSTON AREA. THE SALARY WAS FURTHER CONFIRMED BY THE EXECUTIVE SEARCH FIRM RETAINED TO CONDUCT THE SEARCH FOR EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR. COMPENSATION AND NEW HIRES WERE ALSO INFORMED BY/CONSISTENT WITH A COMPENSATION AND BENEFIT SURVEY PERFORMED BY A LOCAL NONPROFIT WITH A MEMBERSHIP OF ORGANIZATIONS COMPARABLE TO THE CONSERVANCY.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST. FORM 990 IS ALSO AVAILABLE ON THE WEBSITE.

CONSULTANTS - PROGRAM 110,241 31,500 11,590

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Collaborate with organizations and stakeholders to implement watershed management strategies in Chautauqua County.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Implementation of Chautauqua Lake Watershed Management Plan and Macrophite Management Strategy.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focuses exclusively on watershed management and water quality in Chautauqua Lake.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Dedicated to a single mission around watershed management.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over $1.8M in revenue and established for over 11 years, the Alliance demonstrates strong financial stability and a clear focus on watershed preservation.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (99%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE ALLIANCE WAS FORMED FOR THE PURPOSE OF WORKING IN COLLABORATION WITH LAKE AND WATERSHED-RELATED ORGANIZATIONS, MUNICIPALITIES, AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS IN CHAUTAUQUA COUNTY, NY, TO PROMOTE AND FACILITATE IMPLEMENTATION OF RECOMMENDATIONS FROM THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE WATERSHED MANAGEMENT PLAN AND THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE MACROPHYTE MANAGEMENT STRATEGY BY PRIORITIZING PROJECTS, SECURING FUNDING AND ALLOCATING RESOURCES.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE A LOCAL GRANT FINAL REPORT. FOR CERTAIN GRANTS, THE ALLIANCE REQUESTS INVOICES AND/OR OTHER DOCUMENTATION OF GRANT EXPENDITURES INCURRED.

THE ALLIANCE WAS FORMED FOR THE PURPOSE OF WORKING IN COLLABORATION WITH LAKE AND WATERSHED-RELATED ORGANIZATIONS, MUNICIPALITIES, AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS IN CHAUTAUQUA COUNTY, NY, TO PROMOTE AND FACILITATE IMPLEMENTATION OF RECOMMENDATIONS FROM THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE WATERSHED MANAGEMENT PLAN AND THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE MACROPHYTE MANAGEMENT STRATEGY BY PRIORITIZING PROJECTS, SECURING FUNDING AND ALLOCATING RESOURCES.

VOLUNTEERS INCLUDED INDIVIDUALS SERVING AS OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS OF THE ALLIANCE DURING THE YEAR.

MEMBERS OF THE ALLIANCE MUST FALL WITHIN ONE OR MORE OF THE CATEGORIES NOTED IN THE BY-LAWS OF THE ALLIANCE (ARTICLE 1, SECTION 1.2). THE CATEGORIES OF MEMBERS INCLUDE: 1.) COUNTY, TOWN OR VILLAGE LOCATED IN THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE WATERSHED (THE "WATERSHED"); 2.) SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT; 3.) NORTHERN CHAUTAUQUA LAKE SEWER DISTRICT; 4.) SOUTH AND CENTER CHAUTAUQUA LAKE SEWER DISTRICT; 5.) CHAUTAUQUA COUNTY VISITOR'S BUREAU; 6.) NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS LOCATED IN THE WATERSHED WITH A CHAUTAUQUA LAKE AND/OR WATERSHED RELATED MISSION OR PURPOSE; 7.) CHAUTAUQUA INSTITUTION; 8.) COUNTY CHAMBER OF COMMERCE; 9.) ADDITIONAL ENTITIES WITH A RELATIONSHIP TO THE CHAUTAUQUA LAKE OR WATERSHED DESIGNATED BY THE MEMBERS FROM TIME TO TIME. MEMBERSHIP REQUIREMENTS, IN ADDITION TO THOSE SET IN ARTICLE 1 OF THE BY- LAWS, SHALL BE SET BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

A MEMBER CAN NOMINATE A CANDIDATE FOR THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE ALLIANCE BY GIVING PROPER NOTICE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE BY-LAWS. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS IS ELECTED BY A MAJORITY VOTE OF THE MEMBERS AT THE ANNUAL MEETING OF THE MEMBERS. OFFICERS OF THE ALLIANCE ARE ELECTED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

A COPY OF THE FORM 990 IS TO BE REVIEWED BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, TREASURER, AND THE AUDIT COMMITTTEE BEFORE SIGNING AND SUBMISSION. A COPY IS ALSO TO BE MADE AVAILABLE TO ALL OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS IN EITHER PAPER OR ELECTRONIC FORMAT FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO SUBMISSION.

BOTH STAFF AND BOARD MEMBERS ARE REQUIRED TO ANNUALLY COMPLETE AND DELIVER TO THE SECRETARY A WRITTEN STATEMENT OF ALL POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST THAT THE INDVIDUAL MAY HAVE AT THE TIME. ANY BOARD OR STAFF MEMBER WHO HAS A POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST IN ANY MATTER COMING BEFORE THE BOARD OR BEFORE ANY OF ITS COMMITTEES SHALL NOT PARTICIPATE IN ANY DISCUSSION OF OR VOTE IN CONNECTION WITH THE MATTER. AT THE DISCRETION OF THE BOARD OR COMMITTEE CHAIR, THE BOARD OR STAFF MEMBER WITH THE POTENTIAL CONFLICT CAN BE ASKED TO LEAVE THE MEETING UNTIL THE DISCUSSION AND VOTE HAVE BEEN COMPLETED.

GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, THE ALLIANCE'S INDEPENDENTLY COMPILED, REVIEWED OR AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND EXEMPT ORGRANIZATION INFORMATION RETURNS MAY BE REQUESTED AT THE ALLIANCE'S BUSINESS OFFICE.

mainOrgs.name: Save Barnegat Bay Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Restore and protect Barnegat Bay and its ecosystem through education and advocacy.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education and outreach programs, community partnerships, and native plant demonstrations.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on water ecosystem protection and education within the Barnegat Bay watershed.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Engages in education, outreach, and community projects related to watershed health.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Strong community engagement with over 100 educational programs and numerous partnerships.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: SAVE BARNEGAT BAY has been established for over 10 years with $907K revenue and a solid program expense ratio. Their community-focused initiatives demonstrate strong capability in water conservation and ecosystem restoration.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Launched multiple community programs and advocacy campaigns, including 'Charting A Course' and 'Clean-Up Crew'.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (81%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: OUR MISSION IS TO RESTORE AND PROTECT BARNEGAT BAY AND ITS ECOSYSTEM. WE ADVANCE OUR MISSION BY BEING A STRONG AND INDEPENDENT VOICE FOR THE BAY THROUGHOUT THE WATERSHED, INCLUDING ALL OF OCEAN AND PART OF MONMOUTH COUNTIES. WE DO THIS THROUGH EFFECTIVE EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ADVOCACY CAMPAIGNS THAT ENGAGE OUR SUPPORTERS WHO CHERISH THE BAY AS A NATURAL, RECREATIONAL, AND ECONOMICAL RESOURCE FOR THE COMMUNITY AND ALL OF NEW JERSEY. OUR SUPPORTERS GENEROUSLY BACK THE WORK OF SAVE BARNEGAT BAY, GIVING US FLEXIBILITY AND A STRONG FOUNDATION FROM WHICH TO GROW.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: EDUCATION AND OUTREACHFOLLOWING THE PRINCIPLE THAT AWARENESS LEADS TO EDUCATION, AND EDUCATION EMPOWERS ACTION, SAVE BARNEGAT BAY DELIVERED OVER 100 PROGRAMS REACHING MORE THAN 5,000 PARTICIPANTS AND GENERATING ? 16,000 IMPRESSIONS ACROSS 25+ MUNICIPALITIES.HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDED THE THIS WAY TO BARNEGAT BAY SCHOOL INITIATIVE, THE SUMMER STEAM SERIES, AND TEACHER PROFESSIONAL-DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS TOTALING 840 HOURS. THROUGH THE SMART YARD HEALTHY BAY CAMPAIGN, SBB BUILT OR DISTRIBUTED 258 RAIN BARRELS ACROSS EIGHT TOWNS, HELPING RESIDENTS REDUCE STORMWATER RUNOFF. THE STUDENT GRANT PROGRAM SUPPORTED 17 STUDENTS ON 7 PROJECTS PRESENTED PUBLICLY DURING THE SUMMER. SBB ALSO HOSTED THREE SCREENINGS OF THE DOCUMENTARY DRIFT, REACHING 140 ATTENDEES.GRANT-FUNDED INITIATIVES INCLUDED STOP THE STING (JELLYFISH POLYP ABATEMENT), THE TOMS RIVER PATHOGEN TRACK-DOWN PROJECT, AND THE MUNICIPAL STORMWATER EDUCATION INITIATIVE.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: PROJECTS AND COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIPSSBB MAINTAINED FIVE NATIVE-PLANT DEMONSTRATION GARDENS AND EXPANDED THE EMILY DECAMP HERBARIUM COLLECTION AT ISLAND BEACH STATE PARK, GEORGIAN COURT UNIVERSITY, AND THE ECOCENTER. THE ECOCENTER SERVED AS A COMMUNITY HUB FOR SCOUT TROOPS, GREEN TEAMS, AND PARTNER NONPROFITS. SBB LED EIGHT CLEANUPS THAT MOBILIZED 90 VOLUNTEERS FOR 195 HOURS, REMOVING 49 BAGS (? 150 LBS) OF LITTER FROM SITES IN WARETOWN, TOMS RIVER, JACKSON, POINT PLEASANT, BERKELEY, AND SEASIDE HEIGHTS. STAFF ALSO REPRESENTED SBB AT MAJOR REGIONAL EVENTS SUCH AS ACUA EARTH DAY (? 10,000 VISITORS) AND LBIF BAY DAY, EXPANDING OUTREACH AND MERCHANDISE FUNDRAISING.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
NO COMMITTEE MAY ACT ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD.

REVIEWED BY BOARD AT MONTHLY MEETING PRIOR TO FILING.

BOARD MEMBERS DISCLOSE POTENTIAL CONFLICTS TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS THROUGH OFFICER CHARGED WITH CORPORATE ETHICS RESPONSIBILITIES. ANNUAL DISCLSOURES ARE ALSO MADE.

SBB PARTICIPATED IN THE 2021-22 NJ CENTER FOR NONPROFITS SALARY & BENEFITS SURVEY AND THE BOARD USED THEIR MOST RECENT SALARY SURVEY CHART FOR CEO SALARY BY BUDGET LEVEL. SINE THE DATA WAS FROM MID-2021, THE BOARD TOOK INFLATION, LABOR FORCE SHORTAGES AND OTHER FACTORS INTO ACCOUNT WHEN CONSIDERING THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S CURRENT COMPENSATION PACKAGE.

UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.name: Friends Of Cobbossee Watershed District
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Engages communities in protecting and improving lakes, ponds, and streams in the Cobbossee Watershed.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education programs, shorefront protection, and community outreach through various hands-on activities.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on watershed protection and water quality improvement.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Several related programs addressing education and shorefront protection.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Operates an educational boat promoting water quality awareness.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With 23.9 years of experience and a strong financial position (current ratio of 7.92), the organization effectively engages local communities in critical water conservation efforts.

mainOrgs.missionStatement: TO ENGAGE INDIVIDUALS, BUSINESSES AND COMMUNITIES IN PROTECTING AND IMPROVING THE LAKES, PONDS AND STREAMS OF THE COBBOSSEE WATERSHED.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: EDUCATION PROGRAMS SCHOOL EDUCATION - PROVIDES YEAR-ROUND CLASSROOM PROGRAMS TO AREA ELEMENTARY, MIDDLE, AND HIGH SCHOOL CHILDREN FOCUSING ON WATERSHED AND WATER QUALITY ISSUES. FIELD TRIPS - OUTDOORS, MULTI-STATION LEARNING EVENTS. TADPOLE PATROL - HANDS-ON ACTIVITIES INCLUDING WATER TESTING AND SAMPLING TO LEARN MORE ABOUT WATERSHED EDUCATION, NON-POINT SOURCE POLLUTION AND INVASIVE AQUATIC PLANTS. NATURE DAY CAMPS - MULTI-DAY PROGRAMS OFFERED TO ELEMENTARY AGE SCHOOL CHILDREN DURING FEBRUARY AND APRIL VACATION AND COMBINES LESSONS WITH OUTSIDE ACTIVITIES THAT FOCUS ON ECOLOGY AND WATERSHED PROTECTION ISSUES.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: SHOREFRONT PROTECTION PROGRAMS SLOW THE FLOW - PROVIDES DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION SERVICES FOR EROSION CONTROL AND SHORELINE STABILIZATION PROJECTS FOR LAKE PROPERTY OWNERS THROUGHOUT THE WATERSHED. LAKE-SMART-START - PROVIDES AREA LAKEFRONT PROPERTY OWNERS WITH A SITE VISIT, RECOMMENDATIONS AND LANDSCAPING SKETCH FOR THEIR SPECIFIC PROPERTY.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
OTTER - THE OTTER IS THE BOAT OWNED BY THE ORGANIZATION THAT IS OPERATED IN THE SUMMER TO EDUCATE PEOPLE ON WHAT THEY CAN DO TO KEEP THE LAKES AND STREAMS CLEAN AND THE PROMOTE AWARENESS OF WATER QUALITY ISSUES. THE BOAT SPENDS MANY WEEKS ON MULTIPLE LAKES, ENLISTING NEW MEMBERS AND PROVIDING INFORMATION TO MANY PEOPLE.

WAYDE RANKIN DONNA DAVIS-RANKIN TREASURER SECRETARY MARRIED COUPLE MANY BOARD OF DIRECTOR MEMBERS

THE COMPLETED FORM 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR WHOM THEN FORWARDS TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS' PRESIDENT AND TREASURER. THE PRESIDENT AND TREASURER REVIEW THE FORM 990 AND APPROVE IT FOR FILING. COPIES OF THE 990 ARE DISTRIBUTED TO THE REMAINDER OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AFTER IT IS FILED.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS EVALUATES THE PERFORMANCE OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR ON AN ANNUAL BASIS. BASED ON THIS EVALUATION, COMPENSATION LEVELS ARE REVIEWED AND APPROVED. THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR PERFORMS ANNUAL REVIEWS OF ALL EMPLOYEES. COMPENSATION ADJUSTMENTS ARE APPROVED BASED ON THESE BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE WHEN A WRITTEN REQUEST IS RECEIVED BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR.

Markdown Content:
Lake Agawam Conservancy[mhnsn1](https://lakeagawam.org/author/mhnsn1/ "Posts by mhnsn1")2026-03-19T18:39:02-04:00

## Restoring Lake Agawam,

 its watershed, and the surrounding ecosystems

![Image 1](blob:http://localhost/fa185bc9ed96557884f58fe717f15cfd)

Lake Agawam Conservancy (LAC) is dedicated to reviving Lake Agawam, its watershed, and its surrounding ecosystems to protect human health and ecological vitality. The Lake’s deterioration is a symptom of the pollution that plagues all our local water bodies. Protecting this shared natural resource for future generations is of paramount importance. LAC works alongside Southampton’s community and elected officials to address the root sources of water contamination including toxic landscape chemicals and aging septic systems. LAC supports scientific monitoring of the Lake water and nature-based and scientifically innovative interventions to cleanse the Lake in the short and long term.

Our long planned, extensive wetlands restoration of the eastern shoreline of Lake Agawam along Gin Lane is nearing completion. After decades of neglect, that shoreline was sinking in rubble and consumed by invasive species—a 12 foot high wall of Japanese Knotweed and Phragmites that threatened the viability of native, water cleansing wetland plants.

![Image 2](https://lakeagawam.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/gin-lane-before.jpg)![I

mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: Maine Rivers is a mature organization with 23.2 years of operations and a healthy revenue stream of $853K. Its strong advocacy efforts and focus on river health showcase its commitment to water conservation.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Initiatives on fish passage restoration, including the successful spawning of alewives in China Lake for the first time since 1789.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (99%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: Maine Rivers is a statewide advocacy organization. Our mission is to protect, restore and enhance the ecological health of Maine's river systems. We support and direct efforts to conserve and improve the health of Maine's rivers and waters. We work both independently and in conjunction with like-minded individuals, groups, organizations and other entities to protect and restore Maine's rivers.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Maine Rivers is active directing and supporting on-the-ground restoration efforts to improve the health of waters in the Kennebec River watershed.This includes tributaries to the Sebasticook River flowing from Threemile Pond, Seaward Mills Stream and Webber Pond, as well as China Lake. Our efforts include attention to the Medomak River watershed where two impassible dams and improperly sized road culverts block fish passage for native sea-run fish species. We negotiated agreements for the rights to remove a high priority dam in Sabattus Stream and another high priority dam on Lemon Stream in the Sandy River watershed. Additionally we finalized an award for federal funding from NOAA that will bring +$12M for river restoration efforts in Maine's Merrymeeting Bay watersheds over three years.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
While the Maine Legislature was in session Maine Rivers monitored legislation relating to laws and programs that protect waterways in the State of Maine. During the months that correspond to this reporting period (April 1, 2024-March 31, 2025), Maine Rivers did not engage in activities which would require reigstering as a lobbyist as per the Maine Commission on Governmental Ethics and Elections Practices. We submitted testimony in support of "Resolve, Establishing the Commission to Study Unregulated Storm Water Pollution" as well as "An Act to Establish a Climate Superfund Cost Recovery Program to Impose Penalties on Climate Polluters." Maine Rivers maintains a strong interest in the federal Clean Water Act. We monitor initiatives that impact hydropower operations, however none of these interests involved lobbying more than 5 hours per month. Regarding II-B, Line G: Direct contract with legislators, their staff and government officials, or a legislative body: Testimony delivered to Committees of the Maine Legislature is limited to 3 minutes per bill, therefore communication totaled less than one hour for this reporting period.

Maine Rivers is a membership organization, as described in the Bylaws. An annual meeting is held to present relevant information and to elect officers.

Yes, members vote at the annual meeting.

photo courtesy of Linwood Riggs photo courtesy of Bridget Besaw

Our mission is to protect, restore and enhance the ecological health of Maine’s river systems. We depend on your support.

#### [China Lake Alewife Restoration Initiative](https://mainerivers.org/project/china-lake-alewife-restoration-initiative/)

With removal of the Edwards Dam in Augusta in 1999 and the removal of the Fort Halifax Dam in Winslow in 2008, sea-­run fish passage has been restored from the ocean directly to the Sebasticook River. In 2021 for the first time since 1789, native alewives were able to freely make their way to spawn in China Lake, then safety out migrate back to the ocean.

[![Image 1](https://mainerivers.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/medomak-broken-dam-long-800x500.jpeg)](https://mainerivers.org/project/medomak-brook/)

#### [Medomak Brook](https://mainerivers.org/project/medomak-brook/)

Maine Rivers is spearheading efforts to improve fish passage in the Medomak watershed, including alewife passage into and out of Washington Pond as well as at numerous other sites.

[READ MORE](https://mainerivers.org/project/medomak-brook/)

Home[gclwcfadmin](https://gclwcf.org/author/gclwcfadmin/ "Posts by gclwcfadmin")2022-07-21T02:19:30+00:00

## THE WATERSHED

###### Tucked away in the northwest corner of New Jersey, bounded by the Kittatinny ridge and acting as the primary feeder to the Paulinskill River, a jewel of nature exists in the form of two natural lakes. About 18,000 years ago, both Lake Owassa and Culver Lake were created by the last glacier to impact northern New Jersey and may have been connected at one time. As the watershed environment transitioned from forests to farmland to housing developments, years of sediment and nutrient loading very gradually affected the water quality and the lakes’ biology. This process is called eutrophication and can adversely affect water in the lakes and also in the Culver Brook, Paulinskill and Delaware Rivers.

## WHAT WE DO

## GREATER CULVER LAKE WATERSHED FACT

A goal of restoring water clarity to the 1950’s level, when Culver Lake was first monitored, can be met by diligent attention to watershed management, education and voluntary effort.

## YOUR SUPPORT MATTERS

###### The Greater Culver Lake Watershed Conservation Foundation (GCLWCF) relies solely on private tax-deductible donations from individuals, families, and foundations to fund its environmental advocacy programs.

###### Your generosity will ensure continuity of the water quality efforts not only at Culver Lake and Lake Owassa but

mainOrgs.notableSignals: Volunteer-driven organization with significant community engagement.

mainOrgs.missionStatement: of safe shellfish harvesting opportunities for both commercial and recreational users. QBC has established a series of specific activities which will attempts to: Mitigate the negative impacts of the Bay and Educate public users to protect the Bay.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Third: QBC is one of Maine's largest Invasive Green Crab trappers. QBC traps approximately 10,000 lbs. of Invasive Green Crabs out of Quahog Bay yearly.Fourth: QBC routinely performs stationary water sampling within Quahog Bay. Thesesamples are tested for pollutants, data is documented and shared with the State of Maine Marine Research Groups.Fifth: QBC has launched an Oyster farm within Quahog Bay. Each oyster filters 50 gallons of bay water per day and when they reach maturity, will be sold and profits go directly back into QBC.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
Form 990 is prepared by an outside tax professional. The form is then reviewed by the organization's management. After a full review, the final version of the tax return is provided to all members of the organization's voting body. A representative of management authorizes the final form 990 which is then e-filed with the internal revenue service.

Members of the Board of Directors review all potential conflicts of interest periodically. Top management and all Board members are required to disclose potential conflicts and any related party affiliations. The organization seeks full transparency on all relationships. Any potential conflicts (in fact or appearance) are discussed openly and resolved in accordance with the organization's policies and procedures.

The Organization's only paid employees are interns. All of the management activities of Quahog Bay Conservation are carried out by an all-volunteer force.

The Organization's only paid employees are interns. All of the management activities of Quahog Bay Conservation are carried out by an all-volunteer force.

Tax returns are available for download from several websites and by request from the organization's office in Harpswell, Maine.

ALL OF THE ORGANIZATION'S GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, AND OTHER LEGALFILINGS ARE MAINTAINED IN A SECURE ENVIRONMENT AND HELD AVAILABLE FOR INSPECTION BYTAX AUTHORITIES AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC. TAX RETURNS ARE POSTED ANNUALLY TOWWW.GUIDESTAR.ORG. THEY ARE ALSO AVAILABLE BY REQUEST FROM THE ORGANIZATION'S OFFICE.

## _Preserving the marine environment by 

promoting resilient maritime industry 

best practices & educating the public 

 to Save Our Seas._

![Image 1](https://namepa-website-media.s3.amazonaws.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/21133654/NAMEPA_Circle_N_2023-Transparent-320x320.png)

NAMEPA is a trusted resource for maritime professionals and community members working to protect our marine environment.

We advance MARPOL compliance, share practical best practices, and deliver education and workforce development programs that strengthen safety, environmental performance, and career pathways across the maritime industry.

Through partnerships with industry, educators, and communities, we support responsible decision-making that helps safeguard our oceans, lakes, and rivers for the long term.

## Our 2026 Annual Sponsors

[![Image 2](https://namepa-website-media.s3.amazonaws.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/17125255/OIP-9.webp)](http://www.intermepa.org/)

_**NAMEPA is a proud member of [INTERMEPA](http://intermepa.org/), joining a global network of leading maritime associations committed to advancing best practices and safeguarding our oceans.**_

## Stewardship

We bring together industry professionals, students, communities, seafarers, conservationists, & regulators to promote responsible practices.

mainOrgs.name: Delaware Bay And River Cooperative Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Control oil spills and promote public welfare in Delaware waterways.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Spill coverage and equipment access for safe cargo transportation.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Some connection to water quality and spill control in river systems.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Focused on spill control and safe transportation in waterways.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The Delaware Bay and River Cooperative, Inc. is an emerging organization focused on oil spill control, with $3.2M in revenue and an improving financial standing despite a slight revenue decline.
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TO PROMOTE THE WELFARE OF THE PUBLIC BY PLANNING AND PROVIDING FOR THE PROMPT AND EFFICIENT CONTROL OF OIL SPILLS, INCLUDING DISCHARGES OF PERSISTENT OIL OCCURRING ON OR IN THE WATERS AND HARBORS AND THE ABUTTING PUBLIC AND PRIVATE PROPERTIES AND BEACHES OF PORTIONS OF THE DELAWARE RIVER, DELAWARE BAY, DELAWARE-CHESAPEAKE CANAL AND SCHUYLKILL RIVER.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: THE SAFE TRANSPORTATION OF CARGO & PRESERVATION OF WATERWAYS THROUGH PROVIDING MEMBERS SPILL COVERAGE ON A PER-VOYAGE BASIS AND PROVIDING ACCESS TO EQUIPMENT TO THE EXTENT IT IS REQUIRED BY LAW.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE COOPERATIVE ADOPTED FASB ASC 740 - ACCOUNTING FOR UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES. FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2023 AND 2022, THE COOPERATIVE HAS DETERMINED IT DID NOT HAVE A MATERIAL TAX LIABILITY FOR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS.

THE MEMBERS OF THE DELAWARE BAY AND RIVER COOPERATIVE, INC CONSIST OF OIL COMPANIES WHO PAY DUES ESTABLISHED FOR ANY CLASS OF MEMBERSHIP.

EACH MEMBER COMPANY HAS A REPRESENTATIVE ON THE BOARD. THE BOARD ELECTS MEMBERS OF THE GOVERNING BODY.

mainOrgs.name: Water Well Trust Incorporated
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Aid domestic water well owners and support water quality and quantity.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education and financial support for safe drinking water through wells.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Directly addresses water quality and access through well support.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission centered on domestic water wells.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: WATER WELL TRUST INCORPORATED has been active for over 15 years with stable growth, $3.6M in revenue, and a high current ratio of 10, indicating strong financial health and capacity to support its mission in providing safe drinking water.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: The organization highlights ongoing efforts to provide clean water wells to families in rural America without access to clean water.
mainOrgs.missionStatement: WATER WELL TRUST INC SHALL PROVIDE EDUCATION AND FINANICAL SUPPORT TO AID DOMESTIC WATER WELL OWNERS AND WATER QUALITY AND QUANTITY PROVIDED BY DOMESTIC WATER WELLS. WATER WELL TRUST INC HAS THE FOLLOWING OBJECTIVES: TO PROVIDE WELLS TO THOSE IN THE UNITED STATES WHO DO NOT HAVE A SAFE DRINKING WATER SUPPLY IN THERIR HOMES; TO PROTECT WELLS BY IDENTFIFYING RESOURCES FOR REHABILITATING EXISTING WELLS OR CONSTRUCTING NEW WELLS WHERE NEEDED; TO PROVIDE SAFE DRINKING WATER TO LOW-INCOME AND MINORITY HOUSEHOLDS IN AREAS BEST SERVED BY WELLS; TO DEVELP PROJECTS THAT USE WELLS AS APART OF AN AFFORDABLE, SAFE DRINKING WATER DELIVERY SYSTEM; AND TO DEVELP AND DELIVER EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS TO PROMOTE WELLS AND TO PROTECT THE HEALTH AND DRINKING WATER SUPPLIES OF THOSE SERVED BY WELLS; AND TO PROVIDE SYPTIC SYSTEMS TO HOMES THAT ARE IN NEED OF PROPER DISPOSAL OF WASTE WATER.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
WWT IS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION AS DESCRIBED IN SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL AND STATE INCOME TAXES. ACCORDINGLY, NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES HAS BEEN MADE IN THE ACCOMPANYING STATEMENTS. THE CENTER FILES AN ANNUAL TAX RETURN FOR INFORMATION PURPOSES. WWT SHALL INITIALLY RECOGNIZE THE FINANCIAL STATEMENT EFFECTS OF A TAX POSITION WHEN IT IS MORE-LIKELY-THAN NOT, BASED ON THE TECHNICAL MERITS, THAT THE POSITION WILL BE SUSTAINED UPON EXAMINATION. IT ALSO PROVIDES GUIDANCE FOR DE-RECOGNITION, CLASSIFICATION, INTEREST AND PENALTIES, ACCOUNTING IN INTERIM PERIODS, DISCLOSURE, AND TRANSITION. WWT BELIEVES THAT IT HAS APPROPRIATE SUPPORT FOR ANY TAX POSITIONS TAKEN, AND AS SUCH, DOES NOT HAVE ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT ARE MATERIAL TO THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. WWTS ANNUAL TAX RETURNS ARE SUBJECT TO EXAMINATION BY AUTHORITIES FOR A PERIOD OF THREE YEARS FROM THE DATE THEY ARE FILED.

WATER WELL TRUST INC SHALL PROVIDE EDUCATION AND FINANICAL SUPPORT TO AID DOMESTIC WATER WELL OWNERS AND WATER QUALITY AND QUANTITY PROVIDED BY DOMESTIC WATER WELLS. WATER WELL TRUST INC HAS THE FOLLOWING OBJECTIVES: TO PROVIDE WELLS TO THOSE IN THE UNITED STATES WHO DO NOT HAVE A SAFE DRINKING WATER SUPPLY IN THERIR HOMES; TO PROTECT WELLS BY IDENTFIFYING RESOURCES FOR REHABILITATING EXISTING WELLS OR CONSTRUCTING NEW WELLS WHERE NEEDED; TO PROVIDE SAFE DRINKING WATER TO LOW-INCOME AND MINORITY HOUSEHOLDS IN AREAS BEST SERVED BY WELLS; TO DEVELP PROJECTS THAT USE WELLS AS APART OF AN AFFORDABLE, SAFE DRINKING WATER DELIVERY SYSTEM; AND TO DEVELP AND DELIVER EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS TO PROMOTE WELLS AND TO PROTECT THE HEALTH AND DRINKING WATER SUPPLIES OF THOSE SERVED BY WELLS; AND TO PROVIDE SYPTIC SYSTEMS TO HOMES THAT ARE IN NEED OF PROPER DISPOSAL OF WASTE WATER.

FORM 990'S DRAFT IS REVIEWED BY THE BOARD PRIOR TO FILING.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS REVIEWS AND DETERMINES THE COMPENSATION OF THE ORGANIZATION'S TOP MANAGEMENT.

DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.name: Driftless Area Land Conservancy Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Enhance health and beauty of Wisconsin's landscapes through land protection and restoration.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Advocacy for land protection and community outreach for education.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Strong focus on land conservation which includes water-related resources.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Engages in advocacy and education programs related to land and water conservation.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Mature organization with substantial volunteer engagement and community involvement.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With nearly 25 years of experience, DRIFTLESS AREA LAND CONSERVANCY operates with strong financial health and significant community engagement, uniquely positioned to impact local conservation efforts.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (94%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (77%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: MAINTAIN AND ENHANCE THE HEALTH, DIVERSITY AND BEAUTY OF SOUTHWEST WISCONSIN'S NATURAL AND AGRICULTURAL LANDSCAPE THROUGH PERMANENT LAND PROTECTION AND RESTORATION AND IMPROVE PEOPLE'S LIVES BY CONNECTING THEM TO THE LAND AND EACH OTHER.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: ADVOCACY - THE CONSERVANCY'S ADVOCACY EFFORTS FOCUS ON POLICIES AND PROGRAMS THAT SUPPORT THE LONG-TERM PROTECTION OF NATURAL, HISTORICAL, AND CULTURE RESOURCES OF SOUTHWEST WISCONSIN'S DRIFTLESS AREA. IN 2024, THE CONSERVANCY CONTINUED ITS EFFORTS TO OPPOSE A MASSIVE AND UNNEEDED TRANSMISSION LINE PROJECT. ACTIVITIES IN 2024 WERE LARGELY FOCUSED ON LEGAL MATTERS REGARDING THE PROPOSED CROSSING OF THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER NATIONAL FISH AND WILDLIFE REFUGE. THE CONSERVANCY ALSO CONTINUES TO BE INVOLVED IN ADVOCATING FOR THE KNOWLES NELSON STEWARDSHIP FUND WHICH SUPPORTS LAND CONSERVATION THROUGHOUT WISCONSIN.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: OUTREACH AND EDUCATION - THE CONSERVANCY'S COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT AND OUTREACH PROGRAMS AIM TO CONNECT PEOPLE TO THE LAND AND TO ONE ANOTHER. THE CONSERVANCY AIMS TO EDUCATE AND INSPIRE CURRENT AND FUTURE GENERATIONS TO PROTECT AND STEWARD OUR UNIQUE DRIFTLESS LANDSCAPE. WE CONTINUED TO BUILD OUR VOLUNTEER PROGRAM, AND HAVE ENGAGED 119 VOLUNTEERS OVER 1597 HOURS OF DONATED TIME. IN 2024, THE CONSERVANCY ENGAGED OVER 700 PEOPLE IN DALC'S MISSION AND WORK THROUGH IN-PERSON AND VIRTUAL PRESENTATIONS, FIELD TRIPS, VOLUNTEER WORK DAYS, GATHERINGS, AND EVENTS.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE CONSERVANCY'S POLICY WHEN FACED WITH ANY SUSPECTED CONSERVATION EASEMENT VIOLATION IS TO PROMPTLY INVESTIGATE AND DOCUMENT THE SUSPECTED VIOLATION, DETERMINE WHETHER IN FACT A VIOLATION OF THE EASEMENT HAS OCCURRED, DETERMINE THE CAUSE OR SOURCE OF THE VIOLATION, ASSESS THE IMPACT OF THE VIOLATION ON THE CONSERVATION VALUES OF THE PROPERTY PROTECTED BY THE EASEMENT, AND ESTABLISH AND IMPLEMENT AN APPROPRIATE REMEDY. WHENEVER POSSIBLE, THE CONSERVANCY WILL WORK WITH THE LANDOWNER TO HAVE THE LANDOWNER CORRECT THE SITUATION WITHOUT FURTHER ACTION OR INVOLVEMENT FROM THE CONSERVANCY.

CONSERVATION EASEMENTS ACCEPTED OR PURCHASED BY DRIFTLESS AREA LAND CONSERVANCY ARE NOT RECOGNIZED AS ASSETS OR REVENUE IN THE ACCOMPANYING FINANCIAL STATEMENTS BECAUSE DRIFTLESS AREA LAND CONSERVANCY DOES NOT HOLD FEE TITLE TO THESE PROPERTIES AND THERE ARE NO EXPECTED FURTHER ECONOMIC BENEFITS ASSOCIATED WITH THE EASEMENTS. IN ADDITION, CONSERVATION EASEMENTS CARRY SIGNIFICANT OBLIGATIONS TO MONITOR AND DEFEND THEIR TERMS. IF PURCHASED, THE COSTS OF CONSERVATION EASEMENTS ARE EXPENSED WHEN THE EASEMENTS ARE ACQUIRED.

DALC HAS ADOPTED ACCOUNTING GUIDANCE FOR RECOGNIZING AND MEASURING UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS. DALC FOLLOWS STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS FOR INCOME TAX ACCOUNTING AND GENERALLY AVOIDS RISKS ASSOCIATED WITH POTENTIALLY PROBLEMATIC TAX POSITIONS THAT MAY BE CHALLENGED UPON EXAMINATION. MANAGEMENT BELIEVES ANY LIABILITY RESULTING FROM TAXING AUTHORITIES IMPOSING INCOME TAXES FROM ACTIVITIES DEEMED TO BE UNRELATED TO DALC'S TAX EXEMPT STATUS WOULD NOT BE MATERIAL TO THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. DALC'S FEDERAL EXEMPT ORGANIZATION RETURNS ARE SUBJECT TO EXAMINATION BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE, GENERALLY FOR THREE YEARS AFTER THEY ARE FILED. WITH FEW EXCEPTIONS, DALC IS NO LONGER SUBJECT TO SUCH EXAMINATIONS FOR TAX YEARS BEFORE 2021.

THE PREPARED FORM 990 IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE MEMBERS OF THE GOVERNING BODY BEFORE THE RETURN IS FILED WITH THE IRS.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR COLLECTS AND REVIEWS CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENTS COMPLETED ANNUALLY BY EACH BOARD MEMBER AND COMMUNICATES TO APPLICABLE BOARD MEMBERS ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. BOARD MEMBERS WHO HAVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST ARE EXPECTED TO EXCUSE THEMSELVES FROM PARTICIPATION IN DELIBERATION AND DECISION MAKING ABOUT MATTERS INVOLVING THE CONFLICT.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AUTHORIZES THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S COMPENSATION ANNUALLY, BASED IN PART ON INFORMATION OBTAINED FROM INFORMALLY SURVEYING COMPENSATION PAID BY COMPARABLE ORGANIZATIONS. AUTHORIZATION IS DOCUMENTED IN THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S PERSONNEL FILE.

THE CONSERVANCY MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

REAL ESTATE: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 36,565. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 36,565. LAND MANAGEMENT: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 107,020. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 107,020. CONSULTING: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 14,365. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 13,974. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 28,339.

IN THE 2023 FORM 990, RIGHT-OF-USE ASSETS WAS REPORTED ON LINE 14. FOR CURRENT YEAR, THESE AMOUNTS HAVE BEEN RECLASSIFIED TO LINE 15 TO PROPERLY REFLECT RIGHT-OF-USE ASSETS IN ACCORDANCE WITH FORM 990 INSTRUCTIONS.

mainOrgs.name: Water Finance Exchange Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: WATER FINANCE EXCHANGE seeks to boost funding solutions for U.S. water systems, focusing on rural and disadvantaged communities.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Project Development and Partnership Development to connect communities with financial resources.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Strong focus on enhancing funding for water systems, particularly in disadvantaged areas.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Two related programs centered around financial access and community support in water projects.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Established pre-development funds for Texas and rural water systems nationwide.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: WATER FINANCE EXCHANGE has been active for over 5 years with stable revenue of $2.8M. The organization emphasizes rural water challenges and shows strong financial planning through its pre-development funds.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: No recent news or events listed on the website.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (98%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE WATER FINANCE EXCHANGE'S (WFX) OBJECTIVE IS TO SIGNIFICANTLY INCREASE FUNDING SOLUTIONS IN THE U.S. WATER, WASTEWATER AND STORMWATER SYSTEMS, WITH EMPHASIS ON RURAL AND DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: PROJECT DEVELOPMENT. WFX DOES NOT INTEND TO BE A PRIMARY FUNDER OF WATER INFRASTRUCTURE; RATHER, ITS FINANCE ACTIVITIES ARE NARROWLY DESIGNED TO FILL COMMUNITY GAPS THAT ARE PREVENTING FULL FINANCING FROM OCCURRING. WFX CONNECTS COMMUNITIES TO THE RIGHT EXPERTISE AND ACCESSIBLE FINANCIAL RESOURCES WHICH HELP TO SUPPORT PRE-DEVELOPMENT AND DE-RISK PROJECTS.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: PARTNERSHIP DEVELOPMENT (FACILITATE INVESTMENT AND BUILD SELF-SUPPORTING NETWORKS). WFX BELIEVES THAT IT CAN SUPPORT THE EFFORTS OF SMALLER COMMUNITIES TO ACCESS FUNDING BY ASSEMBLING THE NEEDED EXPERTISE, BUILD TRUSTED RELATIONSHIPS WITH COMMUNITIES, COMPLETE PROJECTS, AND LEARN AND DISSEMINATE INFORMATION ABOUT RECURRING ISSUES AND SOLUTIONS THAT ALLOW FOR SCALING-UP FUNDING OF SIMILARLY SITUATED COMMUNITIES AND REGIONAL INITIATIVES THAT WOULD OTHERWISE BE UNAVAILABLE. WFX WILL CONTINUE TO SERVE AS A NATIONAL PLATFORM TO ACCELERATE THE ADOPTION OF BEST PRACTICES, INCREASE THE ABILITY OF SMALLER COMMUNITIES TO ACCESS FINANCING, AND CLOSE THE WATER INFRASTRUCTURE EQUITY GAP FOR SMALL AND RURAL COMMUNITIES.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
WFX'S FLEXIBLE PRE-DEVELOPMENT FUNDS OFFER FORGIVABLE LOANS TO COMMUNITIES UP TO $250,000 AND MAY BE UTILIZED TO ADDRESS FINANCING ROADBLOCKS OR SERVE AS BRIDGE LOANS. WFX CAN QUICKLY DEPLOY FUNDS TO HELP COMPLETE CATALYTIC TASKS TO ADVANCE INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS. WFX HAS ESTABLISHED A PRE-DEVELOPMENT FUND THAT IS FOCUSED SPECIFICALLY ON TEXAS. THE EXISTING BALANCE, EXCLUDING OUTSTANDING LOANS IS $850,051 WFX HAS ALSO ESTABLISHED A NATIONAL PRE-DEVELOPMENT FUND SPECIFICALLY FOR RURAL WATER SYSTEMS THROUGHOUT THE US. THE EXISTING BALANCE IS $269,052. THE BALANCE OF LOANS RECEIVABLE IS $220,358.

BOARD REVIEWS AND APPROVES FORM 990 FOR FILING

DURING ROUTINE MEETINGS OF ITS DIRECTORS, WFX POLICY REQUIRES EACH DIRECTOR TO DISCLOSE POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST, IN ADDITION TO SIGNING A CONFLICT OF INTEREST AGREEMENT ON AN ANNUAL BASIS.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND CONFLICTS OF INTEREST POLICY AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

THE 990 TAX FORM WILL BE REVIEWED BY THE FINANCE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, AND THE COMMITTEE WILL RECOMMEND TO THE FULL BOARD THAT THE 990 TAX FORM BE APPROVED AND FILED.

THE INTERESTED PARTY SHALL HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO PROVIDE FACTUAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE PROPOSED CONFLICT. THE DISINTERESTED MEMBERS OF THE BOARD MAY APPROVE THE PROPOSED CONFLICT UPON FINDING THAT IT IS IN THE BEST INTERESTS OF SOUTH CAROLINA RURAL WATER ASSOCIATION. IF THE BOARD DECIDES THAT THE INTERESTED PARTY HAS IN FACT FAILED TO DISCLOSE A POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST, THE BOARD SHALL TAKE SUCH DISCIPLINARY AND CORRECTIVE ACTION AS THE BOARD SHALL DETERMINE. THE MINUTES OF THE MEETINGS SHALL REFLECT THAT THE CONFLICT DISCLOSURE WAS MADE TO THE BOARD.

THE CEO, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR/PROGRAM MANAGER JOBS ARE REVIEWED BY THE PERSONNEL COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD ANNUALLY, AND ANY INCREASES IN COMPENSATION ARE APPROVED BY THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS, AS RECOMMENDED BY THE PERSONNEL COMMITTEE, AT THE LAST BOARD MEETING OF THE YEAR.

COMPENSATION PROCESS FOR OFFICERS, OTHER OFFICERS, OR KEY EMPLOYEES JOBS ARE REVIEWED BY THE CEO, AND ANY INCREASES IN COMPENSATION ARE RECOMMENDED BY THE CEO TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS APPROVES ANY INCREASES IN COMPENSATION.

DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE ORGANIZATION IS INCORPORATED AND IS A TAX EXEMPT ORGANIZATION UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. THE ORGANIZATION FILES ITS FEDERAL FORM 990 IN THE U.S. FEDERAL JURISDICTION AND A STATE REGISTRATION AT THE OFFICE OF THE STATE'S ATTORNEY GENERAL FOR THE STATE OF OHIO.

THE DRAFT FORM 990 WAS SHARED WITH THE FINANCE COMMITTEE CHAIR FOLLOWED BY THE FULL FINANCE COMMITTEE, ANY NECESSARY CHANGES WERE MADE AND THE FINAL VERSION WAS THEN PROVIDED TO THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS PRIOR TO FILING.

THE CLEVELAND WATER ALLIANCE HAS A WRITTEN POLICY THAT EACH BOARD MEMBER MUST ANNUALLY SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENT

THE PROCESS FOR DETERMINNG THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR COMPENSATION INCLUDED BENCHMARK OF OTHER SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS, BOTH REGIONAL AND THROUGHOUT THE UNITED STATES, WHICH WAS CONDUCTED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

CLEVELAND WATER ALLIANCE MAKES ITS FORM 990, GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST, EITHER BY PROVIDING COPIES OR PHYSICAL INSPECTION OF THE OFFICE.

CFRACC IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. AS SUCH, INCOME EARNED IN THE PERFORMANCE OF ITS EXEMPT PURPOSE IS NOT SUBJECT TO INCOME TAX. ANY INCOME EARNED THROUGH ACTIVITIES NOT RELATED TO ITS EXEMPT PURPOSE IS SUBJECT TO INCOME TAX AT NORMAL CORPORATE RATES. FOR THE YEARS THEN ENDED AUGUST 31, 2024, CFRACC HAD NO TAX LIABILITY ON UNRELATD BUSINESS ACTIVITY. THE ORGANIZATION BELIEVES THAT IT HAS APPROPRIATE SUPPORT FOR ANY TAX POSITIONS TAKEN, AND AS SUCH, DOES NOT HAVE ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT ARE MATERILA TO THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S COMPENSATION IS SET ANNUALLY BY THE CENTER FOR RURAL AFFAIRS' BOARD OF DIRECTORS AND IS BASED ON PERFORMANCE AND COMPARABLES WITHIN THE NONPROFIT INDUSTRY.

THE CFO REVIEWS THE FORM 990 BEFORE IT IS SUBMITTED.

CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS MONITORED BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECOTR. THE PORGAM COUNCIL, AND BOARD. IT THEN GOES TO THE BOARD FOR A VOTE AND IS RECORDED IN THE NOTES.

THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS DETERMINES AFTER MARKETPLACE COMPARISONS.

IT IS AVAILABLE ON THE CENTER FOR RURAL AFFAIRS' WEBSITE AND ALSO IS AVAILABLE UPON REQUESTS BY MAIL OR EMAIL.

mainOrgs.name: Potomac Riverkeeper Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Stop pollution and restore clean water in the Potomac and Shenandoah rivers.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Monitoring toxic algal outbreaks and PFAS levels; community engagement and cleanups.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on water quality and pollution in local rivers.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Programs include monitoring, cleanup, and educational initiatives regarding water quality.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Active community engagement with multiple partnerships for monitoring and cleanups.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over 25 years of experience and $1.6M in revenue, Potomac Riverkeeper Inc effectively engages communities to combat water pollution while maintaining strong financial health despite recent revenue decline.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Declining revenue trend (-45% over 3 yrs); Revenue concentrated in Contributions (88%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (79%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: To stop pollution and to restore clean water in the Potomac and Shenandoah rivers and their tributaries through enforcement and community engagement.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Shenandoah: We upgraded our monitoring equipment for toxic algal outbreaks so we dont have to rely on the state to determine when and where they occur. We filed multiple algae complaints with the Commonwealth to encourage the state to monitor for algal outbreaks. We organized a cleanup with 16 partner organizations of 62 sites on Earth Day, removing 60 tires and 200 bags of trash from the Shenandoah River System. We renewed our internship program with four universities: Shenandoah University, Howard University Law School, Trinity University, and Hood College. We also expanded partnerships with community groups led by people of color for stopping pollution, reducing public health risks to communities, and increasing recreational access programs.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Upper Potomac: We expanded our investigation and awareness campaign for PFAS (forever chemicals), including partnering with USGs on testing PFAS levels in fish tissue and blood and partnering with faculty at Frostburg State. We expanded our community science water quality monitoring program in partnership with Hood College to test for conventional pollutants and also acid mine drainage.

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Preserves natural and cultural resources of the Tar River Basin through partnerships to protect rural land and ensure clean water.

mainOrgs.notableSignals: Holds 98 conservation easements, established enforcement policy since 2011.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over 25 years of active engagement, the organization currently manages $1.7M in revenue and maintains strong financial health, indicated by a current ratio of 98.68 and extensive unrestricted funds for continued stewardship.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Participated in community outreach events to engage local landowners in conservation efforts.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (77%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TAR RIVER LAND CONSERVANCY PRESERVES THE NATURAL AND CULTURAL RESOURCES OF THE TAR RIVER BASIN AND SURROUNDING AREAS BY WORKING IN PARTNERSHIP WITH PRIVATE LANDOWNERS, PUBLIC AGENCIES AND OTHERS TO PROTECT RURAL LAND, RIPARIAN CORRIDORS AND ENSURE CLEAN WATER.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: PURCHASED A CONSERVATION EASEMENT ON 61.43 ACRES IN HALIFAX COUNTY, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO FISHING CREEK.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: PURCHASED A CONSERVATION EASEMENT ON 33.8 ACRES IN NASH COUNTY, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO FISHING CREEK.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
CONSERVATION EASEMENT ENFORCEMENT POLICY WAS ADOPTED IN 2011 BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

CONSERVATION EASEMENTS ARE NOT REPORTED AS INTERESTS IN LAND IN THE REVENUE AND EXPENSE STATEMENT. THE INCOME RECEIVED FROM GRANTS AND OTHER DONATIONS FOR THE ACQUISITIION OF EASEMENTS ARE REPORTED AND THE EXPENSES RELATED TO THE ACQUISITION OF EASEMENTS ARE REPORTED AS EXPENSES. EASEMENTS ARE LISTED AS AN ITEM ON THE BALANCE SHEET. TAR RIVER LAND CONSERVANCY ACQUIRES CONSERVATION EASEMENTS THROUGH A NUMBER OF WAYS. EASEMENTS ARE SOMETIMES DONATED, GIVING THE DONORS OPPORTUNITY FOR INCOME TAX AND ESTATE TAX BENEFITS. WHEN AVAILABLE FINANCIAL RESOURCES ARE PRESENT, TRLC MAY PURCHASE THE EASEMENT FROM A LANDOWNER. THIS PRACTICE ISGENERALLY REFERRED TO AS PURCHASING DEVELOPMENT RIGHTS OR "PDR". NO VALUE IS RECORDED FOR THES PDRS, AS THEY CANNOT BE SOLD OR TRANSFERRED AND CANNOT BE ENCUMBERED. TRLC CURRENTLY HOLDS 98 CONSERVATION EASEMENTS.

THE ORGANIZATION'S ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE USED FOR THE STEWARDSHIP AND LEGAL DEFENSE OF CONSERVATION EASEMENTS HELD BY THE ORGANIZATION AND FOR THE STEWARDSHIP AND LEGAL DEFENSE OF FEE PROPERTIES OWNED BY THE ORGANIZATION.

TAR RIVER LAND CONSERVANCY DOES NOT BELIEVE THERE ARE ANY UNRECOGNIZED TAX BENEFITS OR COSTS AS OF SEPTEMBER 30, 2024. INCOME TAX RETURNS FROM 2021 THROUGH 2023 ARE OPEN TO EXAMINATION BY TAX AUTHORITIES.

LINDA FISHER'S TENURE ON THE TRLC BOARD OF DIRECTORS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2021. WILDLANDS ENGINEERING, INC., OF CHARLOTTE, NC, ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT WITH MS. FISHER TO COMPLETE A MITIGATION PROJECT ON HER PROPERTY. WILDLANDS AND MS. FISHER NEGOTIATED THE PROJECT INDEPENDENTLY OF TRLC, AND TRLC WAS NOT INVOLVED IN DETERMINING ANY FINANCIAL OR OTHER BENEFITS PROVIDED TO MS. FISHER BY WILDLANDS. TO COMPLETE THE PROJECT, A THIRD-PARTY WAS NEEDED TO HOLD THE PERMANENT CONSERVATION EASEMENT ENCUMBERING THE MITIGATION AREAS. TRLC EVALUATED THE CONSERVATION BENEFITS OF THE PROJECT, AND THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, FOLLOWING ITS WRITTEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AGREED TO HOLD THE CONSERVATION EASEMENT AT ITS DECEMBER 20, 2023 BOARD OF DIRECTORS MEETING. THE CONSERVATION EASEMENT GRANTED BY MS. FISHER TO TRLC IS OF RECORD IN DEED BOOK 3366, PAGE 642-674, NASH COUNTY REGISTER OF DEEDS.

THERE WAS ONE DONATION OF LAND IN THE CURRENT YEAR - PARCELS ON ROCK SPRING CHURCH ROAD IN GRANVILLE COUNTY NC.

TAR RIVER LAND CONSERVANCY PRESERVES THE NATURAL AND CULTURAL RESOURCES OF THE TAR RIVER BASIN AND SURROUNDING AREAS BY WORKING IN PARTNERSHIP WITH PRIVATE LANDOWNERS, PUBLIC AGENCIES AND OTHERS TO PROTECT RURAL LAND, RIPARIAN CORRIDORS AND ENSURE CLEAN WATER.

TAR RIVER LAND CONSERVANCY ACHIEVED ITS LAND PROTECTION MISSION THROUGH THE FOLLOWING ACTIONS: 4) PURCHASED FEE SIMPLE TITLE TO 57.501 ACRES IN GRANVILLE COUNTY, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO KNAP OF REEDS CREEK. 5) PURCHASED FEE SIMPLE TITLE TO 246.66 ACRES IN NASH COUNTY, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO FISHING CREEK. 6) ACQUIRED FEE SIMPLE TITLE TO 20.11 ACRES, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO UNNAMED TRIBUTARIES IN THE UPPER NEUSE RIVER BASIN AND ADJOINS A LARGER TRACT OF TRLC PROTECTED LAND. 7) RECEIVED A DONATION OF A CONSERVATION EASEMENT TO 42.59 ACRES, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO SWIFT CREEK. THIS CONSERVATION EASEMENT IS TIED TO A MITIGATION PROJECT. 8) PURCHASED FEE SIMPLE TITLE TO 50.07 ACRES, WHICH LAND PROVIDES BUFFERS TO FISHING CREEK. 9) AWARDED ACCREDITATION RENEWAL BY THE LAND TRUST ACCREDITATION COMMISSION. RENEWAL OF ACCREDITATION REQUIRES THAT A LAND TRUST DEMONSTRATE FINANCIAL ACCOUNTABILITY, STRONG ORGANIZATIONAL LEADERSHIP, SOUND TRANSACTIONS AND LASTING STEWARDSHIP OF THE LANDS THEY CONSERVE. 10) SPENT SIGNIFICANT TIME AND RESOURCES ON LAND PROTECTION PROJECTS LOCATED IN GRANVILLE COUNTY, WHICH LANDS WILL PROVIDE A BUFFER FOR CAMP BUTNER AND LOCAL RESOURCES. 11) SPENT SIGNIFICANT TIME AND RESOURCES ON LAND PROTECTION PROJECTS LOCATED IN PERSON COUNTY, WHICH LANDS WILL PROVIDE A BUFFER FOR FLAT RIVER AND LOCAL RESOURCES. 12) SPENT SIGNIFICANT TIME AND RESOURCES ON LAND PROTECTION PROJECTS AND OUTREACH TO LANDOWNERS IN HALIFAX AND NASH COUNTIES, WHICH LANDS PROVIDE BUFFERS TO FISHING CREEK AND LITTLE FISHING CREEK. 13) MONITORED CONSERVATION EASEMENTS AND FEE PROPERTIES HELD BY TRLC. 14) MONITORED 75 CONSERVATION EASEMENTS HELD BY THE STATE OF NORTH CAROLINA. 15) WORKED TO IMPROVE THE WATER QUALITY FOR FISHING CREEK BY PLANTING OVER 30,000 TREES, REMOVING OLD BUILDINGS, AND DECOMMISSIONING AN ANIMAL WASTE LAGOON ON A FARM FORMERLY USED FOR HOG PRODUCTION IN HALIFAX COUNTY THAT WAS PURCHASED BY TRLC LAST YEAR. THE CLEAN-UP AND TRANSFORMATION OF THIS PROPERTY IS A MULTI-YEAR PROJECT. 16) CONTINUED TO IMPROVE PUBLIC ACCESS TO NATURE TRAILS IN GRANVILLE COUNTY BY ADDING TWO NEW TRAIL LOOPS TO AN EXISTING PRESERVE, BY MAINTAINING EXISTING TRAILS THROUGH THE WORK OF A CREW OF DEDICATED LOCAL VOLUNTEERS AND OTHER GROUPS, AND BY WORKING TO COMPLETE AN ADDITIONAL NETWORK OF TRAILS THAT SHOULD BE OPENED TO THE PUBLIC DURING THE NEXT FISCAL YEAR.

LAYMAN ENTERPRISE, LLC 5336 WAYNE ROAD CHAMBERSBURG, PA 17202

THE 990 IS REVIEWED BY MANAGEMENT AND EMAILED TO BOARD MEMBERS FOR THEIR REVIEW.

BOARD MEMBERS ARE PROVIDED A COPY OF THE POLICY ANNUALLY. THEY READ AND SIGN A STATEMENT ACKNOWLEDGING THAT THEY HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THE POLICY. AT ANY BOARD MEETING WHERE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST MAY ARISE, THE BOARD UNDERTAKES AN ANALYSIS OF THE SITUATION BASED ON THE POLICY AND DETERMINES WHETHER OR NOT THERE IS A CONFLICT. IF THERE IS A CONFLICT, THEY HANDLE THE ISSUE ACCORDING TO THE POLICY.

DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST

mainOrgs.name: Alice Ferguson Foundation Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Inspires environmental stewardship and equips people to protect nature.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Bridging the Watershed and capital projects for environmental education.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Strong environmental focus with specific water education programs.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Two related programs centered on environmental education and stewardship.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Located on a unique 330-acre educational campus.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The Alice Ferguson Foundation, active for 18 years, has a strong capacity with a current ratio of 6.34 and optimal unrestricted months. Its programs successfully educate thousands on environmental stewardship.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: They offer various educational programs for schools and community engagement events focusing on environmental stewardship.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Declining revenue trend (-12% over 3 yrs); Revenue concentrated in Contributions (85%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (65%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: The Alice Ferguson Foundation's mission is to inspire environmental stewardship by giving people the tools to understand, appreciate, and protect nature.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Bridging the Watershed: Bridging the Watershed is an outreach program of the Alice Ferguson Foundation, in partnership with the National Park Service and area school systems serving students and teachers in National Parks throughout the Washington, DC, Virginia and Maryland region. The purpose is to provide meaningful watershed educational experiences that connect students to their place in the natural world. This is accomplished by standards-based curriculum and field studies for students, teachers, and park rangers training.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Farm and Facilities/ Capital Projects:In 2024, the campus infrastructure was renovated to current energy efficiency standards. Among this work included a new solar array and HVAC conversion.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
Antique vehicle is provided for public display and preservation.

Management has evaluated the Foundation's tax positions and concluded that the Foundation's financial statements do not include any uncertain tax positions.

For over 70 years, the Alice Ferguson Foundation has been dedicated to inspiring environmental stewardship by equipping people with the tools to understand, appreciate, and protect nature. Through hands-on environmental education programs, we have reached hundreds of thousands of students across Maryland, Virginia, and Washington, DC. Located on a 330-acre property in Accokeek, Maryland, the Foundation's Hard Bargain Farm Environmental Campus offers a unique educational environment that connects students with the natural world. Our curriculum and outdoor education model provide immersive, STEM-focused, NGSS Standards based experiential learning. Our programs are designed to enhance student achievement in critical STEM subjects and align with the growing emphasis on environmental literacy as a requirement for student success. 2024 Impact: - Served 4,128 students through our day and overnight environmental education programs. - 1,852 students and community members participated in the Trash Free Potomac Watershed Initiative. - Hosted 540 children and community members through community events and summer camp day and overnight programs. - Delivered professional development workshops to 65 teachers.

Per the Bylaws, Alice Ferguson Foundation is a member organization with an Annual Meeting. Members must be 18 years of age or older to vote at the Annual Meeting and Special Meetings. Only members in good standing at the time of such meeting(s) shall be entitled to vote. All questions and elections shall be decided by majority of members present.

Members meet annually to elect the Board of Directors and Officers of the organization.

The Foundation's Articles and Bylaws may be amended by a majority vote of the members of the Foundation at any annual or special meeting, provided that the proposed changes have been included in the notice of the meeting.

The 990 was reviewed by the Executive Director and Controller prior to filing. After review, Form 990 is distributed in its entirety to all board members prior to its filing.

Board members, the Executive Director, and the Deputy Executive Director annually complete an affirmation of disclosure and compliance with the conflicts of interest policy. In addition, the Board of Directors at least annually discusses any issues or potential issues and stresses the importance of compliance with the policy.

After review of the Executive Director salary data from comparable organizations in the geographical area, the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors of the Alice Ferguson Foundation (President, Vice President, Secretary, and Treasurer) makes a recommendation on the salary to the board as a whole, which then acts on the recommendation.

Governing documents and form 990s are available to the public upon request and can be provided in electronic or paper format.

## **HELPING PEOPLE FIND JOY IN NATURE**

We are environmental educators sharing the wonder and excitement of the Potomac River watershed with thousands of students, teachers, and citizens each year. And each year, we can’t do it without your support.

Since we were founded 70 years ago, over 1 _million_ students from Maryland, Virginia, and Washington, DC have explored nature through the Alice Ferguson Foundation. Join us for your next [learning adventure](https://www.fergusonfoundation.org/our-programs/) or [special event](https://www.fergusonfoundation.org/events/).

## Become a Part of Our Story

* * *

For over 70 years, the Alice Ferguson Foundation has enriched the region through hands-on learning experiences, conservation efforts, and a deep commitment to fostering a love for the natural world. You can help support this legacy by getting involved and contributing to the Foundation’s mission of environmental education and community engagement.

![Image 1](https://www.fergusonfoundation.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/IMG_7418-520x780.jpg)

Join a community dedicated to environmental education, conservation, and inspiring a deeper connection to the natural world.
