mainOrgs.websiteTextExcerpt:
Title: Wildlands Conservation | Wildlands Conservation

URL Source: https://wildlandsconservation.org/

Markdown Content:
## Wildlands Mission and Projects

Wildlands Conservation’s mission is to conserve and manage wildlands. “Wildlands” is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit land trust and conservation organization composed of ecologists, land managers, and educators dedicated to keeping our lands wild for wildlife, people, and future generations.

### Land Protection

Wildlands helps landowners and assists them with determining the various options available to them for preserving their lands in perpetuity. We hold conservation easements for individuals, developments, and conservation banks, and provide easement monitoring and reporting to ensure commitments are being upheld.

[Read More](https://wildlandsconservation.org/land-protection/)

### Research

We advance our mission by conducting cutting edge research on listed species, native wildlife communities, and habitat management techniques to provide critical information to decision makers and land managers.

[Read More](https://wildlandsconservation.org/research/)

### Land Management

Wildlands understands the importance of stewardship. Our knowledgeable staff conducts management activities including prescribed burning, exotic species treatment, and native restoration plantings on both public and private conservation lands throughout Florida and the Southeast.

[Read More](https://wildlandsconservation.org/land-management/)

## Auth

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Protecting the Caribbean environment through research and community-based actions.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Sint Maarten Conservation, Water Smart Schools US Virgin Islands.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focuses on environmental protection with significant water-related initiatives.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Includes multiple related programs with a focus on water quality and waste management.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over 25 years in operation and $321K in revenue, EPIC demonstrates a solid financial standing. They successfully engage communities and promote sustainable practices crucial for Caribbean water resources.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: 2026 Caribbean Resiliency and Climate Conversations Webinar Series and BESTLife2030 project for environmental education.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (67%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: Protecting the Caribbean environment through research and community-based actions.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Sint Maarten Conservation - The locally led affiliate EPIC Sint Maarten is addressing waste disposal issues on the island through the Perpetual Plastics program, which takes plastic waste and converts it into usable items, while also educating the public about ways to reduce and reuse plastics. Sustainability is also promoted through the international Blue Flag and Green Key eco-labels, which are awarded to businesses that meet stringent environmental criteria. Regular litter cleanups get volunteers involved in improving coastal habitats while also raising awareness about the pervasive problem of litter. Habitat improvements also include native plant restoration and establishing secure nesting sites for seabirds while using outreach to increase concern for native flora and fauna. In addition, researchers are monitoring remnant bat populations to determine threats and management actions for conservation.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Water Smart Schools US Virgin Islands - A grant-funded project supported EPIC to collaborate with partners in the U.S. Virgin Islands on the Water Smart Schools VI program which completed its second year. It is a comprehensive drinking water safety and waste reduction program that was made available to seven public schools. Activities included the distribution of water quality testing kits for classrooms and facilitation of the Water Heroes water quality testing curriculum and the USVI Marine Debris curriculum, including participation in Eco-Schools, a school environmental audit, the opportunity to receive bronze level certification, and ongoing educational support. The goals achieved included increased access to clean safe drinking water for Virgin Islands school children, teachers and staff, reduced amount of plastic waste generated in schools, specifically through the reduction of plastic water bottles, increased literacy and awareness for Virgin Islands school children, teachers, and the wider community about issues of water quality and testing, water conservation and pollution, sources of freshwater, and plastic waste, including impacts on health and the environment.

Markdown Content:
Environmental Protection in the Caribbean (EPIC)

is an independent non-profit founded in 2000 with the mission of protecting the Caribbean environment through research and community-based actions.

EPIC’s 2025 Digital Annual Impact Report is now available. Explore key conservation milestones, community partnerships, and regional impact stories from across the Caribbean.

The 2026 Caribbean Resiliency and Climate Conversations Webinar Series brings together experts and organizations from across the region to share practical solutions for climate resilience. Explore upcoming sessions, watch past webinars, and register to join the conversation.

Across the U.S. Virgin Islands, restaurants are reducing waste through the VI Clean Coasts Eco-Certification program. EPIC has expanded outreach and communications, launched a new Eco-Certification webpage with an interactive map, and helped connect businesses to resources and funding to adopt sustainable, cost-effective practices that protect local communities and coastlines.

Students across Sint Maarten are gaining hands-on environmental education through classroom lessons and field visits to Fort Amsterdam. mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The Johnson Creek Watershed Association is a mature organization with a strong revenue trend of 27.8%. With a solid financial standing, strong community engagement, and successful stewardship programs, it effectively addresses key watershed issues.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Upcoming volunteer restoration events scheduled in May 2023.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Days cash below tier threshold (70 < 90); Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%); Staff shrinkage ≥20% YoY
mainOrgs.missionStatement: JOHNSON CREEK WATERSHED COUNCIL (THE ORGANIZATION) IS AN OREGON NONPROFIT CORPORATION FORMED IN 1995. THE ORGANIZATION IS AN OUTGROWTH OF CITIZEN GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT COLLABORATION TO DEVELOP COMMUNITY SOLUTIONS TO KEY WATERSHED ISSUES. OUR MISSION IS TO PROMOTE RESTORATION AND STEWARDSHIP OF A HEALTHY JOHNSON CREEK WATERSHED THROUGH SOUND SCIENCE AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: OUTREACH AND COMMUNITY STEWARDSHIP -MORE THAN 1,600 VOLUNTEERS SHOWED UP AT 82 STEWARDSHIP EVENTS TO PLANT 4,853 TREES, SHRUBS AND PLANTS, AND REMOVE 6.9 TONS OF GARBAGE FROM JOHNSON CREEK, AND INSTALL 8 NEW RAIN GARDENS ON NEWLY-DEPAVED LANDSCAPES. IN ADDITION, JCWCS AMERICORP MEMBER WORKED WITH YOUTH DURING 59 EVENTS INSPIRING THE NEXT GENERATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDS, SCIENTISTS AND ADVOCATES.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: MONITORING -JCWC PARTICIPATES IN THE INTERJURISDICTIONAL COMMITTEE INCLUDING THE COUNCIL AND 10 AGENCY PARTNERS WHOSE EFFORTS GATHER AND MONITOR WATER QUALITY, FISH AND WILDLIFE DATA THAT TELLS EVERYONE ABOUT THE HEALTH OF THE JOHNSON CREEK WATERSHED. FROM THIS DATA, JCWC IS BETTER ABLE TO PLAN BOTH RESTORATION PROJECTS AND COMMUNITY STEWARDSHIP EVENTS, BOTH OF WHICH HAVE IMMEASURABLE BENEFITS TO THE LANDS, WATERS, WILDLIFE, AND PEOPLE WHO LIVE, WORK AND PLAY IN THE WATERSHED.

mainOrgs.websiteTextExcerpt:
Title: Johnson Creek Watershed Council • Discover. Restore. Connect.

URL Source: https://www.jcwc.org/

Markdown Content:
#### A healthy watershed is within reach.

#### Promoting restoration and stewardship of a healthy Johnson Creek watershed through sound science and community engagement.

Johnson Creek flows 26 miles from its headwaters near Boring, Oregon, to where it meets the Willamette River. Along the way, it passes through forests, farms, golf courses, parks, industrial stretches, and neighborhoods, and through the four cities: Happy Valley, Gresham, Portland, and Milwaukie.

It’s a vibrant creek with many challenges.

## Our Work

![Image 1](https://www.jcwc.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/07/LYI-interns-sample-macroinvertebrates-at-Leach-rotated.jpg)

### Discover.

Science is at the heart of our efforts to restore ecological function and connect the upland, riparian, and in-stream habitats in the watershed. We also lead collaborative projects, write peer-reviewed publications, and offer opportunities to link research with on-the-ground restoration. Discover your Johnson Creek Watershed [here](https://www.jcwc.org/home/science/ "Science Hub").

![Image 2](https://www.jcwc.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/new-channel-flowing-into-property-from-the-south.jpg)

### Restore.

We do restoration throughout the watershed, leading projects, organizing volunteer work parties, assisting creekside landowners with stewardship, overseeing construction on culverts and dams, and part

THE FINANCE COMMITTEE REVIEWS THE FORM 990 AND THE ANNUAL FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND AUDIT REPORT AT SCHEDULED FINANCE COMMITTEE MEETINGS PRIOR TO ISSUANCE. THE FORM 990 AND THE ANNUAL FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND AUDIT REPORT ARE SUBMITTED BY THE FINANCE COMMITTEE TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO ISSUANCE. IF MEETINGS ARE NOT SCHEDULED DURING THE REQUIRED TIME, THE INFORMATION IS SENT TO THE FINANCE COMMITTEE AND BOARD MEMBERS FOR REVIEW.

ALL BOARD MEMBERS AND EMPLOYEES MUST DISCLOSE ANY INTERESTS THAT COULD GIVE RISE TO CONFLICTS AS SOON AS THEY ARE KNOWN. THIS IS DISCUSSED IN MEETINGS HELD WITH BOARD MEMBERS, MANAGEMENT AND STAFF ON A REGULAR BASIS.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS REVIEWS AND APPROVES THE PRESIDENT AND CEO'S COMPENSATION. THE CEO REVIEWS AND DETERMINES THE COMPENSATION FOR ALL OFFICERS AND OTHER KEY EMPLOYEES. PROJECT WET FOUNDATION USES NATIONAL, STATEWIDE, AND LOCAL COMPENSATION REPORTS FOR SALARY COMPARABILITY.

ALL DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION UPON REQUEST. CONTACT INFORMATION IS PROVIDED ON THE PROJECT WET WEBSITE.

THE FINANCE COMMITTEE AND BOARD OF DIRECTORS ASSUME RESPONSIBILITY FOR OVERSIGHT OF THE AUDIT AND SELECTION OF AN INDEPENDENT AUDITOR.

mainOrgs.name: Colorado Foundation For Water Education
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Educate Coloradans on water issues for sustainable future decisions.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education, outreach, leadership programs, and publications on water topics.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Strong focus on water education aligns closely with water quality and sustainability.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Programs include education, outreach, leadership, and awareness-building activities.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Flagship publication reaches over 7,500+ people, 2,500+ guides distributed annually.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The Colorado Foundation for Water Education has been active for 23 years with a mature status. Despite a revenue decline of 27.5%, it maintains strong liquidity with a current ratio of 6.19, indicating sound financial health for its educational mission.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Sustaining Colorado Watersheds Conference and various webinars scheduled for this year.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Declining revenue trend (-27% over 3 yrs); Revenue concentrated in Contributions (74%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (79%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: WATER EDUCATION COLORADO ENSURES COLORADANS ARE INFORMED ON WATER ISSUES AND EQUIPPED TO MAKE DECISIONS THAT GUIDE OUR STATE TO A SUSTAINABLE WATER FUTURE.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: OUR EDUCATION AND OUTREACH PROGRAMS INCLUDE TOURS, WEBINARS, WORKSHOPS, THE ANNUAL SUSTAINING COLORADO WATERSHEDS CONFERENCE, AND LEADERSHIP FOR THE STATEWIDE WATER EDUCATOR NETWORK, AS WELL AS A VARIETY OF PUBLIC AWARENESS-BUILDING ACTIVITIES TO CULTIVATE A CONNECTED AND ENGAGED NETWORK OF INFORMED DECISION MAKERS, CONSTITUENTS AND PRACTITIONERS. ------------------------- SEE SCHEDULE O -------------------------------
mainOrgs.program3Desc: OUR LEADERSHIP PROGRAMS, WATER LEADERS AND WATER FLUENCY, EQUIP LEADERS WITH THE CONFIDENCE AND SKILLS TO NAVIGATE THE COMPLEX WORLD OF COLORADO WATER AND ADVANCE COLLABORATIVE SOLUTIONS. ------------------------- SEE SCHEDULE O -------------------------------
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
SECTION 4A - CONTINUED NEWS AND PUBLICATIONS: HEADWATERS MAGAZINE, PRODUCED THREE TIMES PER YEAR, IS WATER EDUCATION COLORADO'S FLAGSHIP PUBLICATION, COVERING THE MOST PRESSING WATER ISSUES IN COLORADO WITH COMPELLING, IMPARTIAL REPORTING AND STORYTELLING. HEADWATERS IS DISTRIBUTED THROUGHOUT THE STATE OF COLORADO AND BEYOND, REACHING OVER 7,500+ PEOPLE WITH EACH ISSUE. WATER EDUCATION COLORADO'S HIGH-QUALITY REFERENCE GUIDE SERIES OF CITIZEN'S GUIDES BOASTS TEN GUIDES TO DIFFERENT WATER TOPICS, WHICH ARE USED IN A VARIETY OF EDUCATIONAL SETTINGS, INCLUDING WORKSHOPS, SEMINARS, MEMBER EVENTS, COMMUNITY PROGRAMMING, COLLEGE CURRICULUMS, AND MORE. EACH EDITION HAS A SHELF LIFE OF 5+ YEARS. MORE THAN 2,500+ GUIDES ARE DISTRIBUTED EACH YEAR. FRESH WATER NEWS IS AN INDEPENDENT, NONPARTISAN NEWS INITIATIVE LAUNCHED BY WECO IN 2018 TO PROVIDE TIMELY, IN-DEPTH COVERAGE OF WATER ISSUES ACROSS COLORADO AND THE WEST. SINCE ITS LAUNCH, FRESH WATER NEWS HAS PUBLISHED ORIGINAL REPORTING ON A WEEKLY BASIS. FRESH WATER NEWS HAS APPROXIMATELY 5,200 WEEKLY SUBSCRIBERS, AND FWN ARTICLES ARE ALSO REPUBLISHED BY NUMEROUS MEDIA PARTNERS ACROSS COLORADO. WECO ALSO OFFERS A VARIETY OF OTHER DIGITAL RESOURCES, INCLUDING A BLOG, WEBINARS, FACT SHEETS AND MORE. SECTION 4B - CONTINUED WATER EDUCATION COLORADO'S ANNUAL RIVER BASIN TOUR TAKES LEGISLATORS AND OTHER PUBLIC OFFICIALS, WATER MANAGERS, EDUCATORS AND MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC ON A MULTI-DAY TOUR OF A RIVER BASIN IN COLORADO. PARTICIPANTS HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO GAIN AN ON-THE-GROUND UNDERSTANDING OF THE HISTORY, WATER MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, AND CURRENT CHALLENGES OF THE LOCAL WATERSHEDS. VARIOUS SMALLER TOURS, WORKSHOPS AND WEBINARS HELD THROUGHOUT THE YEAR SHINE THE SPOTLIGHT ON A WIDE VARIETY OF CURRENT WATER ISSUES.THE SUSTAINING COLORADO WATERSHEDS CONFERENCE CONVENES 250 PROFESSIONALS ANNUALLY TO SHARE THEIR WORK IN ORDER TO EXPAND COOPERATION AND COLLABORATION THROUGHOUT COLORADO IN NATURAL RESOURCE CONSERVATION, PROTECTION AND ENHANCEMENT. THE WATER EDUCATOR NETWORK IS AN AFFILIATE GROUP OF EDUCATION AND OUTREACH PROFESSIONALS CONVENED AND SUPPORTED BY WECO TO ENHANCE THE AMOUNT, QUALITY AND EFFECTIVENESS OF WATER EDUCATION ACROSS THE STATE. COLLABORATION, RESOURCE SHARING, AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES ARE ALL BENEFITS OF THIS PROGRAM, WHICH ALSO INCLUDES ACCESS TO PROJECT WET CURRICULUM. IN 2022, WATER EDUCATION COLORADO SPEARHEADED THE WATER '22 PUBLIC AWARENESS CAMPAIGN, A YEAR-LONG INITIATIVE DEDICATED TO RAISING AWARENESS OF THE VALUE OF WATER, RISKS TO SUSTAINABILITY, OUR ROLE AS A HEADWATERS STATE, AND SIMPLE ACTIONS COLORADANS CAN TAKE TO CONSERVE AND PROTECT WATER QUALITY. SECTION 4C - CONTINUED LEADERSHIP PROGRAMS: THE WATER LEADERS PROGRAM TRAINS 20 WATER INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS EACH YEAR IN LEADERSHIP SKILLS, AND HAS GRADUATED MORE THAN 262 LEADERS SINCE 2006. THE WATER LEADERS PROGRAM POSITIVELY IMPACTS THE COLORADO WATER PROFESSION BY DEVELOPING A PIPELINE OF SKILLED WATER LEADERS ACROSS DIVERSE FIELDS READY TO CHART THE PATH FORWARD TO INNOVATIVE AND COLLABORATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS IN AN INCREASINGLY COMPLEX WORLD. THE WATER FLUENCY PROGRAM PROVIDES PARTICIPANTS WITH A PRACTICAL UNDERSTANDING OF COLORADO WATER RESOURCE ISSUES AND THE RELATED IMPLICATIONS TO LOCAL DECISION-MAKING PROCESSES. PARTICIPANTS LEARN THROUGH A COMBINATION OF EXPERT SPEAKER PRESENTATIONS, PEER DISCUSSION, READING ASSIGNMENTS AND INTERSESSION APPLICATIONS OF LEARNING. WATER FLUENCY REACHES 35 ELECTED OFFICIALS, CITY AND COUNTY STAFF, BOARD MEMBERS, COMMUNITY LEADERS, AND EDUCATORS EACH YEAR.

990 IS PREPARED BY OUTSIDE FINANCIAL EXPERT, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND TREASURER REVIEW 990 BEFORE SIGNING.

CONFLICTS OF INTEREST ARE DISCLOSED BY THE MEMBERS ON AN ONGOING BASIS AS CONFLICTS ARISE.

THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD REVIEWS THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S COMPENSATION ANNUALLY AND CONSIDERS THE SALARY SURVEY PUBLISHED BY THE COLORADO NONPROFIT ASSOCIATION AS A BENCHMARK IN COMPARING WHAT OTHER ORGANIZATIONS OF SIMILAR BUDGET SIZE, OPERATING IN THE DENVER METRO AREA, ARE PAYING THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTORS IN THEIR ORGANIZATIONS. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE ALSO REVIEWS THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S PERFORMANCE AS PART OF DETERMINING COMPENSATION ADJUSTMENTS.

UPON REQUEST

mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE ORGANIZATION QUALIFIES AS AN ORGANIZATION EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND A SIMILAR STATE STATUTE AND IS NOT SUBJECT TO TAX AT THE ENTITY LEVEL FOR FEDERAL AND STATE INCOME TAX PURPOSES. THE ORGANIZATION ACCOUNTS FOR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PROVISIONS OF FASB CODIFICATION TOPIC INCOME TAXES. FASB CODIFICATION TOPIC INCOME TAXES CLARIFIES THE ACCOUNTING FOR UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES AND REQUIRES THE ORGANIZATION TO RECOGNIZE IN THEIR FINANCIAL STATEMENTS THE IMPACT OF A TAX POSITION TAKEN OR EXPECTED TO BE TAKEN IN A TAX RETURN, IF THAT POSITION IS MORE LIKELY THAN NOT TO BE SUSTAINED UNDER AUDIT, BASED ON THE TECHNICAL MERITS OF THE POSITION. MANAGEMENT HAS ASSESSED THE TAX POSITIONS OF THE ORGANIZATION AND DETERMINED THAT NO POSITIONS EXIST THAT REQUIRE ADJUSTMENT OR DISCLOSURE UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF FASB CODIFICATION TOPIC INCOME TAXES. THE ORGANIZATION FILES INFORMATIONAL "RETURN OF ORGANIZATION EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAX" (FORM 990) IN THE U.S. FEDERAL JURISDICTION AND ARKANSAS.

THE 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE BOARD

THE BOARD REVIEWS AND DISCUSSES ANNUALLY

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS FORM 990 AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT TAX CONSULTANT, MANAGEMENT REVIEWS THE FORM 990 AND PROVIDES A COPY TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE IRS.

THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS DISCUSSED AT THE TIME OF THE BOARD MEETINGS TO REMIND EVERYONE OF THE POLICIES THAT WERE PUT IN PLACE TO IDENTIFY AND ADDRESS POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST.

THE BOARD'S POLICY WHEN DETERMINING SALARIES IS TO CONSIDER COMPARABLE DATA FROM LIKE ORGANIZATIONS AND PRIOR APPROVAL BY INDEPENDENT BOARD MEMBERS IS REQUIRED. THIS PROCESS WAS LAST COMPLETED IN 2024 FOR THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR. IN 2024, THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR RECEIVED A RAISE TO $90,000 PER YEAR AS PART OF THE ANNUAL BOARD-APPROVED BUDGET.

IT IS THE BOARD'S POLICY WHEN DETERMINING EMPLOYEE COMPENSATION TO REVIEW COMPENSATION FOR COMPARABLE POSITIONS IN SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS. SALARY CHANGES MUST BE BOARD APPROVED.

THE ORGANIZATION'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST. THE ORGANIZATION'S FORM 990 IS AVAILABLE ON ITS WEBSITE AND ON GUIDESTAR.

PRIOR PERIOD ADJUSTMENT = $16812

mainOrgs.name: Project Big Wood A Nonprofit Corporation
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Improve environmental health and ecology of the Big Wood River Basin by protecting and enhancing its watershed.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Water quality monitoring, community events, and volunteer engagement.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on watershed protection and water quality in river basin.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission to improve the Big Wood River Basin.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Engages youth volunteers and collaborates on global water quality data.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: PROJECT BIG WOOD, an emerging nonprofit active for nearly four years, focuses on river health in Idaho with robust volunteer engagement and effective water monitoring programs, although facing some cash flow limitations.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Upcoming volunteer days and community events to support river restoration.
mainOrgs.websiteConsistencySignal: Website emphasizes community engagement and advocacy, while 990 highlights environmental health specifically.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (59%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: PROJECT BIG WOOD IS ORGANIZED TO IMPROVE THE ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH AND ECOLOGY OF THE BIG WOOD RIVER BASIN IN CENTRAL IDAHO BY PROTECTING, ENHANCING, SUSTAINING AND RECONNECTING THE BIG WOOD RIVER, ITS TRIBUTARIES AND ITS WATERSHED.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
PROJECT BIG WOOD IS ORGANIZED TO IMPROVE THE ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH AND ECOLOGY OF THE BIG WOOD RIVER BASIN IN CENTRAL IDAHO BY PROTECTING, ENHANCING, SUSTAINING AND RECONNECTING THE BIG WOOD RIVER, ITS TRIBUTARIES AND ITS WATERSHED.

VOLUNTEERS PERFORM TASKS SUCH AS WATER SAMPLING & STAFFING COMMUNITY EVENTS. WE GENERALLY HAVE 32 KIDS PER MONTH MINIMUM VOLUNTEERING, AND UP TO 75 THROUGHOUT THE YEAR.

DATA COLLECTION FOR WATER QUALITY AND RIVER HEALTH WE LAUNCHED OUR WATER MONITORING PROGRAM IN 2023. IT CONSISTS OF TWO PARTS: WATER QUALITY AND QUANTITY. PROJECT BIG WOOD BEGAN OUR WATER QUALITY SAMPLING PROGRAM. IT INVOLVES TESTING 13 SITES IN COLLABORATION WITH SCIENCE ON THE FLY, A NON PROFIT GATHERING WORLD WIDE WATER QUALITY DATA IN ORDER TO GAIN A DEEPER UNDERSTANDING OF ISSUES COMMUNITIES ARE FACING ACROSS THE WORLD. WE TEST ONCE A MONTH AND ANALYZE OUR SAMPLES FOR DISSOLVED ORGANIC CARBON, NITRATES, PHOSPHATES, AND SODIUM CHLORIDE. OUR BELIEF IS BY GATHERING MORE DATA WE WILL BE ABLE TO CREATE A BASELINE OF DATA ON THE BIG WOOD RIVER WATERSHED THAT HELPS INFORM FUTURE RESTORATION EFFORTS AND MANAGEMENT DECISIONS VALLEY WIDE. MOVING ONTO WATER QUANTITY-WE PURCHASED 9 BARO LOGGERS TO INSTALL IN SOUTH VALLEY CANALS TO BETTER UNDERSTAND WATER LOSS THROUGH THE SYSTEM AND IMPROVE WATER DELIVERY TO USERS. THESE LOGGERS WILL BE STRATEGICALLY INSTALLED IN THE BYPASS CANAL SYSTEM, A MEASURE AIMED AT ENHANCING OUR UNDERSTANDING OF WATER LOSS WITHIN THE SYSTEM, BOTH IN THE CANAL AND THE RIVER CHANNEL. THIS PROJECT IS IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE IRRIGATORS ON THE BYPASS CANAL SYSTEM IN ORDER TO BETTER UNDERSTAND WATER LOSS THROUGH THE SYSTEM, IMPROVE WATER DELIVERY TO WATER RIGHT USERS, AND PROVIDE POSSIBLE CONNECTIVITY TO MAGIC AND IMPROVED FISH PASSAGE. WE ANTICIPATE COMPLETION IN FALL OF 2024. PROJECT BIG WOOD PURCHASED 9 BARO LOGGERS. THESE LOGGERS WILL BE STRATEGICALLY INSTALLED IN THE BYPASS CANAL SYSTEM, A MEASURE AIMED AT ENHANCING OUR UNDERSTANDING OF WATER LOSS WITHIN THE SYSTEM, BOTH IN THE CANAL AND THE RIVER CHANNEL. COLLABORATING CLOSELY WITH IRRIGATORS ON THE BYPASS CANAL SYSTEM, PROJECT BIG WOOD SEEKS TO REFINE INSIGHTS INTO WATER LOSS DYNAMICS, ENHANCE WATER DELIVERY TO WATER RIGHT USERS, AND POTENTIALLY REESTABLISH CONNECTIVITY TO MAGIC, THUS FACILITATING IMPROVED FISH PASSAGE. ALTHOUGH THE PROJECT IS CURRENTLY UNDERWAY, WE ANTICIPATE AN INITIAL REPORT OF FINDINGS IN THE FALL OF 2024. SIMULTANEOUSLY, PROJECT BIG WOOD HAS UNDERTAKEN A ROBUST WATER QUALITY SAMPLING INITIATIVE. THIS ENDEAVOR INVOLVES THE METICULOUS TESTING OF 13 SITES IN COLLABORATION WITH SCIENCE ON THE FLY, A NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO GATHERING GLOBAL WATER QUALITY DATA. THE OBJECTIVE IS TO GARNER A COMPREHENSIVE UNDERSTANDING OF THE CHALLENGES FACED BY WATERWAYS AND THE ASSOCIATED COMMUNITIES WORLDWIDE. OUR MONTHLY TESTING PROTOCOLS INVOLVE THE ANALYSIS OF WATER SAMPLES FOR DISSOLVED ORGANIC CARBON, NITRATES, PHOSPHATES, AND SODIUM CHLORIDE. OUR UNDERLYING PHILOSOPHY IS ROOTED IN THE BELIEF THAT THROUGH THE ACCUMULATION OF EXTENSIVE DATA, WE CAN ESTABLISH A ROBUST BASELINE FOR THE BIG WOOD RIVER WATERSHED. THIS FOUNDATIONAL DATA, IN TURN, WILL INFORM FUTURE RESTORATION EFFORTS AND GUIDE CRUCIAL MANAGEMENT DECISIONS. THOUGH THE PROGRAM IS ONGOING, WE REMAIN COMMITTED TO THE PURSUIT OF KNOWLEDGE THAT EMPOWERS EFFECTIVE ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP. COMMUNITY EDUCATION AND ADVOCACY: PROJECT BIG WOOD IS COLLABORATING WITH THE COUNTY AND OTHER NON-GOVERNMENT ORGANIZATIONS (NGOS) IN OUR COMMUNITY TO EXPAND AND DEVELOP A NEW ATLAS BASED ON THE 2023 LIDAR FLIGHT DATA. PROJECT BIG WOOD IS DEDICATED TO BOTH FINANCING AND CRAFTING A DYNAMIC DOCUMENT THAT NOT ONLY BENEFITS OUR COMMUNITY BUT ALSO ADVANCES OUR UNDERSTANDING OF THE BIG WOOD RIVER WATERSHED. ADDITIONALLY, PROJECT BIG WOOD HAS INITIATED THE PRODUCTION OF VISUAL WATER REPORTS. OFFERING A QUARTERLY DEPICTION OF CURRENT CONDITIONS ON THE BIG WOOD RIVER IN REAL-TIME. THIS INITIATIVE AIMS TO PROVIDE A VISUAL SNAPSHOT OF THE RIVER THROUGHOUT THE YEAR, ENABLING US TO CREATE A GRAPHICAL REPRESENTATION OF CHANGES OVER TIME. WATERSHED RESTORATION: IN EARLY 2023, WE ENGAGED ECOSYSTEM SCIENCES TO REASSESS THE DESIGN OF THE NORTHWOODS PROJECT. WITH THE RECENT COMPLETION OF A FINALIZED THREE-PHASE DESIGN, WE ARE POISED TO TRANSITION INTO PROJECT DEVELOPMENT AND PERMITTING. THE OVERARCHING GOAL OF THIS INITIATIVE IS TO RESTORE THE NORTHWOODS REACH OF THE BIG WOOD RIVER AND IMPROVE NATURAL RIVER FUNCTIONS. ACTIVATING LOCAL PRIDE: PROJECT BIG WOOD INITIATED THE CULTIVATION OF LOCAL PRIDE BY INVOLVING OUR FLY SHOPS, GUIDES, AND COMMUNITY IN CITIZEN SCIENCES. OUTFITTERS, GUIDES, ANGLERS AND PASSIONATE RIVER USERS ARE CRUCIAL STAKEHOLDERS IN OUR WATERSHED, SERVE AS SIGNIFICANT ALLIES AND POSSESS VALUABLE INSIGHTS INTO THE STATUS OF OUR FISHERIES. BY LEANING ON THEIR EXPERIENCES, WE AIM TO TAP INTO A WEALTH OF KNOWLEDGE THAT CAN CONTRIBUTE TO THE MISSION OF PROJECT BIG WOOD AND PROVIDE A DEEPER UNDERSTANDING OF CHANGES ON THE BIG WOOD RIVER. IN SPRING OF 2023, WE PARTNERED WITH SAWTOOTH BREWERY TO CRAFT A FLAGSHIP LAGER THAT EMBODIES THE ESSENCE OF PROJECT BIG WOOD AND PROMOTES AWARENESS OF THE INTERCONNECTEDNESS OF OUR WATER SYSTEMS. THIS UNIQUE BREW SERVES AS A TANGIBLE REPRESENTATION OF THE LINK BETWEEN THE BIG WOOD RIVER AND EACH INDIVIDUAL CAN, SYMBOLIZING THE INTEGRAL ROLE WATER PLAYS IN OUR COMMUNITY. PROJECT BIG WOOD PARTICIPATED IN COMMUNITY EVENTS TO RAISE AWARENESS ABOUT THE CHALLENGES OUR WATERSHED IS FACING AND TO FOSTER COLLABORATIVE EFFORTS FOR CHANGE WITHIN OUR COMMUNITY. WE WERE AND WILL BE ACTIVELY PRESENT AT IF4, HAILEY ROCKS, AND RIVER FEST, FURTHERING OUR COMMITMENT TO ENGAGING WITH THE COMMUNITY AND ADDRESSING THE ISSUES AFFECTING OUR WATERSHED. PROJECT BIG WOOD PARTNERED WITH GUIDES AND COMMUNITY MEMBERS TO PRODUCE A FILM TO SHOW AT COMMUNITY EVENTS ABOUT THE BIG WOOD RIVER AND ITS CHALLENGES.

BOARD MEMBERS REVIEW AN ELECTRONIC COPY OF THE FORM 990 PRIOR TO APPROVAL AND E-FILING.

## Who speaks

 for the river?

We do.

### Our mission is to unite our community for a better Big Wood River through data, education, advocacy, and earthworks. ​

### Here’s what that work looks like in action

![Image 1](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/2481d5_44ce4d727f9546f4b427fcba8017af8b~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_230,h_307,q_90,enc_avif,quality_auto/2481d5_44ce4d727f9546f4b427fcba8017af8b~mv2.jpg)

Data

Tracking water temps, river flow, and fish health—because good data leads to better decisions.

## What's happening​

COME FOR THE CAUSE,

 STAY FOR THE COMMUNITY

From volunteer days to happy hours, here's what's coming up on our calendar.

## We can't do it alone

That’s why we work shoulder-to-shoulder with local partners and the community to prioritize projects, drive action, and restore the river we all depend on.

Form 990 is presented to the Executive Director and Board of Directors with an opportunity to comment before the Form 990 is filed.

In the event that such director has a conflict of interest which might in any way limit such director's fair and impartial participation in board deliberations or decisions, such director shall inform the board as to the circumstances of such conflict. No director shall cast a vote, nor take part in the final deliberation in any matter in which he or she, any member of his or her immediate family, or any organization to which such director has allegiance or a personal interest that may be seen as competing with or limiting the interests of the corporation. Any director who believes he or she may have such a conflict of interest shall so notify the board prior to deliberation, on the matter in question, and the board shall make the final determination as to whether any such director has a conflict of interest in such matter. The minutes of the board meeting shall generally reflect the disclosure of any conflict of interest and recusal of the interested director.

Compensation for the executive director is determined by the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors. Compensation and benefit information was directly gathered from similarly-sized peer organizations, in the same geographic location. The information is reviewed by the executive committee and a compensation package is developed and approved by the Board of Directors.

The organization makes it governing documents, conflict of interest policy and financial statements available to the public upon request addressed to the organization's executive director.

## The Madison

## Protecting the River That Inspires the World

[**_The Madison_**](https://www.madisonriverfoundation.org/the-madison-river-montana)**series is introducing millions of people to the beauty of Montana’s Madison Valley and sparking new interest in Madison River Montana and the real river behind the story. The Madison River is a massive lifeline that plays a crucial role in the Greater Yellowstone ecosystem and is one of the three rivers that form the mighty Missouri, the longest river in North America. mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE GREATER EDWARDS AQUIFER ALLIANCE (GEAA) HAS ORIGINATED A COMPREHENSIVE PROGRAM OF SCIENCE, ADVOCACY, AND PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT AIMED AT PROTECTING THE QUALITY AND QUANTITY OF SPRING FLOWS FROM THE EDWARDS AND TRINITY AQUIFERS. WE ARE IN NEED OF SUPPORT TO FUND SCIENCE, ADVOCACY AND PUBLIC PARTICIPATION PROJECTS BY GEAA AND OUR MEMBER GROUPS IN KEY POLICY MAKING PROCESSES THAT ARE CURRENTLY UNDERWAY.

SAN ANTONIO SOIL CARBON PROJECT - GEAA WAS AWARDED A GRANT FROM THE CITY OF SAN ANTONIO TO ASSIST CITY AND FEDERAL AUTHORITIES IN A STUDY OF THE IMPACT OF GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE ON SOIL CARBON SEQUESTRATION AND WATER QUALITY IN THIRTY-TWO SAN ANTONIO PARKS. THE PROJECT WILL SERVE AS A PROOF-OF-CONCEPT IN ORDER TO DEMONSTRATE THAT ENHANCED CARBON SEQUESTRATION AND GROUNDWATER INFILTRATION CAN BE ACHIEVED IN URBAN AREAS BY EMPLOYING BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES. THIS IS THE FIRST PROJECT OF THIS TYPE TO RESEARCH HOW SIGNIFICANT GAINS IN CARBON SEQUESTRATION MIGHT BE ACHIEVED BY ENHANCING SOIL ORGANIC MATTER AND APPROPRIATE PLANT MANAGEMENT IN URBAN OPEN SPACES. IF SUCCESSFUL, THIS PROJECT WILL HELP SAN ANTONIO TO MEET ITS CLIMATE ACTION GOALS. LEGISLATIVE PROGRAM - WE PROVIDE EXPERT TESTIMONY AND RESEARCHES ISSUES AS NEEDED BY THE MEMBERS OF THE HOUSE AND SENATE NATURAL RESOURCE COMMITTEES AND OUR REGIONAL DELEGATION TO INFORM DECISIONS MADE IN THE STATE LEGISLATURE PERTINENT TO THE MANAGEMENT OF OUR WATER RESOURCES. WE VISIT WITH LEGISLATORS AND RESEARCH IMPACTS OF PROPOSED LEGISLATION. WE ALSO ASSEMBLE AND SECURE CONSENSUS ON AN AGENDA FOR EACH LEGISLATIVE SESSION AND PROVIDE TESTIMONY ACCORDINGLY. E-MAIL ALERTS THAT EDUCATE THE PUBLIC ABOUT ACTIONS TO SUPPORT OR OPPOSE BILLS OF PARTICULAR IMPORTANCE ENGAGE HUNDREDS OF INDIVIDUALS IN GEAA'S ADVOCACY EFFORTS. TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES OUR MEMBER GROUPS, GOVERNMENTAL AGENCIES AND POLICYMAKERS, AND THE PUBLIC AT LARGE WITH TECHNICAL EXPERTISE AND ACCESS TO INFORMATION THAT ADDRESSES ISSUES OF REGIONAL AND LOCAL CONCERN AS RELATED TO THE PRESERVATION OF THE QUALITY AND QUANTITY OF WATER EDWARDS AQUIFER, ITS CONTRIBUTING ATERSHEDS, AND ITS INDIGENOUS PLANT AND ANIMAL SPECIES. CITIZENS, NEWS MEDIA, AND, INCREASINGLY, ELECTED OFFICIALS AND PUBLIC AGENCIES RELY ON GEAA STAFF TO PROVIDE OR DIRECT THEM TO TECHNICAL EXPERTISE REGARDING THE MANAGEMENT AND PROTECTION OF THE EDWARDS AND TRINITY KARST AQUIFER FORMATIONS. PUBLIC COMMENT AND AD HOC ISSUES - GEAA RECEIVES REQUESTS FOR TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ON A REGULAR BASIS ON A WIDE VARIETY OF ISSUES FROM INDIVIDUAL LANDOWNERS, REGIONAL MUNICIPALITIES, AND STATE AND FEDERAL GOVERNMENTAL AGENCIES SOLICITING PUBLIC COMMENT ON PROGRAMS AND POLICIES RELATING TO OUR GROUND AND SURFACE WATER SYSTEMS. THE EXPERTISE OF OUR TECHNICAL TAFF IS CRITICAL TO EXPANDING OUR CAPACITY TO PROMOTE ADEQUATE PROTECTION OF OUR WATER RESOURCES AND IS ESSENTIAL TO ACCOMPLISHING OUR GOALS. WE RELY UPON THE LARGESSE OF SEVERAL EXPERTS WHO HAVE VOLUNTEERED THEIR TIME AND THE BENEFIT OF THEIR EXPERTISE AS NEEDED ON SPECIFIC PROJECTS. BECAUSE OF THE RECOGNIZED EXCELLENCE OF OUR WORK, GEAA IS REGULARLY CALLED UPON TO RESEARCH SPECIFIC ISSUES AND PROJECTS, TO COMMENT ON PROPOSED PROJECTS, RULES AND PROGRAMS, AND TO GENERATE POLICY AND REGULATORY RECOMMENDATIONS AND REMEDIES. GEAA PROVIDES PRACTICAL GUIDANCE TO THE DEVELOPMENT AND CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRIES, ENHANCING OUR ABILITY TO FURTHER OUR GOAL OF AQUIFER ROTECTION BY FURNISHING EXPERTISE ON PROVEN METHODS AND PRACTICES TO DEVELOPERS REFERRED TO GEAA BY GOVERNMENT AGENCIES TO ADVISE ON SPECIFIC PROJECTS. ADDITIONALLY, GEAA WILL CONTINUE TO WORK WITH MUNICIPALITIES WITHIN THE EDWARDS REGION TO PROMOTE THE ADOPTION OF SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT TECHNIQUES INTO MUNICIPAL BUILDING CODES AND STATE- RECOMMENDED BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES. REGIONAL FLOOD PLANNING AND GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE - GEAA STAFF MEMBERS ARE SERVING ON THE TEXAS WATER DEVELOPMENT BOARD'S REGIONAL FLOOD PLANNING GROUPS FOR THE SAN ANTONIO AND GUADALUPE RIVER BASINS. THROUGH THIS THREE-YEAR FLOOD PLANNING PROCESS, WE ARE PROMOTING LAND PRESERVATION AND THE USE OF GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE TO MITIGATE FLOODING WHILE ADVISING LOCAL COMMUNITIES TO INCORPORATE GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNIQUES INTO THEIR ORDINANCES AND POLICIES. UNDER YOUR FEET CAMPAIGN - MUCH OF THE RECENT GROWTH IN OUR REGION HAS TAKEN PLACE OVER THE RECHARGE AND CONTRIBUTING ZONES OF THE EDWARDS AQUIFER, RESULTING IN INCREASED CONTAMINATION OF OUR WATER SUPPLIES AND SPRING FLOWS. A SUITE OF PRESENTATIONS, ON-LINE RESOURCES, SOCIAL MEDIA, AND PRINT MATERIALS WILL TEACH FOLKS WHO LIVE ON THE RECHARGE ZONE ABOUT THE GEOLOGY OF THE EDWARDS AQUIFER, HOW TO LIVE IN THIS EXTREMELY INVIRONMENTALLY SENSITIVE AREA, AND ABOUT ACTIONS THEY CAN TAKE TO PROTECT OUR AQUIFER, INCLUDING HOW TO HANDLE AND STORE HAZARDOUS MATERIALS, WHAT COMMON HOUSEHOLD PRACTICES POSE A THREAT TO THE INTEGRITY OF SEWAGE SYSTEMS, AND HOW TO PLANT AND MAINTAIN LANDSCAPING IN THIS UNIQUE REGION. GEAA WILL ASK PARTICIPANTS TO TAKE A PLEDGE TO DO THEIR PART TO REDUCE HARMFUL STORMWATER RUNOFF ON THEIR PROPERTIES. RESEARCH ON THE IMPACTS OF SURFACE AND SUBSURFACE POLLUTION TO THE EDWARDS AQUIFER - THE AGENCIES TASKED WITH OVERSIGHT OF WATER QUALITY OF THE EDWARD AQUIFER, MOST NOTABLY THE TCEQ AND THE EDWARDS AQUIFER AUTHORITY (EAA) RELY ON THE USE OF STORMWATER MANAGEMENT AS THE CHIEF STRATEGY FOR PROTECTING WATER QUALITY OF THE EDWARDS AQUIFER. MEASURES TO ADDRESS THE QUALITY OF STORMWATER INFILTRATING THE EDWARDS, HOWEVER, PROVIDE NO PROTECTION FROM THE INTRODUCTION OF SUBSURFACE POLLUTION OF THE AQUIFER FROM HUMAN SEWAGE, WHICH BECOMES PARTICULARLY PROBLEMATIC WITH INCREASED HIGH DENSITY RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT OF THE EDWARDS AQUIFER RECHARGE AND CONTRIBUTING ZONES. THE GEAA TECHNICAL TEAM, WITH THE HELP OF STUDENT INTERNS FROM TRINITY UNIVERSITY AND TEXAS STATE UNIVERSITY, IS CURRENTLY RESEARCHING THE IMPACT OF ON-SITE SEWAGE FACILITIES (SEPTIC TANKS) VERSUS CENTRALIZED SEWAGE SYSTEMS ON THE EDWARDS AQUIFER RECHARGE ZONE. WE HAVE PRODUCED A MAP INDICATING THE LOCATIONS OF ALL WASTEWATER TREATMENT EFFLUENT DISCHARGES, AS WELL AS LAND APPLICATION PERMITS, WITHIN OUR SERVICE AREA. THE FINDINGS AID IN GEAA'S EDUCATION, OUTREACH, AND POLICY EFFORTS. WE ARE ALSO CONDUCTING RESEARCH ON THE EFFECTIVENESS OF ENGINEERED STORMWATER REDUCTION FACILITIES ON THE RECHARGE ZONE IN BEXAR COUNTY. GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE MODEL PROJECTS - IN 2022 GEAA COMPLETED OUR MODEL LOW IMPACT DEVELOPMENT RETROFIT PROJECT AT LORENCE CREEK. FUNDED BY THE CITY OF SAN ANTONIO'S EDWARDS AQUIFER PROTECTION PROGRAM, THE RESULTS OF STORMWATER MONITORING HAS DETERMINED THAT GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNIQUES EMPLOYED AT THIS SITE WERE SUCCESSFUL IN MITIGATING NON-POINT SOURCE POLLUTION ON THE EDWARDS AQUIFER RECHARGE ZONE. THIS YEAR, GEAA IS COLLABORATING WITH THE CITY OF CASTROVILLE AND FRIENDS OF CASTROVILLE PARK TO DESIGN AND BUILD A TREATMENT TRAIN OF LID FEATURES AND ARTIFICIAL WETLANDS TO IMPROVE THE QUALITY OF EFFLUENT RELEASED FROM CASTROVILLE'S WASTE WATER TREATMENT PLANT BEFORE IT IS DISCHARGED INTO THE MEDINA RIVER. GEAA STAFF IS ASSISTING CASTROVILLE IN SECURING FUNDS FOR THE PROJECT FROM STATE AND PRIVATE SOURCES. GEAA EDUCATION PROGRAM - GEAA STAFF PROVIDES INFORMATION TO TEACHERS OF THE LIFE SCIENCES THAT ENABLE THEM TO TEACH HUNDREDS OF STUDENTS EACH YEAR ABOUT THE MYSTERIES OF OUR KARST GROUNDWATER SYSTEM - THE EDWARDS AQUIFER, USING OUR KARST AQUIFER MODEL AND HANDS ON LAB EXERCISES AND TRIPS TO GOVERNMENT CANYON STATE NATURAL AREA. GEAA ALSO TRAINS TEACHERS TO USE OUR FIVE-PART FILM, INSIDE THE EDWARDS AQUIFER, WHICH INCORPORATES LAB EXERCISES, VOCABULARY AND RESOURCE MATERIALS CONSONANT WITH THE TEXAS ASSESSMENT OF KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS REQUIREMENTS. GEAA COLLABORATIONS AND CONTRIBUTES EXPERTISE AND STAFF TIME TO THE CAMP BULLIS SENTINEL LANDSCAPE PROJECT, THE JOINT BASE SAN ANTONIO COMPREHENSIVE USE PLAN, TEXAS HILL COUNTRY CONSERVATION NETWORK, THE NO DUMPING SEWAGE CAMPAIGN, SAN ANTONIO RIVER AUTHORITY'S ENVIRONMENTAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE, THE TEXAS WATER CONSERVATION ACTION NETWORK, TEXAN BY NATURE, THE ALLIANCE FOR CLEAN TEXAS, AND OUR FIFTY-NINE MEMBER ORGANIZATIONS AND WORKS WITH A STATE-WIDE GREEN INFRASTRUCTURE ADVISORY TEAM TO COORDINATE EFFORTS FOR SUSTAINABLE LAND USE.

DRAFT VERSION OF FORM 990 PRESENTED TO BOARD MEMBERS BEFORE RETURN IS ACCEPTED AND FILED.

THE OFFICERS AND BOARD MEMBERS ANNUALLY SIGN A "CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCL OSURE FORM" DISCLOSING ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST OR STATING THAT NO CONFLI CTS EXIST.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS APPROVES THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S SALARY THROUGH PE RFORMANCE ASSESSMENTS AND APPROVAL OF THE ANNUAL BUDGET.

THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEME NTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

mainOrgs.name: Powder Basin Watershed Council
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Promote, retain, restore, and enhance watersheds in Northeast Oregon.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Watershed assessment, planning, monitoring, outreach, and education.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focus on watershed restoration and enhancement in Northeast Oregon.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Combines assessment, education, and stakeholder engagement related to watersheds.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Council structure with appointed governmental representation.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With 17.1 years in operation and $793K revenue, the Powder Basin Watershed Council has a mature governance structure and a strong focus on watershed health, engaging community stakeholders effectively.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: High school interns constructed a beaver dam analogue on Camp Creek during summer 2023.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Days cash below tier threshold (78 < 90); Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (99%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: To promote, retain, restore and enhance watersheds of the Powder Basin in Northeast Oregon.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Watershed Assessment, Planning and Monitoring
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Watershed Outreach and Education
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 4: Support & Misc OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 5: Stakeholder Engagement

The organization is a council of 14 appointed councilors. Appointments are made by various governmental agencies such as Baker County, US Forest Service, etc. There are 7 voting and 7 non-voting councilors. The council elects 5 councilors to act as a board of directors in charge of operational governance. The board of directors is accountable to the council.

The Council elects board of directors and approves charter amendments. All other governance decisions are made at the board of directors level.

No committees are empowered to act on behalf of the Board of Directors or the general Council.

The Board of Directors reviews a draft of the Form 990 prior to filing. The Board approves and changes or revisions.

The Board of Directors meeting agenda begins with an item "declaration of conflict of interest". Members review the agenda for any items in which a conflict of interest may arise or has previously been declared. Any conflicts are disclosed and the appropriate action is taken. Actions may include a member being excluded from discussion and/or voting on any items where a possible conflict of interest exists.

The Board of Directors performs an annual evaluation of the Executive Director (ED) and sets compensation amounts.

The Executive Director evaluates other employees and recommends compensation for Board approval. Board Members are not compensated for their service.

At the December meeting of the Watershed Council, which is an open, public meeting, an announcement is made that governing documents, organization policies and procedures, Form 990, and other fiscal records are available at the office for public review. This announcement is recorded in the minutes and pulished on the Council website.

mainOrgs.name: California Bountiful Foundation
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Advances public awareness and education of California agriculture.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Educating on pesticide policy and grant preparation for farm tours.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focus is primarily on agriculture, not directly on water conservation.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Programs are related to agriculture and education rather than focused on water conservation.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With 27 years of experience, the foundation has developed a mature approach to agricultural education, evidenced by a high current ratio. However, its primary focus on agriculture limits alignment with the foundation's water conservation goals.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (96%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TO ADVANCE PUBLIC AWARENESS, EDUCATION, AND UNDERSTANDING OF CALIFORNIA AGRICULTURE.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: CALIFORNIANS FOR SMART PESTICIDE POLICY (CSPP) - A COALITION MADE UP OF ALLIED AGRICULTURAL ASSOCIATIONS FOCUSED ON EDUCATING DECISIONMAKERS ON THE BENEFITS OF MODERN AGRICULTURAL TOOLS. ITS MISSION IS TO INFORM PESTICIDE POLICIES AND ACTIONS IN CALIFORNIA BY USING THE BEST AVAILABLE SCIENCE AND DATA WHILE ADHERING TO STATE REGULATIONS AND STATUTES.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: CALIFORNIA CATTLE COUNCIL GRANT - PREPARATION FOR GRANT WORK TO IMPLEMENT FARM TOURS TO INFORM AND EDUCATE POLICYMAKERS, DECISION LEADERS, AND THEIR STAFF ABOUT THE BENEFITS OF THIS AGRICULTURAL SECTOR, INCLUDING THE SCIENCE OF ANIMAL BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, AND WELFARE.

mainOrgs.name: Middle Fork Willamette Watershedcouncil
mainOrgs.missionSummary: We work with communities for a healthy Middle Fork Willamette Watershed through habitat restoration and community engagement.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Habitat restoration and community engagement for watershed health.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on watershed health, directly addressing water quality and habitat restoration.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Involves two related programs targeting habitat restoration and community involvement.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With nearly 18 years of experience, the Middle Fork Willamette Watershed Council maintains solid financial metrics, a mature governance structure, and focused programs on watershed health.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Recent projects include habitat restoration in Elijah Bristow State Park and Thurston Hills Oak Restoration.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Days cash below tier threshold (48 < 90); Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (95%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (74%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: WE WORK WITH COMMUNITIES FOR A HEALTHY MIDDLE FORK WILLAMETTE WATERSHED THROUGH HABITAT RESTORATION, YOUTH EDUCATION, AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: HABITAT RESTORATION: THE GOAL OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO WORK WITH PARTNERS, RESEARCHERS AND STAKEHOLDERS TO PRIORITIZE, PLAN, IMPLEMENT AND MONITOR HIGH PRIORITY RESTORATION AND ENHANCEMENT PROJECTS THAT TARGET LISTED SPECIES, WATER QUALITY AND RARE HABITATS IN THE MIDDLE FORK WATERSHED.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT: THE GOAL OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO ENGAGE THOSE WHO LIVE, WORK AND RECREATE IN THE MIDDLE FORK WILLAMETTE WATERSHED IN THE PROTECTION, RESTORATION AND STEWARDSHIP OF ITS NATURAL RESOURCES.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
OTHER PROGRAMS

THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS PRIOR TO SUBMISSION.

ANNUALLY INTERESTED PERSONS ARE GIVEN A COPY OF THE MFWWC'S CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND ASKED TO COMPLETE AND RETURN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENT.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR IS HIRED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AND SUBJECT TO AN ANNUAL REVIEW PROCESS. THE BOARD'S ANNUAL EVALUATION INCLUDES A PERFORMANCE EVALUATION AND DETERMINATION OF SALARY FOR THE UPCOMING YEAR, BASED ON PERFORMANCE AND COMPARABLE COMPENSATION.

GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST

mainOrgs.websiteTextExcerpt:
Title: River Science

URL Source: https://www.river.science/

Markdown Content:
## **Our Programs and Focus Areas**

[![Image 1](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/56b0a927f699bb770fa83679/70f94305-5eb2-42e5-815b-2f30ca475852/River+Corps+Color.png)](https://www.river.science/rivercorps)

The River Corps is an AmeriCorps program ran by River Science that places Members at organizations and agencies throughout Colorado to focus on improving the efficiency, efficacy, and scale of river data and management to improved recreation, water resources, and overall resiliency. To thrive in these technical positions, Members will receive comprehensive training in data collection, communication, and construction techniques.

[![Image 2](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/56b0a927f699bb770fa83679/98dc55ad-6f7c-4a4f-ab5c-cd3dac779b71/River-Watch-Logo-2020-Black.png)](https://www.river.science/river-watch)

Since 1990, River Watch has been collecting water quality data around the State of Colorado to provide the data necessary to monitor and protect our rivers. Our mission is to provide rigorous training to volunteer stewards who then monitor water quality and other indicators of watershed health. These high-quality data help educate communities and inform decision-makers about the condition of Colorado’s waters. Today, River Watch is the nation’s largest and longest running volunteer water quality collection program.

[![Image 3](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/cont

Title: Fighting to Protect Our Flint River

URL Source: https://www.flintriverkeeper.org/

Published Time: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 03:37:03 GMT

Markdown Content:
Court filing: Robert Sullins, Karol Sullins, and Flint Riverkeeper, Inc. vs. City of Griffin, Georgia

## Flint Riverkeeper® (FRk) was established in 2008

to address the various issues that threaten the Flint River and its tributaries. We work to restore and protect the quality and flow of this immensely valuable resource. The Flint River and associated aquifers provide water for drinking, swimming, fishing, commercial, industrial and agricultural pursuits for more than one-million people.

FRk works within the entire Flint watershed, comprising much of West and South Georgia. Beginning as springs in East Point, the river flows under Atlanta’s Hartsfield-Jackson airport, mingles with major tributaries in Coweta and Fayette counties and then flows over 340 miles to the Georgia and Florida State borders. There it pairs with the Chattahoochee River at Lake Seminole to form the Apalachicola River. The drainage basin is over 8,000 square miles and traverses the Piedmont region’s granite shoals until it reaches the Fall Line, where it gradually gives way to sandy banks and ancient limestone caverns.

![Image 1: slider-home-06](https://www.flintriverkeeper.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/12/slider-home-06.jpg)

mainOrgs.name: Tetiaroa Society
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Dedicated to education, conservation, and research for sustainable management of tropical island ecosystems.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Tetiaroa Education Program and Blue Climate Initiative aimed at sustainability and climate solutions.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focuses on ecosystems and sustainability, with water security in context of tropical islands.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Two main related programs addressing education and ocean-related solutions.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: With over 14 years of experience and a solid financial position, Tetiaroa Society has established itself in conservation education and climate action initiatives, focusing on sustainable practices for island ecosystems.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: In 2025, Tetiaroa Society advanced research and restoration efforts while sharing Polynesian knowledge, resulting in a significant impact report.
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TETIAROA SOCIETY IS A CHARITABLE, NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO EDUCATION, CONSERVATION, AND RESEARCH ACTIVITIES RELATED TO TROPICAL ISLAND SOCIO-ECOSYSTEMS, PARTICULARLY THAT OF TETIAROA ATOLL, FRENCH POLYNESIA. THE GOALS OF THE ORGANIZATION ARE: -TO PROMOTE SUSTAINABILITY THROUGH AN INTERDISCIPLINARY STUDY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY, THE HUMANITIES, AND THE ARTS AND CREATIVE SCIENCES; -TO ENCOURAGE THE PRESERVATION OF POLYNESIAN ECOSYSTEMS, BIODIVERSITY, AND CULTURE; -TO DEVELOP AND TEST NEW TECHNOLOGIES AND APPROACHES - PARTICULARLY IN RELATION TO RENEWABLE ENERGY, FOOD, WATER SECURITY, AND THE CONTROL AND IMPACT OF INVASIVE SPECIES - THAT ARE APPROPRIATE FOR TROPICAL ISLANDS IN AN ERA OF RAPID GLOBAL CHANGE; AND -TO HELP ISLAND COMMUNITIES ACIEVE SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT. TETIAROA SOCIETY IS COMMITTED TO THE PRESERVATION AND RESTORATION OF TETIAROA ISLAND THROUGH A UNIQUE CONSERVATION PLAN THAT SUPPORTS SCIENTIFIC INNOVATION TO CREATE SUSTAINABLE INTERDEPENDENCE.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: THE TETIAROA EDUCATION PROGRAM OFFERS YOUNG PEOPLE A UNIQUE OPPORTUNITY TO EXPLORE SCIENCE AND TRADITIONAL KNOWLEDGE ON A REMARKABLE ISLAND. LOCAL STUDENTS (AGES 8 TO 18) LEARN ABOUT THEIR CULTURAL AND NATURAL HERITAGE AND TAKE HOME VALUABLE LESSONS IN SUSTAINABILITY. INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS ARE INTRODUCED TO A TROPICAL ISLAND THAT SERVES AS A GLOBAL MODEL FOR CONSERVATION AND SUSTAINABLE PRACTICES. THE PROGRAM AIMS TO INSPIRE AND ENRICH ALL WHO PARTICIPATE. IN 2024, WE WORKED WITH:- THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY ISP COURSE- CHADWICK HIGH SCHOOL- LAKESIDE HIGH SCHOOL- MULTIPLE FRENCH POLYNESIAN SCHOOL GROUPS
mainOrgs.program3Desc: THE BLUE CLIMATE INITIATIVE ("BCI") IS TETIAROA SOCIETY'S GLOBAL EFFORT TO COMBAT CLIMATE CHANGE WITH OCEANRELATED SOLUTIONS. THIS INITIATIVE BRINGS TOGETHER SCIENTISTS, COMMUNITY LEADERS, ENTREPRENEURS, BUSINESS LEADERS AND INFLUENCERS TO ACCELERATE OCEAN RELATED SOLUTIONS TO THE GREATEST ENVIRONMENTAL CRISIS HUMANKIND HAS CONFRONTED: CLIMATE CHANGE. IN 2024, THE BLUE CLIMATE INITIATIVE CONTINUED WORK IN THE CAMPAIGN AGAINST DEEP SEA MINING ON TWO FRONTS: ECONOMICS AND CULTURE. THE BCI CO-SPONSORED A SIDE EVENT AT THE MARCH INTERNATIONAL SEABED AUTHORITY (ISA) MEETING. THE PANEL MADE THE CASE THAT DSM IS ECONOMICALLY UNFEASIBLE DUE TO NEW BATTERY TECHNOLOGIES AND THE LACK OF NEED FOR DEEP SEA MINERALS. IN JULY, THE TEAM RETURNED TO THE ISA WHERE THEY REPRESENTED TETIAROA SOCIETY IN ITS NEW OBSERVER STATUS. TETIAROA SOCIETY HELPED TO ORGANIZE A SIDE EVENT WITH INDIGENOUS REPRESENTATIVES FROM MICRONESIA AND TONGA, INCLUDING TWO SENIOR (PWO) NAVIGATORS WHO PRESENTED THEIR VIEWS ON DEEP SEA MINING.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE FUND IS RESTRICTED FOR FUTURE RESEARCH PROJECTS THAT CONTRIBUTE TO THE UNDERSTANDING AND WISE MANAGEMENT OF THE OCEANS AND TROPICAL ISLAND ECOSYSTEM THROUGH RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND CONSERVATION RELATED TO THE ATOLL OF TETIAROA, FRENCH POLYNESIA, AND TO ENGAGE IN SUCH OTHER ACTIVITIES AS ARE APPROPRIATE AND CONSISTENT WITH THE FOREGOING. THE PRESIDENT AND VICE PRESIDENT OF TETIAROA SOCIETY FORM THE COMMITTEE THAT DETERMINE HOW THE FUNDS ARE USED. THERE ARE LIMITS TO THE DISTRIBUTIONS FROM THE FUND EACH YEAR AS DETERMINED BY THE ENDOWMENT FUND AGREEMENT.

TETIAROA SOCIETY'S EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE DETERMINES GRANTEES BASED UPON A COMPLETED APPLICATION AND THE NEEDS OF THE GRANTEE. THE COMMITTEE ALSO DETERMINES IF A GRANT SHOULD BE MADE BASED UPON CERTAIN TETIAROA SOCIETY CRITERIA. THEN IF A GRANT IS MADE, A GRANT AGREEMENT IS EXECUTED WITH THE GRANTEE THAT SPECIFIES THE USE OF THE FUNDS, THE PAYMENT TERMS, THE GRANTEE'S REPORTING REQUIREMENTS, THE RIGHT OF TETIAROA SOCIETY TO INSPECT THE GRANTEE'S BOOKS AND RECORDS TO ENSURE THAT FUNDS ARE SPENT PROPERLY AND IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE GRANT AGREEMENT, THE REQUIREMENT TO RETURN FUNDS THAT ARE NOT SPENT PROPERLY, NON-PERMITTED USE OF FUNDS AND OTHER MATTERS. TETIAROA SOCIETY THEN KEEPS IN CLOSE CONTACT WITH THE GRANTEE VIA EMAIL, BY PHONE AND IN PERSON TO MONITOR THE USE OF THE FUNDS, REVIEWS THE REPORTS RECEIVED FROM THE GRANTEE AND FOLLOW UP WITH THE GRANTEE AT LEAST TWICE PER YEAR TO ENSURE THAT THE GRANT FUNDS WERE USED ACCORDING TO THE TERMS OF THE GRANT.

ACCRUAL

FORM 926 IS NOT REQUIRED TO BE FILED BECAUSE THE TRANSFERS TO FOREIGN CORPORATIONS DO NOT MEET THE REPORTING REQUIREMENT IN IRC SEC. 6038(A)(1)(A) OR 367(D).

THE NUMBER OF VOLUNTEERS WAS DETERMINED BY REVIEWING THE ACTIVITIES AND ACTIONS OF TETIAROA SOCIETY IN 2024. BOTH THE BOARD MEMBERS AND HONORARY BOARD MEMBERS PARTICIPATED IN BOARD ACTIVITY AND ON EXECUTIVE COMMITTEES THROUGHOUT THE YEAR. THE BOARD MEMBERS HELPED SET TETIAROA SOCIETY POLICY AND OVERSAW FUNDRAISING PLANNING THROUGHOUT THE YEAR. TETIAROA SOCIETY ALSO HAD SEVERAL INDIVIDUALS THAT VOLUNTEERED TO HELP RAISE FUNDS AND HELP WITH THE IMPLEMENTATION OF SEVERAL RESEARCH PROJECTS OF TETIAROA.

THE TETIAROA EDUCATION PROGRAM OFFERS YOUNG PEOPLE A UNIQUE OPPORTUNITY TO EXPLORE SCIENCE AND TRADITIONAL KNOWLEDGE ON A REMARKABLE ISLAND. LOCAL STUDENTS (AGES 8 TO 18) LEARN ABOUT THEIR CULTURAL AND NATURAL HERITAGE AND TAKE HOME VALUABLE LESSONS IN SUSTAINABILITY. INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS ARE INTRODUCED TO A TROPICAL ISLAND THAT SERVES AS A GLOBAL MODEL FOR CONSERVATION AND SUSTAINABLE PRACTICES. THE PROGRAM AIMS TO INSPIRE AND ENRICH ALL WHO PARTICIPATE. IN 2024, WE WORKED WITH: - THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY ISP COURSE - CHADWICK HIGH SCHOOL - LAKESIDE HIGH SCHOOL - MULTIPLE FRENCH POLYNESIAN SCHOOL GROUPS

MIKE MEDAVOY AND LARRY DRESSLER HAVE A FAMILY RELATIONSHIP.

THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE WILL REVIEW THE FORM 990 BEFORE FILING WITH THE IRS.

ALL TETIAROA SOCIETY BOARD MEMBERS ARE COVERED BY THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. TETIAROA SOCIETY OPERATIONS ARE REVIEWED EVERY YEAR FOR POTENTIAL CONFLICTS AND THE PRESIDENT REVIEWS ALL COFLICT OF INTEREST POLICIES. IN 2024, NO CONFLICTS WERE NOTED OR DISCOVERED AND THUS NO ACTIONS WERE TAKEN BY TETIAROA SOCIETY UNDER THE POLICY. IF THERE WAS A CONFLICT, THE CONFLICTED PERSON WOULD BE RECUSED FROM VOTING AND DISCUSSIONS ON THE MATTER.

THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE PROVIDED UPON REQUEST.

CONTRACT SERVICES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 81,891. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 9,034. TOTAL EXPENSES 90,925.

mainOrgs.name: Walter Munk Foundation For The Oceans
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Preserve Walter Munk's legacy through scientific research, education, and ocean conservation.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Research on Lake Altaussee and conservation of Mobula Munkiana.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on ocean conservation and ecosystem preservation.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Engages in two distinct yet related conservation research programs.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The foundation has established its mission over 6.9 years with a strong focus on ocean conservation, but a declining revenue trend and low months of unrestricted funds may pose challenges.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Bingqian Gao wins Dr. Sylvia Earle Award.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: MISSION IS TO CONTINUE WALTER MUNK'S LEGACY OF DARING EXPLORATION AND DISCOVERY THROUGH SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND OCEAN CONSERVATION.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: WALTER MUNK VISITED ALTAUSSEE, AUSTRIA IN AUGUST 2018 TO ARRANGE FOR AN EXPEDITION TO STUDY LAKE ALTAUSSEE IN THE SPRING OF 2019. IT WAS HIS WAY OF GIVING BACK TO HIS BOYHOOD COMMUNITY HE LOVED, BY PROVIDING INFORMATION ON THE LAKE THAT WOULD ALLOW THE COMMUNITY TO PRESERVE ITS PRISTINE ALPINE TREASURE. IT WAS A MULTI-DISCIPLINARY RESEARCH EFFORT TO OBTAIN A BETTER SCIENTIC UNDERSTANDING OF LAKE ALTAUSSEE IN TERMS OF ITS CULTURAL, ECOLOGICAL, AND GEOLOGICAL SIGNICANCE. OVER THE PAST FOUR YEARS, THE STUDY HAS EXPANDED TO INCLUDE 28 SCIENTISTS, RESEARCHERS, PHD AND MASTERS STUDENTS. ERWIN HEINE, PH.D., UNIVERSITY OF NATURAL RESOURCES AND APPLIED LIFE SCIENCE IN VIENNA IS THE PI FOR THE EXPEDITIONS. DIMITRI DEHEYN, PH.D., FROM SCRIPPS INSTITUTION OF OCEANOGRAPHY JOINED US TO WORK WITH THE LOCAL HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS TO FURTHER HIS STUDY OF PLASTICS AND THEIR IMPACT IN THE LAKE AND THE SURROUNDING AREA. WE ARE ARRANGING A STUDENT EXCHANGE PROGRAM THAT WILL PROVIDE AN OPPORTUNITY FOR INTERNSHIPS IN DIMITRI DEHEYN'S LAB AT SCRIPPS.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: WMFO SUPPORTS CONSERVATION EFFORTS FOR THE MOBULA MUNKIANA, WALTER'S NAMESAKE, THE SMALLEST OF THE MANTA RAYS, FOUND IN WARM AND TEMPERATE SEAS. IT IS CONSIDERED VULNERABLE DUE TO FISHING BYCATCH PRESSURE AND HABITAT LOSS. THE FOUNDATION SUPPORTS SCIENTISTS AND ORGANIZATIONS CONDUCTING MOBULA MUNKIANA RESEARCH, CONSERVATION AND EDUCATION ACTIVITIES. THE GOAL IS TO FIND EFFECTIVE CONSERVATION MEASURES TO PROTECT CRITICAL HABITAT.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
ORGANIZATION'S PRIMARY EXEMPT PURPOSE THE PURPOSE OF THIS CORPORATION IS TO CONTINUE WALTER MUNK'S WORK AND LEGACY OF DARING EXPLORATION AND DISCOVERY THROUGH SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND OCEAN CONSERVATION. IN PARTICULAR, THE CORPORATION'S GOALS AND OBJECTIVES ARE AS FOLLOWS: A) TO PROMOTE STEWARDSHIP OF OUR OCEANS BY: I. CREATING AWARENESS OF THE ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS RELATED TO THE OCEANS; II. TEACHING RESPONSIBLE USE AND PROTECTION OF OUR OCEANS THROUGH CONSERVATION AND SUSTAINABLE PRACTICES; III. CONSERVING OUR AQUATIC ECOSYSTEMS AND PROTECTING SPECIES THAT ARE ENDANGERED; IV. DEVELOPING AND REACHING OUT TO DOERS, DONORS, AND PRACTITIONERS. CONTINUED B) WORK WITH STUDENTS OF ALL AGES THROUGH OUTREACH EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS: I. PUBLICIZING EXPEDITIONS AND RELATED ACTIVITIES; II. COLLECTING AND SHARING DATA; AND III. PUBLISHING SCIENTIFIC PAPERS. C) TO ESTABLISH COLLABORATIONS AND INTERDISCIPLINARY PARTNERSHIPS WITH GLOBAL GOVERNMENTAL AGENCIES, INSTITUTIONS, AND ORGANIZATIONS WITH SIMILAR GOALS; D) TO ADVOCATE FOR A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF CLIMATE CHANGE AND SEA LEVEL RISE; AND E) TO RAISE MONEY TO SUPPORT THE COPORATION'S CHARITABLE GOALS.

CORPORATION IS RELATIVELY NEW AND DOES NOT HAVE COMMITTEES. CURRENTLY, ONLY A BOARD OF DIRECTORS EXISTS AND IS DOCUMENTING THEIR MINUTES UNTIL THEY HAVE A COMMITTEE IN PLACE.

THE DRAFT OF THE 990 IS DISTRIBUTED TO THE FULL BOARD TO HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO REVIEW AND COMMENT BEFORE THE 990 IS FINALIZED.

1. ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST THAT MAY AFFECT A MATTER UNDER CONSIDERATION SHALL BE DISCLOSED BY THE TRUSTEE TO THE BOARD AND MADE A MATTER OF RECORD AS SOON AS THE POSSIBLE CONFLICT IS DETERMINED. 2. THE INTERESTED TRUSTEE SHALL NOT VOTE ON SUCH MATTER AND SHALL NOT ATTEMPT TO EXERT INFLUENCE IN CONNECTION WITH THE MATTER. HE OR SHE MAY BE PRESENT AT THE MEETING OF THE BOARD OR COMMITTEE, BUT HE/SHE SHALL LEAVE THE MEETING DURING THE DISCUSSION OF, AND THE VOTE ON, THE TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT INVOLVING THE POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST. 3. THE MINUTES OF THE MEETING SHALL REFLECT THAT A DISCLOSURE WAS MADE AND THE INTERESTED TRUSTEE'S ABSTENTION FROM VOTING. 4. A MEMBER OF ANY COMMITTEE WHOSE JURISDICTION INCLUDES COMPENSATION MATTERS AND WHO RECEIVES COMPENSATION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, FROM THE CORPORATION FOR SERVICES IS PRECLUDED FROM VOTING ON MATTERS PERTAINING TO THAT MEMBER'S COMPENSATION. 5. A MASTER LIST OF ALL DISCLOSED POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST IS COMPILED ANNUALLY AND REVIEWED PERIODICALLY TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE.

THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

CONSULTING: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 58,224. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 147,167. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 205,391. PAYROLL FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 1,525. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 1,525.

mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE FORM 990 IS PROVIDED TO ALL MEMBERS OF THE GOVERNING BODY FOR REVIEW AND APPROVAL PRIOR TO FILING.

THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS REVIEWED AT THE COMMITTEE'S ANNUAL MEETING IN JANUARY. BOARD MEMBERS AND EMPLOYEES ARE REQUIRED TO REVEAL POSSIBLE CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. WHEN PROJECTS ARE IMPLEMENTED ON THE GROUND, THE PROJECT IS REVIEWED IN TERMS OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND MEMBERS WITH POTENTIAL CONFLICTS ARE IDENTIFIED AND ADVISED OF THE CONFLICT AND WAYS TO ALLEVIATE THE CONFLICT, SUCH AS NOT PARTICIPATING IN DECISIONS REGARDING THE PROJECT.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTORS COMPENSATION IS SET BY THE FOUR OFFICERS OF THE COMMITTEE. THE OFFICERS CONSIDER THE SKILLS OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, PAY FOR COMPARABLE POSITIONS WITH SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS, AND THE COMPLEXITY OF THE RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER SUPERVISION. COMPENSATION IS GENERALLY REVIEWED ANNUALLY, HOWEVER, CHANGES IN PAY DO NOT NECESSARILY OCCUR ANNUALLY.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS FORM 1023 AND 990 AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION UPON REQUEST.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

OTHER PROFESSIONAL FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 377,732. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 16,807. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 394,539.

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Dedicated to restoring and maintaining coral reef communities in Islamorada, FL through community involvement and education.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Coral transplantation and community education for reef conservation.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on coral reef conservation directly affects water quality and marine ecosystems.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission centered around coral reef restoration and education.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: I CARE Corporation is an emerging nonprofit with a revenue of $672K, showing a strong growth trend. Their focused mission on coral reef conservation is crucial for local marine ecosystems.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Introduced a new 'Sponge Science' page and promotes hands-on coral restoration events.

mainOrgs.missionStatement: ISLAMORADA'S CONSERVATION AND RESTORATION EDUCATION (ICARE) IS DEDICATED TO INCORPROATING EFFORTS OF LOCAL BUSINESSES, RESIDENTS AND VISITORS IN RESTORING AND MAINTAINING CORAL REEF COMMUNITIES IN ISLAMORADA, FL. THE MISSION IS TO INCREASE COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT IN REEF CONSERVATION THROUGH CITIZEN SCIENCE AND EDUCATION, CORAL TRANSPLANTATION AND CONTINUED MONITORING OF CORALS, REEF FISHES AND INVERTEBRATES. THE GOALS FOR THE REEF ARE TO COLLECT DATA ON THE FACTORS IMPACTING CORAL TRANSPLANT SURVIVAL, INCREASE CORAL COVER FROM 3-5% TO 10% OVER THE NEXT THREE YEARS, TO HELP ECOLOGICAL PROCESEES TO THESE REEF COMMUNITIES, WHILE INCREASING EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH OPPORTUNITIES FOR MEMBERS OF THE RECREATIONAL DIVING COMMUNITY
mainOrgs.program2Desc: THE ORGNIZATION OUTPLANTED OVER 4,000 CORAL
mainOrgs.program3Desc: THE ORGANIZATION EDUCATED AND TRAINED OVER 1,000 DIVERS AND COMMUNITY MEMBERS AS TO THE CONDITION OF THE REEF AND HOW THEY CAN BE PART OF THE RESTORATION EFFORTS

mainOrgs.name: Peaks To People Water Fund
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Protect water resources in the Cache La Poudre and Big Thompson watersheds by restoring forests.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Forest restoration projects to improve watershed health and reduce wildfire risk.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Direct focus on watershed protection and water quality through forest restoration.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Single clear mission aimed at watershed protection through forest restoration.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Collaborating with local watershed health partnerships and scientific institutions.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: PEAKS TO PEOPLE WATER FUND is an established organization with a strong revenue trend of 350.6%, focusing on forest restoration to protect local water resources in critical watersheds.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE PEAKS TO PEOPLE WATER FUND AIMS TO PROTECT RESOURCES IN THE CACHE LA POUDRE AND BIG THOMPSON WATERSHEDS BY RESTORING FORESTS TO REDUCE THE RISK OF SEVERE WILDFIRES. THESE FOREST RESTORATION PROJECTS NOT ONLY PROTECT WATER FROM SEDIMENTATION AND DEBRIS, BUT ALSO IMPROVE FOREST HEALTH AND HABITAT, DECREASE FIREFIGHTING COSTS, AND REDUCE HOME LOSS AND INFRASTRUCTURE DAMAGE. OUR GOAL IS TO DRAMATICALLY ACCELERATE FOREST RESTORATION TO REDUCE THE THREAT OF SEVERE WILDFIRE BY ENGAGING DOWNSTREAM WATER USERS TO DONATE FUNDS FOR THIS WORK.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE PEAKS TO PEOPLE WATER FUND AIMS TO PROTECT RESOURCES IN THE CACHE LA POUDRE AND BIG THOMPSON WATERSHEDS BY RESTORING FORESTS TO REDUCE THE RISK OF SEVERE WILDFIRES. THESE FOREST RESTORATION PROJECTS NOT ONLY PROTECT WATER FROM SEDIMENTATION AND DEBRIS, BUT ALSO IMPROVE FOREST HEALTH AND HABITAT, DECREASE FIREFIGHTING COSTS, AND REDUCE HOME LOSS AND INFRASTRUCTURE DAMAGE. OUR GOAL IS TO DRAMATICALLY ACCELERATE FOREST RESTORATION TO REDUCE THE THREAT OF SEVERE WILDFIRE BY ENGAGING DOWNSTREAM WATER USERS TO DONATE FUNDS FOR THIS WORK.

OVER THE PAST FISCAL YEAR, PEAKS TO PEOPLE WORKED TO PROTECT WATER RESOURCES IN THE CACHE LA POUDRE AND BIG THOMPSON WATERSHEDS FROM THE THREAT OF WILDFIRE. WE SUPPORTED FOREST HEALTH PROJECTS TO PROTECT IMPORTANT DRINKING WATER SOURCES, AND SCOPED ADDITIONAL PROJECTS TO BE FUNDED IN THE NEXT FISCAL YEAR. WE CONVENED THE BIG THOMPSON WATERSHED HEALTH PARTNERSHIP AND WE ARE COORDINATING THIS COLLABORATIVE GROUP TO INCREASE THE PACE AND SCALE OF WORK IN THE WATERSHED. WE CONTINUED OUR PARTNERSHIP WITH THE COLORADO FOREST RESTORATION INSTITUTE TO FURTHER DEVELOP THE SCIENCE IN THE FIELD TO ENSURE FUNDS ARE ALLOCATED TO THE MOST EFFECTIVE FOREST MANAGEMENT PROJECTS AND TO CONTINUOUSLY-IMPROVED METHODS FOR FOREST RESTORATION. THROUGH OUR WEBSITE, BLOGGING AND SOCIAL MEDIA, WE EDUCATED THE PUBLIC ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF WILDFIRE MITIGATION AND WATER RESOURCES.

A DRAFT OF THE 990 IS PROVIDED TO EACH MEMBER OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR THEIR REVIEW AND INPUT.

ALL BOARD MEMBERS SIGN AN ACKNOWLEDGEMENT ANNUALLY THAT THEY HAVE RECEIVED THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND AGREE TO ITS TERMS. THE PRESIDENT ASKS IF THERE ARE ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST AT THE OUTSET OF EACH BOARD MEETING. IN ADDITION, PERIODIC REVIEWS ARE CONDUCTED THAT INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING SUBJECTS: 1) WHETHER COMPENSATION ARRANGEMENTS AND BENEFITS ARE REASONABLE, BASED ON COMPETENT SURVEY INFORMATION, AND THE RESULT OF ARM'S LENGTH BARGAINING; 2) WHETHER PARTNERSHIPS, JOINT VENTURES, AND ARRANGEMENTS WITH MANAGEMENT ORGANIZATIONS CONFORM TO THE ORGANIZATION'S WRITTEN POLICIES, ARE PROPERLY RECORDED, REFLECT REASONABLE INVESTMENT OR PAYMENTS FOR GOOD AND SERVICES, FURTHER CHARITABLE PURPOSES AND DO NOT RESULT IN INUREMENT, IMPERMISSIBLE PRIVATE BENEFIT OR IN AN EXCESS BENEFIT TRANSACTION.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS VOTES ON THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S COMPENSATION AFTER EVALUATING PERFORMANCE, EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS, AND BENCHMARKING USING INDEPENDENT SALARY SURVEYS.

NO DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Facilitates communication and coordination on fisheries and water quality issues among four Klamath River Basin tribes.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education and monitoring of water quality and fisheries for member tribes.

mainOrgs.notableSignals: Consists of four member Indian tribes with decision-making by consensus.

mainOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (100%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: TO FACILITATE INTER-TRIBAL (AMONG FOUR MEMBER TRIBES) AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL COMMUNICATION, EDUCATION, AND COORDINATION RELATING TO FISHERIES AND WATER QUALITY ISSUES CONCERNING THE KLAMATH RIVER BASIN.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
THE COMMISSION PERIODICALLY REVIEWS FINANCIAL AND PROGRAM REPORTS COMPLETED BY SUBRECIPIENT INDIAN TRIBES THROUGHOUT THE PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE. ADDITIONALLY, THE COMMISSION REVIEWS SUBRECIPIENT TRIBES ANNUAL SINGLE AUDIT REPORTS AS WELL AS CONDUCTS SITE VISITS TO MONITOR PROJECTS AS DEEMED NECESSARY.

FOR 2022, NO FORM W-2 OR W-3 WAS FILED TO REPORT OFFICE/DATA MANAGER COMPENSATION DUE TO THE FACT THAT IT IS EXEMPT FROM EMPLOYMENT TAXES UNDER IRC SECTION 7873. THE COMMISSION HAS NO OTHER EMPLOYEES.

THE COMMISSION HAS FOUR MEMBER INDIAN TRIBES AS FOLLOWS: (1) KARUK INDIAN TRIBE, (2) KLAMATH INDIAN TRIBE, (3) YUROK INDIAN TRIBE, (4) HOOPA VALLEY INDIAN TRIBE.

THE GOVERNING BODY OF EACH MEMBER TRIBE APPOINTS ONE REPRESENTATIVE TO SERVE ON THE COMMISSION'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS THEN CARRY OUT THE OBJECTIVES OF THE COMMISSION. ALL DECISIONS AND ACTIONS ARE BASED ON CONSENSUS.

A DRAFT COPY OF THE 990 IS E-MAILED TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO SUBMISSION.

CONFLICTS OF INTEREST ARE EVALUATED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

ALL GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, POLICIES, AND FINANCIAL REPORTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

THE ORGANIZATION'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS WILL BE AUDITED BUT THE AUDIT HAS NOT YET BEEN COMPLETED. THE AUDIT IS EXPECTED TO BE COMPLETED WITHIN THE NEXT COUPLE OF MONTHS.

mainOrgs.scheduleO:
GRANTS ARE AWARDED BASED ON SPECIFIC CRITERIA AND ARE APPROVED BY THE BOARD COMMITTEE THAT OVERSEES ALL RECIPIENTS OF GRANTS.

THE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS IS RELATED TO TWO OFFICERS.

THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT HAVE ANY COMMITTEES WITH AUTHORITY TO ACT ON BEHALF OF THE GOVERNING BODY.

THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE AUTHORIZED OFFICIALS PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE IRS.

GLOBAL CORALITION ENFORCES COMPLIANCE THROUGH THE FOLLOWING PROCESS: - EACH BOARD MEMBER AND KEY EMPLOYEE MUST SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE FORM ANNUALLY. - ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICTS ARE REVIEWED BY THE BOARD CHAIR AND RECORDED IN THE BOARD MINUTES. - MEMBERS WITH A CONFLICT ABSTAIN FROM DISCUSSIONS AND DECISIONS RELATED TO THAT MATTER. - THE PROCESS IS DOCUMENTED AND OVERSEEN BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR. (FULL POLICY ATTACHED IN "CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY" DOCUMENT.)

COMPENSATION FOR THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND KEY EMPLOYEES IS DETERMINED AND APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE PROCESS INCLUDES BOARD REVIEW OF RESPONSIBILITIES AND AVAILABLE RESOURCES. AT THIS TIME, FORMAL COMPARABILITY STUDIES ARE NOT CONDUCTED DUE TO THE SMALL ORGANIZATIONAL SCALE.

THE ORGANIZATION'S GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, FORM 990, FORM 1023, AND FINANCIAL DATA ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

## uniting art, science and communities

**to regenerate our marine ecosystems across 30 of our most critical reef sites.**

## OUR VISION

A global movement of large-scale underwater sculptures and storytelling 

to **unify efforts and accelerate marine protection, coral farming and mangrove restoration**across 30 of the most critical coral reef sites.

Scientists have found that due to the rapid recovery rates of marine ecosystems, with the right interventions, **we can actually rebuild marine life by 2050.**

Title: Juneau Icefield Research Program

URL Source: https://www.juneauicefield.org/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/672e76ef74c9fd70943b44f4/1731098353782-C1S1HUU8AW5X740ZKYCM/Homepage-AndrewOpila-2018.jpeg)

## We teach, train, and inspire the scientific leaders of tomorrow.

#### Our Story

### What is the Juneau Icefield Research Program?

The Juneau Icefield Research Program’s (JIRP’s) mission is two-fold: we offer an eight-week scientific immersion in the wilderness of the Juneau Icefield for undergraduate and graduate students interested in glacier science. We also offer planning and logistic support to research teams collecting data on the Juneau Icefield.

![Image 2](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/672e76ef74c9fd70943b44f4/f62b0a22-7f1d-4248-9bc5-e2d31c99e8f4/JIRP_AOP_2024-0229.jpg?format=750w)

_Andrew Opila, a student in 2018, created a short film about JIRP._

![Image 3](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/672e76ef74c9fd70943b44f4/f785b11f-3132-41d4-b442-48bc8190c9b1/Asset+22%403x.png)

### Academic Affiliations

JIRP is a collaborative endeavor run primarily through the [**Department of Geological Sciences at the University of Colorado, Boulder**](https://www.colorado.edu/geologicalsciences/), with significant support from the [**University of Maine School of Earth and Climate Sciences**](https://umaine.edu/earthclimate/), the [**University of Maine Climate Change Institute**](https://climatechan

## Changing people's lives through SAFE WATER

We deliver safe drinking water and sanitation solutions through diverse filtration systems to communities in need, mostly in Africa and Latin America.

This is more than a water project.

### This is a movement of people changing lives—

beginning with their own.

This isn’t just a story about water. It’s a story about people. And it’s still being written.

This is more than a water project.

### This is a movement of people changing lives—

beginning with their own.

This isn’t just a story about water. It’s a story about people. And it’s still being written.

## 4.4 billion people lack safe water and sanitation

That’s half of the world without a basic human right. No safe water to drink, no toilets, no showers.

More than double the 2020 estimate.

Title: Rock The Ocean - A Nonprofit Dedicated to Ocean Conservation

URL Source: http://rocktheocean.com/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1: rock the ocean logo](https://www.rocktheocean.com/wp-content/themes/rocktheocean/img/Rock-The-Ocean-White-Logo.svg)

## ORGANIZATIONS SAY THEY WOULD GIVE ANYTHING TO SAVE THE OCEAN.

BUT WOULD THEY GIVE EVERYTHING?WE DO.

## 100% of proceeds from your purchase go to saving our oceans and marine life.

## An entire brand

 dedicated to ocean 

 conservation

Rock The Ocean is a non-profit, so every dime we make on everything we do goes to saving the oceans and marine life. Click below to join our movement and fly your flag for ocean conservation.

[Shop the Collection](https://rocktheocean.myshopify.com/)

![Image 2: Rock the Ocean Logo](https://www.rocktheocean.com/wp-content/themes/rocktheocean/img/rock-the-ocean-logo-color.png)

### We’re Not Trying To Save The Whole World...

### Just ¾ of it!

At Rock The Ocean, we love the ocean as much as you do. With 75% of the earth's surface covered in water , the health of the ocean determines the health of our planet. And our mission as a nonprofit is to raise awareness of issues impacting marine life and drive ocean conservation by supporting the experts and organizations on the front lines. We do that through the music festival we founded, Rock The Ocean’s Tortuga Music Festival, as well as through the Rock The Ocean Collection, our events and partnerships.

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Protecting the ocean through education, conservation, and scientific research for multicultural youth.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education and conservation for youth related to ocean threats.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Strong focus on ocean conservation with educational components.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Education and conservation programs targeting ocean issues.

mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (101%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: MERITO is a 501(c)3 charitable organization dedicated to protecting the ocean by facilitating education, conservation, and scientific research opportunities for multicultural youth and their communities while increasing awareness and understanding of the importance of the ocean, ocean-related threats, and environmentally friendly practices. MERITO also strives to motivate diverse youth to pursue science, technology or engineering careers that relate to the ocean.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
Merito participates in local community and environmental based public events including Earth Day and The Aloha Beach Festival. Merito receives donations and sells T shirts as well as collecting fees from other regstered vendors.

Form 990 is reviewed with the Board of Directors and approved prior to filing.

The MERITO Foundation monitors and enforces compliance with the Conflict of Interest Policy throughout the year during the quarterly board meetings and the annual summer retreats which are, when possible, facilitated by external consultants specialized in Capacity Building for non-for-profit organizations. In addition, the board members, officers, and advisors are asked to participate at least once every 2 years in Board Leadership and Capacity training sessions such as the current Board Leadership Institute provided by the California Lutheran University Center for Nonprofit Leadership

The Board of Directors reviews Officer compensation during the annual end of year meeting.

Tax returns and accomplishments reports are available at the organization's website at https://www.meritofoundation.org/anual-reports

Title: Home

URL Source: https://www.meritofoundation.org/

Markdown Content:
## MERITO Foundation

## Committed to protecting the ocean by facilitating education, conservation, and scientific research opportunities to youth and their communities.

![Image 1: IMG_9485 2.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/f967a9_195edcbf84db4fe59d909b1dae7d147a~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_334,h_265,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/IMG_9485%202.jpg)

## Our Vision / Nuestra Visión

Our vision is a healthy ocean cared by diverse coastal communities who live in an environmentally responsible manner regardless of native language, economic status, cultural beliefs, or ethnic background because we all share one ocean.

​

Nuestra visión es un océano saludable cuidado por diversas comunidades costeras que viven de manera responsable con el medio ambiente, sin importar el idioma nativo, el estado económico, las creencias culturales o el origen étnico, porque todos compartimos un mismo océano.

![Image 2: IMG_8985.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/f967a9_561fbda433fb4b75b68f2e45b3a62cf8~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_334,h_265,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/IMG_8985.jpg)

## Our Mission / Nuestra Misión

To protect the ocean by engaging multicultural youth and their communities in transformative experiences in environmental education, scientific research, outdoor recreation, marine conservation & entrepreneurship.

​

Nuestra misión es proteger el océano al proveer experiencia
