Markdown Content:
## Who we are

We are the Blue River Watershed Group, a non-profit organization based in Summit County, Colorado since 2004. Join us in our mission to promote, protect, and restore the Blue River watershed. Get involved with river restoration projects, stream connectivity work, water quality monitoring, river cleanups and more.

Make a difference in the health of the Blue River and its tributaries today!

![Image 1: Picture](https://www.blueriverwatershed.org/storage/10094/a10ea264-5b00-4af1-a03b-cc65f7d36f67/photo-jun-21-2023-5-53-17-pm-1_1.jpg?w=1080)

[![Image 2: Picture](https://www.blueriverwatershed.org/storage/10094/a10ea264-5b00-4af1-a03b-cc65f7d36f67/43-hjd-7690_1.jpg?w=1080)](https://www.blueriverwatershed.org/iwmp-910563)

[![Image 3: Picture](https://www.blueriverwatershed.org/storage/10094/a10ea264-5b00-4af1-a03b-cc65f7d36f67/lupine.jpg?w=1080)](https://www.blueriverwatershed.org/swan-river-restoration)

The Blue River Watershed Group is a local environmental nonprofit that leads and supports science and research-based river restoration projects across the Blue River watershed, addressing the impacts that development, recreation, and historic mining have had on our ecosystems, fisheries, rivers, and communities. Our work improves stream health, aquatic habitat, and water quality in Summit County, Colorado and has positive impa

mainOrgs.name: Howard T Odum Florida Springs Institute Inc
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Improving understanding of springs ecology and promoting management actions for springs restoration in Florida.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Education on springs ecology, hydroecology, and state water law.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focuses on springs which are vital for water quality and conservation.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Mainly focused on education with interconnected themes of springs and water law.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Leading expert on springs health with active community engagement.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: Established for 13 years with $555K revenue, Florida Springs Institute shows strong community impact and education efforts on springs conservation, despite low program expense ratio.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Recent programs include Springs Academy and SpringsWatch, focusing on education and conservation of Florida springs.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (94%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: Florida Springs Institute provides a focal point for improving the understanding of springs ecology and fosters development of science-based education and management actions needed to restore and protect springs throughout Florida.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: EDUCATION : FSI Conducts presentations to school children and adults on ecology, hydroecology, geology, springs problems and solutions, and state and federal water law. FSI also works with local organizations to educate local government and state leaders on issues affecting the springs so they may make informed decisions to protect the States water ressources. FSIs executive director publishes columns and op-eds in local newspapers on spring issues and conducts TV, radio, and newspaper interviews as one of the states leading experts on Springs and current trends in Springs health. FSI conducts short courses on Springs for environment professionals, students and the public. FSI published a childrens activity and coloring book entitled Aquatic Adventures: Florida Springs and Aquafer. Over 400 copies have been distributed.
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
There are no committees with authority to act on behalf of the governing body.

The Form 990 is prepared by a certified public account and the form is reviewd by our office administrator and executive director before signing. Our CPA submits the signed document. A copy is saved to our server and filed in our physical records.

The Florida Springs Institute requires our Board of Directors to sign a conflict of interest agreement annually. This agreement requires the board to disclose any existing conflicts of interest prior to accepting any position.

The Florida Springs Institute determines compensation for top management officials based on the data of comparable compensation for a similarly qualified person in a similar position at other organizations. The Executive Director compensation is reviewd and approved by a committee of the governing board of directors. The Institute also keeps contemporaneous documentation in regards to decision-making regarding compensation agreements.

The Florida Springs Institute makes governing documents available to the public by providing copies upon request.

Change in value of assets held in Trust.

Title: Ocean Defenders Alliance - Return Trip to Boat Wreck Results in Ghost Gear Removed

URL Source: https://oceandefenders.org/

Published Time: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 04:10:03 GMT

Markdown Content:
[![Image 1: Ocean Defenders Alliance](https://www.oceandefenders.org/images/logo/logo-ODA-red-flag-white-text-full-524x170.png)](https://www.oceandefenders.org/)

[![Image 2: Ocean Defenders Alliance](https://www.oceandefenders.org/images/logo/logo-ODA-red-flag-white-text-full-524x170.png)](https://www.oceandefenders.org/)

![Image 3: Slide background](https://www.oceandefenders.org/images/backgrounds/background-tall-kelp-02-2000x1000.jpg)

![Image 4: Slide background](https://www.oceandefenders.org/images/backgrounds/background-tall-kelp-02-2000x1000.jpg)

Does this statistic bother you too?

Every year, more than 800,000 tons of commercial fishing gear is lost in our global oceans.

We don’t like it either. Want to know what we do about it?

![Image 5](https://www.oceandefenders.org/images/home/showcase-debris-01-framed.png)

YOU are vital in the fight against marine debris

mainOrgs.missionSummary: Advocacy and stewardship for the lower 100 miles of the Deschutes River in Oregon.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Advocacy for water quality standards and public outreach through scientific research.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on river conservation and water quality enforcement.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Two main programs centered on advocacy and community outreach.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: The Deschutes River Alliance has been active for nearly 12 years, with a strong financial position indicated by substantial cash reserves and revenue growth. Their mission directly aligns with water conservation efforts in the Deschutes River basin.
mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: DRA emphasizes ongoing advocacy for river health and water quality standards through real-time monitoring and community engagement.

mainOrgs.missionStatement: ADVOCACY AND STEWARDSHIP FOR THE LOWER 100 MILES OF THE DESCHUTES RIVER IN OREGON
mainOrgs.program2Desc: DURING THE YEAR, DESCHUTES RIVER ALLIANCE CONTINUED ADVOCACY TO ENFORCE WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AT THE PELTON-ROUND BUTTE HYDROELECTRIC COMPLEX. THE WATER QUALITY STANDARDS WERE FORMULATED PURSUANT TO PROVISIONS OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT AND ARE INTENDED TO PROTECT AQUATIC LIFE IN THE RIVER.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: THE DESCHUTES RIVER ALLIANCE WORKS DILIGENTLY TO INFORM ITS SUPPORTS, DESCHUTES RIVER BASIN STAKEHOLDERS, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC OF FINDINGS FROM ITS ONGOING SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH. THIS OUTREACH IS CONDUCTED IN THE FORM OF MAILINGS, MEETINGS WITH CLUBS AND ASSOCIATIONS, AND PRODUCTION OF A DOCUMENTARY FILM.

Title: Deschutes River Alliance

URL Source: https://www.deschutesriveralliance.org/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/6489e4b32e53c92ef9b05d0b/b38abb2e-a219-4c52-a806-dc9f5b4f860b/lower-deschutes-river-sunrise.jpg)

## Protecting & Preserving the lower Deschutes River for Future Generations

![Image 2](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/6489e4b32e53c92ef9b05d0b/6c870709-cab1-4a76-a735-c5c8a7feb3f1/Deschutes-river-alliance-logo.png)

The Deschutes River Alliance (DRA) advocates for the protection of the wild and scenic lower Deschutes River. We all have a right to enjoy this treasure. We also have a responsibility to leave it as good or better than we found it. Together, let’s safeguard this river.

![Image 3](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/6489e4b32e53c92ef9b05d0b/845f7398-791e-42c0-8c23-b08f9348c430/redband-1.jpg)

## The Deschutes River Alliance

is the Protector the River Needs

The lower Deschutes River is the beating heart of Maupin, OR. and many other rural communities. It sustains economies by providing recreational tourism. Fish, wildlife, aquatic insects, and humans rely on the cold, clean water of the Deschutes. The Deschutes River Alliance is committed to preserving the integrity of the lower Deschutes River by enforcing state water quality standards.

![Image 4](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/6489e4b32e53c92ef9b05d0b/cd6db57e-294f-4a24-9d99-e78889dcb712/Deschutes+Oct+3+-+2023-2-min

mainOrgs.name: Shoal Creek Conservancy
mainOrgs.missionSummary: Dedicated to restoring, protecting, and enhancing Shoal Creek's ecological and cultural vibrancy in Austin.
mainOrgs.primaryPrograms: Trail Plan and Community Engagement activities focused on the creek.
mainOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Focused on creek restoration and community involvement in water conservation.
mainOrgs.programFocusRationale: Mix of trail development and community engagement related to Shoal Creek.
mainOrgs.notableSignals: Extensive community engagement with over 750 volunteers in 2024.
mainOrgs.capacityNarrative: Shoal Creek Conservancy, established for over 12 years, shows strong community involvement and stewardship. While revenue has a slight decline, days cash and other financial metrics indicate sound operational capacity.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (97%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: THE CONSERVANCY IS DEDICATED TO RESTORING, PROTECTING AND ENHANCING THE ECOLOGICAL, SOCIAL AND CULTURAL VIBRANCY OF SHOAL CREEK IN AUSTIN, TEXAS, FOR THE PEOPLE OF AUSTIN BY ENGAGING THE PUBLIC AND PARTNERING WITH THE COMMUNITY, INCLUDING GOVERNING ENTITIES THE ORGANIZATION WORKS TO IMPROVE THE CREEK AND ITS ASSOCIATED TRAIL THE ENJOYMENT AND USE BY THE PUBLIC.
mainOrgs.program2Desc: Trail Plan: New trail projects also kicked off in 2024, such as the new urban trail between Shoal Creek and North Lamar Blvd near the Gaston Green dog park, as well as the designs for the .7-mile-long trail extension connecting the Shoal Creek Trail to the Red Line Trail and Q2 stadium.
mainOrgs.program3Desc: Community Engagement: In 2024, we hosted 54 volunteer workdays with over 750 volunteers who removed nearly 23,000 pounds of litter from Shoal Creek. Volunteers also dispersed 1,683 native seedballs and planted 957 native plants along the creek.

mainOrgs.program2Desc: SCIENTIFIC AND PUBLIC POLICY ANALYSIS AIMED AT DETERMINING THE FEASIBILITY AND IMPACTS OF RESTORING AND PROTECTING A VIABLE SEA OTTER POPULATION IN OREGON. mainOrgs.missionSummary: Advocates for just and sustainable use of California's water resources.

mainOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: C-WIN submitted testimony opposing the Delta Conveyance Project in July 2025.
mainOrgs.softFlags: Revenue concentrated in Contributions (95%)
mainOrgs.missionStatement: SEE SCHEDULE O
mainOrgs.program2Desc: SEE SCHEDULE O
mainOrgs.scheduleO:
C-WIN IS A NON-PROFIT, TAX EXEMPT CALIFORNIA CORPORATION THAT ADVOCATES FOR THE JUST AND ENVIRONMENTALLY SUSTAINABLE USE OF CALIFORNIA'S WATER, INCLUDING INSTREAM FLOWS AND GROUNDWATER RESERVES, THROUGH RESEARCH, PLANNING, PUBLIC EDUCATION, MEDIA OUTREACH, AND LITIGATION.

LITIGATION CONCERNING APPROPRIATE USES OF CALIFORNIA WATER: C-WIN IS INVOLVED IN MULTIPLE CONCURRENT LAWSUITS. (A) WE HAVE THREE ACTIVE SUSTAINABLE GROUNDWATER MANAGEMENT ACT VALIDATION CASES FILED AGAINST BIGGS-WEST GRIDLEY WATER DISTRICT, COLUSA GROUNDWATER AUTHORITY AND VINA GROUNDWATER SUSTAINABILITY AGENCY TO PREVENT THE GROUNDWATER BASINS FROM GOING INTO A SEVERE OVERDRAFT CONDITION. (B) DELTA CONVEYANCE PROJECT LAWSUIT TO STOP THE $23 BILLION DEPARTMENT OF WATER RESOURCES (DWR) PROPOSED SINGLE TUNNEL PROJECT THROUGH THE USE OF THE CALIFORNIA ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY ACT (CEQA) FOR DWR'S FAILURE TO ACCOUNT FOR ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS. (C) SITES PROJECT AUTHORITY - TO PREVENT THE CONSTRUCTION OF SITES RESERVOIR BASED ON WATER RIGHTS THAT DO NOT EXIST. (D) THREE SEPARATE LAWSUITS AGAINST WESTLANDS WATER DISTRICT FOR EXTENDING THEIR FEDERAL WATER CONTRACTS INDEFINITELY. WESTLANDS WATER DISTRICT IS APPEALING OUR WIN, THE OTHER TWO CASES ARE STILL PENDING. (E) DELTA PLAN AND ITS EIR: C-WIN SUSTAINS A LAWSUIT TO HALT APPROVAL AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE DELTA PLAN BECAUSE IT DOES NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE CALIFORNIA DELTA REFORM ACT OF 2009.

COMMUNICATION, RESEARCH AND PUBLIC EDUCATION: (A) PART OF A COALITION OF OTHER CONSUMER, ENVIRONMENTAL AND DELTA PROTECTION GROUPS THAT ARE FIGHTING THE PROPOSED SINGLE TUNNEL PROJECT ALSO KNOWN AS THE DELTA CONVEYANCE PROJECT. (B) C-WIN IS MOVING FORWARD WITH OUR PUBLIC TRUST CAMPAIGN TO SAVE THE 26 RIVERS OF THE DELTA WATERSHED AND THE 26 MILLION RATEPAYERS SOUTH OF THE DELTA FROM CATASTROPHIC COSTS FOR INFRASTRUCTURE THAT WILL ONLY PRODUCE DEBT AND NO NEW WATER SUPPLIES.

1. NAME OF PERSON: MAX GOMBERG (A) RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN INTERESTED PERSON & ORGANIZATION: MAX GOMBERG IS AN OFFICER AND VOTING BOARD MEMBER OF C-WIN. (B) DESCRIPTION OF TRANSACTION: MAX GOMBERG RECEIVED LEGAL FEES FOR REPRESENTING C-WIN AS PART OF C-WIN'S LEGAL ADVOCACY PROGRAMS.

YVON CHOUINARD (BOARD MEMBER) IS MARRIED TO MALINDA CHOUINARD (BOARD MEMBER). BARBARA VLAMIS (BOARD MEMBER) IS EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR OF AQUALLIANCE.

THE TREASURER OF THE CALIFORNIA WATER IMPACT NETWORK REVIEWS THE FORM 990 WITH THE PREPARER, THEN PRESENTS THE 990 TO THE BOARD FOR APPROVAL, AND THEN SIGNS AND FILES THE RETURN.

THE ORGANIZATION DISCUSSES ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST WITH BOARD MEMBERS ANNUALLY AND ANY CONFLICTS ARE EITHER AVOIDED OR RESOLVED.

ORGANIZING DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST TO THE PRESIDENT/EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, CAROLEE KRIEGER, AT (805) 969-0824 OR WRITTEN REQUEST TO THE MAIN OFFICES AT 2225 S. CARMELINA AVENUE, LOS ANGELES, CA 90064.

RESEARCH, PLANNING, ADVOCACY, PUBLIC EDUCATION AND LITIGATION

manualReviewOrgs.missionStatement: THE PURPOSE OF THE CORPORATION IS TO ACT AS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION WITH A PRIMARY MISSION OF PROMOTING THE HEALTH OF NANTUCKET ISLANDS HARBOR AND ITS MARINE ECOSYSTEM. THE CORPORATION WILL WORK WITH THE NANTUCKET DEPARTMENT OF NATURAL RESOURCES AND OTHER GOVERNMENTAL AND NONGOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS, GROUPS AND INDIVIDUALS IN FURTHERANCE OF ITS PRIMARY MISSION AND OTHER ACTIVITIES. THE CORPORATION MAY SOLICIT DONATIONS, MAKE GRANTS AND OTHERWISE ENGAGE IN ANY AND ALL ACTIVITIES PERMITTED TO A NOT-FOR-PROFIT CORPORATION WHETHER IN FURTHERANCE OF ITS MISSION OR OTHER CHARITABLE ENDEAVORS, OTHER ACTIONS AS MAY BE NECESSARY OR EXPEDIENT FOR THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE AFFAIRS AND ATTAINMENT OF THE PURPOSES OF THE CORPORATION, AND FOR ALL OTHER ACTIVITIES LEGAL AND CONSISTENT WITH THE OPERATION OF A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION IN THE COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS.
manualReviewOrgs.scheduleO:
THE FOUNDATION RECOGNIZED THE EFFECTS OF INCOME TAX POSITIONS WHEN THEY ARE MORE LIKELY THAN NOT TO BE SUSTAINED. MANAGEMENT HAS DETERMINED THAT THE FOUNDATION HAD NO UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT WOULD REQUIRE FINANCIAL STATEMENT RECOGNITION OR DISCLOSURE. THE FOUNDATION IS SUBJECT TO FEDERAL OR STATE INCOME TAX EXAMINATIONS FOR PERIODS SINCE INCEPTION.

THE FOUNDATION HAS ITS FORM 990 PREPARED BY AN OUTSIDE ACCOUNTING FIRM AND HAS ESTABLISHED THE FOLLOWING REVIEW PROCESS TO ENSURE THAT THE INFORMATION REPORTED IS COMPLETE AND ACCURATE. WHEN THE FORM 990 HAS BEEN PREPARED AND REVIEWED BY MANAGEMENT, IT IS ELECTRONICALLY SENT TO THE MEMBERS OF THE FINANCE COMMITTEE FOR ANY COMMENTS. ANY COMMENTS ARE THEN GROUPED, SUMMARIZED AND REVIEWED WITH MANAGEMENT AND THE ACCOUNTING FIRM. ALL ISSUES ARE DOCUMENTED AND ADDRESSED. THE FORM 990 IS THEN SUBMITTED TO THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR THEIR REVIEW AND APPROVAL. EACH ISSUE IS DOCUMENTED AND ADDRESSED UNTIL THE RETURN IS FINALIZED AND APPROVED FOR FILING.

THE FOUNDATION HAS A WRITTEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, WHICH APPLIES TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS AND OFFICERS, THAT IS MONITORED AND ENFORCED ANNUALLY. ALL BOARD MEMBERS AND OFFICERS ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE AN ANNUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE STATEMENT. IN THE EVENT THAT THE BOARD SHOULD ESTABLISH THAT A PROPOSED TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT ESTABLISHES A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, THE BOARD SHALL THEN PROCEED WITH THE FOLLOWING ACTIONS: (1) ANY INTEREST INDIVIDUAL MAY RENDER A REQUEST OR REPORT AT THE BOARD MEETING, BUT UPON COMPLETION OF SAID REQUEST OR REPORT THE INDIVIDUAL SHALL BE EXCUSED WHILE THE BOARD DISCUSSES THE INFORMATION AND/OR MATERIAL PRESENTED, AND THEN VOTES ON THE TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT PROPOSED INVOLVING THE POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST; (2) THE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD SHALL, IF DEEMED NECESSARY AND APPROPRIATE, APPOINT A DISINTERESTED PERSON OR COMMITTEE TO INVESTIGATE ALTERNATIVES TO THE PROPOSED TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT; (3) AFTER EXERCISING DUE DILIGENCE, THE BOARD SHALL DETERMINE WHETHER THE FOUNDATION CAN OBTAIN WITH REASONABLE EFFORTS A MORE ADVANTAGEOUS TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT FROM A PERSON OR ENTITY THAT WOULD NOT GIVE RISE TO A CONFLICT OF INTEREST; (4) IF A MORE ADVANTAGEOUS TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT IS NOT REASONABLY POSSIBLE UNDER CIRCUMSTANCES NOT PRODUCING A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, THE BOARD SHALL DETERMINE BY A MAJORITY VOTE OF THE DISINTEREST DIRECTORS WHETHER THE TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT IS IN THE FOUNDATION'S BEST INTEREST, FOR ITS OWN BENEFIT AND WHETHER IT IS FAIR AND REASONABLE. IT SHALL MAKE ITS DECISION AS TO WHETHER TO ENTER INTO THE TRANSACTION ARRANGEMENT IN CONFORMITY WITH THIS DETERMINATION.

THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS FORM 990 AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION AS REQUIRED UNDER SECTION 6104 OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. FORM 990 IS POSTED GUIDESTAR.ORG AND OTHER SIMILAR TYPES OF WEBSITES. IN ADDITION, GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ARE ALSO AVAILABLE UPON WRITTEN REQUEST AT 96 WASHINGTON STREET, NANTUCKET, MA 02554 OR BY CALLING THE ORGANIZATION DIRECTLY AT 508-680-5033.

THE ORGANIZATION HAS A COMMITTEE THAT ASSUMES RESPONSIBILITY FOR OVERSIGHT OF THE AUDIT OF THE ORGANIZATION'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SELECTION OF AN INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTANT. THIS PROCESS DID NOT CHANGE FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.

manualReviewOrgs.name: Ohio River Foundation
manualReviewOrgs.missionSummary: To protect and improve the Ohio River and its watershed through advocacy and education.
manualReviewOrgs.primaryPrograms: Habitat restoration, environmental education, and community outreach.
manualReviewOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on the Ohio River and watershed conservation.
manualReviewOrgs.programFocusRationale: Programs closely related to river and watershed advocacy and education.
manualReviewOrgs.notableSignals: Established 25.5 years, strong local impact in watershed improvement.
manualReviewOrgs.capacityNarrative: With 25.5 years of experience and a maintenance of cash reserves, the Ohio River Foundation effectively manages its operations. They focus on impactful programs that advocate for river conservation and engage the community, ensuring a sustainable approach to their mission.
manualReviewOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Hosted the Cincinnati Coffee Festival and the Wild & Scenic Film Festival.
manualReviewOrgs.softFlags: Months unrestricted below threshold; Revenue concentrated in Contributions (88%); Program expense ratio below tier threshold (77%)
manualReviewOrgs.missionStatement: To protect and improve the Ohio River and its watershed through advocacy, habitat restoration, school education, and youth programs.
manualReviewOrgs.program2Desc: Education: ORFs education programs are responsible for leading students and teachers in hands-on, enironmental education experiences. Through education programs, our professional educators use innovative teaching methods to engage students in real- world issues and scientific inquiry, exciting and encouraging them to take action to protect our natural resources. Student conservation leadership program activities during the summer further train student leaders from area high schools. Our river explorer field trips and first-in-the-nation mussels in the classroom experiences reach thousands of students each year in greater Cincinnati, Columbus and Lexington.
manualReviewOrgs.program3Desc: Communications: Communications activities market ORF, educate and engage the public, and mobilize support for organizational priorities and Ohio River watershed protection and restoration efforts. Communication tactics are to develop well-researched messages and deliver them through integrated channels, including: earned, paid or donated media; digital media, including the ORF website, social media, and outbound e-mails; and any other forms of direct or indirect communications with our members and the public at large.

Board members and employees are required to report conflicts of interest to the board.

The Executive Directors compensation is approved at the discretion of the Board of Trustees based on comparative data from similar sized conservation nonprofits and the Executive Directors performance.

The governing documents, conflict of interest, and financial statements are available upon public request.

Revenues are recognized when received rather than when earned and expenses are recognized when cash is disbursed rather than when the obligations have been incurred. Modifications to the cash basis of accounting relate to recording revenue and grants receivable on grant revenue when grants are awarded not when they are received and recording of expenses in the period incurred and not when paid.

URL Source: https://www.netartsbaywebs.org/

Markdown Content:
## **Education • Stewardship • Sustainability**

**Friends of Netarts Bay Watershed, Estuary, Beach, & Sea (WEBS)**

Working to sustain the area from Cape Lookout to Cape Meares through education and stewardship.****

### Attend an upcoming Community or Stewardship Event!

WEBS offers over 40 free community events throughout the year. Join us for a paddle, hike, tour, and more!

[View Upcoming Events](https://www.netartsbaywebs.org/events)

### Explore our School Programs with Bel, the Belted Kingfisher.

WEBS offers place-based school programs for students ranging from pre-K through sixth grade.

[Let Bel Show You Around](https://www.netartsbaywebs.org/school-programs)

### Learn about local flora and fauna.

Explore our companion website, Netarts Bay Today, to learn more about Netarts natural history and local species.

[Dive Deeper into Netarts Bay](https://www.netartsbaywebs.org/netarts-bay-today-website)

## Ben Núñez, Sr Scholarship is now open!

Ben Núñez, Sr. scholarship is open to current residents or former graduates of any Tillamook County School who are pursuing or working toward completing advanced education in STEAM-based natural resource fields related to the mission of WEBS. Up to $1500 in scholarships will be awarded to qualified applicants. Learn more and apply today!

## What is WEBS?

**Friends of Netarts Bay Water

excludedOrgs.missionStatement: THE GALAPAGOS LIFE FUND PROVIDES FINANCIAL SUPPORT AND PROMOTES THE PRESERVATION, GROWTH, AND SECURITY OF THE NATURAL CAPITAL OF THE GALAPAGOS ISLANDS AND THEIR MARINE ECOSYSTEMS. IT DOES SO BY GRANTING FUNDING TO RELEVANT ENVIRONMENTAL AND SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS LED BY ORGANIZATIONS, GOVERNMENT AGENCIES, AND OTHER ENTITIES ADDRESSING ISSUES RELATED TO BIODIVERSITY, SOCIETY, ECONOMY, SUSTAINABILITY, AND CLIMATE CHANGE. THE FUND MAY ALSO PLEDGE ASSETS TO SECURE LOANS AND OTHER OBLIGATIONS IN ALIGNMENT WITH ITS PURPOSE.
excludedOrgs.scheduleO:
THE MEMBERS OF THE CORPORATION ARE (1) OCEANS FINANCE COMPANY B.V. AND (2) LEDUNFLY PHILANTHROPY (EACH A "MEMBER").

EACH MEMBER SHALL HAVE THE RIGHT TO APPOINT A DIRECTOR TO THE BOARD. IN ADDITION, EACH MEMBER SHALL HAVE THE RIGHT (I) TO DESIGNATE A DIRECT OR INDIRECT SUBSIDIARY OR CONTROLLED ENTITY TO SERVE IN ITS PLACE AS A MEMBER AND (II) TO SELECT AND APPOINT ITS RESPECTIVE SUCCESSOR TO REPLACE SUCH MEMBER (A "SUCCESSOR MEMBER"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, THAT AS LONG AS THE FOREIGN ENTERPRISE SUPPORT AGREEMENT ("FESA") IS IN FORCE, NO MEMBER OR DESIGNEE OR SUCCESSOR OF A MEMBER SHALL BE APPOINTED OR REPLACED WITHOUT THE PRIOR WRITTEN CONSENT OF THE UNITED STATES INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT FINANCE CORPORATION (THE "DFC CONSENT"). LASTLY, EACH MEMBER SHALL HAVE THE RIGHT TO APPOINT ONE OR MORE OBSERVERS TO ATTEND MEETINGS OF THE BOARD; PROVIDED, THAT SUCH OBSERVERS SHALL NOT ENJOY ANY OF THE RIGHTS OR OBLIGATIONS OF A DIRECTOR, SHALL NOT BE COUNTED FOR PURPOSES OF ESTABLISHING A QUORUM AND SHALL HAVE NO VOTING RIGHTS.

THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT THIRD-PARTY PUBLIC ACCOUNTING FIRM, IN CONJUNCTION WITH MANAGEMENT. MANAGEMENT REVIEWS THE FORM 990 BEFORE THE COMPLETE COPY IS MADE AVAILABLE TO THE BOARD PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE IRS.

THE CORPORATION HAS ADOPTED A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY FOR ALL BOARD MEMBERS, OFFICERS, AND KEY EMPLOYEES. THE CORPORATION REGULARLY AND CONSISTENTLY MONITORS AND ENFORCES COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. ALL BOARD MEMBERS, OFFICERS, AND KEY EMPLOYEES ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE AN ANNUAL DISCLOSURE AND PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING ANY INTERESTS THAT MAY BE POTENTIAL CONFLICTS PURSUANT TO THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. THEY ARE REQUIRED TO PROVIDE ANY CHANGES OR NEW DISCLOSURES SHOULD THEY OCCUR. ALL DISCLOSURES AND SUBSEQUENT UPDATES TO DISCLOSURES ARE REVIEWED BY THE CO-CHAIRS OF THE BOARD AND ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO ANY BOARD MEMBER, OFFICER OR KEY EMPLOYEE UPON REQUEST. BOARD-LEVEL AND KEY PERSONNEL CONFLICTS ARE REVIEWED AND APPROVED, IF APPROPRIATE, BY THE CO-CHAIRS OF THE BOARD. IF A CONFLICT EXISTS WITH A DIRECTOR, CERTAIN RESTRICTIONS MAY BE IMPOSED, SUCH AS EXCUSING THE DIRECTOR FROM THE ROOM DURING DISCUSSION AND/OR VOTING WITH REGARD TO A PROPOSED TRANSACTION. TO ENSURE THE CORPORATION'S COMPLIANCE WITH THIS POLICY, PERIODIC REVIEWS ARE CONDUCTED. WHEN CONDUCTING PERIODIC REVIEWS, THE CORPORATION MAY USE OUTSIDE ADVISORS. IF OUTSIDE ADVISORS ARE USED, THEIR USE SHALL NOT RELIEVE THE BOARD OF ITS RESPONSIBILITY FOR ENSURING THAT PERIODIC REVIEWS ARE CONDUCTED.

THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND ALL OTHER EMPLOYEES' COMPENSATION IS APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. COMPENSATION IS ALSO APPROVED BY THE FINANCE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD (THE "COMMITTEE"). THE COMMITTEE AND FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS HAVE REVIEWED APPROPRIATE MARKET COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS INFORMATION, APPROPRIATE MARKET PRACTICES FOR COMPARABLE ORGANIZATIONS' POSITIONS AND BEST PRACTICES.

THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE ON THE WEBSITE AND UPON REQUEST.

TOTAL EXPENSES 21,610. CONTRACTED SERVICES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 3,562. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 3,562.

excludedOrgs.missionSummary: Conserve, preserve, and restore water quality and ecological health in Louisiana's Florida Parishes.
excludedOrgs.primaryPrograms: Promote floodplain management, sustainable development, and public understanding of ecosystems.
excludedOrgs.missionAlignmentRationale: Core focus on conserving water quality and ecosystems in local waterways.
excludedOrgs.programFocusRationale: Engages in floodplain management, conservation land donations, and community education.

excludedOrgs.capacityNarrative: Northshore Riverwatch is an emerging organization with almost 3 years of activity focused on preserving local ecosystems. With substantial property donations and educational efforts, they demonstrate a commitment to effective water quality management.
excludedOrgs.websiteRecentActivity: Engagement in community events and advocacy for sustainable development and recreational access.
excludedOrgs.missionStatement: Northshore Riverwatch works to conserve, preserve, and restore the water quality and ecological health of the rivers, wetlands, and waterways of Louisiana's Florida Parishes. We promote responsible floodplain management and sustainable development, support recreational access and enjoyment of our natural waters, and advance public understanding of the vital role these ecosystems play in our communities. Through education, advocacy, scientific study, conservation land donations, and enforcement of environmental protections, we strive to ensure that the natural and scenic rivers of the Northshore are protected for the public benefit.
excludedOrgs.program2Desc: Received donation of 60 acres of floodplain/wetlands for conservation with deed restrictions on development. Expense is for $1,250 Qualified Appraisal and 294 for time and travel.
excludedOrgs.program3Desc: Assisting various communities in our area of interest with floodplain development issues and sewerage treatment issues. Providing factual information on flood risk, depth, and BMP for floodplain development.
excludedOrgs.scheduleO:
The organization engaged in limited direct communication with state and parish officials concerning proposed ordinances and administrative rules related to floodplain management, wetland protection, and water quality in the Florida Parishes. These communications were mostly factual and educational in nature and intended to promote environmentally responsible policy. No substantial part of the organization's activities was directed toward influencing legislation. Northshore Riverwatch's limited policy engagement included testimony before state, parish, and municipal councils on floodplain zoning and development, and wastewater management. These activities were educational, consistent with the organization's mission, and represented less than 5 percent of total annual expenditures.

5 parcels totaling 60-acres of riverfront floodplain, upland, and wetland property protected by permanent deed restrictions to preserve wetlands and floodplain habitat in perpetuity along the Little Bogue Falaya River in St. Tammany Parish, Louisiana. FMV of $816,000 from a qualified appraizal..

We received a fee simple donation of 5 parcels for conservation in line with our mission totaling approximately 60 acres of floodplains/wetlands along the Little Bogue Falaya River for conservation/restoration with deed restrictions.

Title: Northshore Riverwatch | Join the Movement

URL Source: https://nsriverwatch.org/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/64396153099fef0ea3d1d538/d11b70e1-0dd3-49a2-a60e-cdfbe57f20d2/20200407_161808.jpg)

## Protecting Natural and Beneficial River Functions in the Florida Parishes.

### Northshore Riverwatch is a grassroots, advocacy based organization dedicated to protecting the rivers and floodplains in the Florida Parishes through public comments, enforcement of regulations, science, education, and recreation.

**Our mission is to foster sustainable development in the Florida Parishes emphasizing preserving and reclaiming natural and beneficial uses of rivers and floodplains.**

### We may be the new kid on the block, but we are old hands defending Northshore Rivers.

Our leadership team brings decades of collective experience in safeguarding and restoring rivers and floodplains on the Northshore of Lake Pontchartrain. Recognizing the growing need, it has become clear that the Florida Parishes require an independent, full-time organization dedicated to our outstanding natural rivers. Northshore Riverwatch focuses on balancing the demands of unprecedented growth with the need to protect waterways and floodplains, ensuring benefits for both current residents and future development.
